lines.x: 'The city is a man-eating behemoth, but still we must dwell in its bowels gratefully.'
'We wait until life digests us, until our flesh and bones are excreted, so that whatever remains may become nutrients to feed the city as it lurches onwards.'
'That is the flourishing of civilization.'
Welcome to the Kazimierz National Arena! Welcome... to the Kazimierz Major! I am none other than your old friend –– Greatmouth Mob!
After a long qualifying stage, this is the moment all of Kazimierz has long been waiting for –– the first match of the Kazimierz Major starts today!
Dueling, jousting, and assorted miscellany! Three categories, eight major events, forty-three minor events! Countless knights and knightclubs will take their share of gold and glory!
How did Mob end up as the MC? Think he greased some palms behind the scenes?
Like he bribed his way in? How much do you think something like that would cost?
Mob's never been the type to keep his mouth shut. What if he let something slip, right here on the big stage?
Hah. You hear about that loudmouth, the Bearded Cage? Heard he 'badmouthed' a pretty big deal of a knight during the Major...
...and that was the last anyone saw of 'im. Want some popcorn?
Pssht. Probably just took the fall and resigned. A by-the-book trick.
*munch* I shed... dish-appeared... *munch*... not resigned. You get it?
Huh... No shittin'?
Today's Major features eight big knightclubs, sixty-four of the normal ones, and two unaffiliated, independent knights!
The Kazimierz Major is a showcase of the beauty and majesty of our knights for all of Terra to enjoy!
Our first match will pit the Blade Helmet Knightclub against the Cloudhaze Knightclub! How many points will the ten knights from each side take home to their clubs?!
A big prize pool is getting bigger as I speak! A portion of everything you spend at this arena will be added to the tournament prize for the knights to fight over!
Which knightclub will take the most trophies? Which knight will stand above the rest and become this Major's Knight-Champion?
Now... LET US SEE FOR OURSELVES––!
5:43 P.M. \ Clear
The Grand Knight Territory Kawalerielki, Wall of Champions
How's the fit, Mr. Malkiewicz?
Good, thanks to you.
That's enough of that. Custom-tailored formalwear is a must for maintaining our image.
Do you still have the tailor's contact information? I believe you'll find his services most helpful.
Sir, could you please get me a glass of wine? Thank you.
What's on your mind?
Nothing in particular...
Maybe... This banquet is just too much for me.
Ah, 'maybe.'
Maybe you haven't gotten over the incident with Mr. Czarny. Quite the shame, for all of us.
He was a conscientious one, who always put his best foot forward, whether as a company employee or as a spokesman for the K.G.C.C.
In fact, I only met him after becoming a spokesman myself. In terms of our work in the Majors, he was far senior to a fresh face like me.
He always treated those he respected well, yes?
Maybe...
Let's do away with these maybes, and lift our spirits instead.

Today is but the second day of the opening ceremonies, and a great day to open the Wall of Champions to the outside world. It would hardly fit the occasion for the resident manager to wear such a dour expression.
Yes, you're right... Sorry...
You should speak up more, Malkiewicz. And there's no need to be so stiff around me. It's certainly not conducive to our working relationship.
What was that? Did something happen outside?
Hmm.
I believe it's a guest of honor, a very important knight. Let me check the time... See, look how punctual she is. We need more knights like her.
Pardon me, I'll be right back. In the meanwhile, do avail yourself of your right to the open bar.
S-Sure...
The sweet release of libation is wonderful indeed. You ought to indulge in it.
Madam Droste, we meet again.
Good evening. Am I late?
Not at all. I'm glad you could make it.
I do believe the glow of the Candle Knight will add a beautiful touch to tonight's opening banquet.
6:15 P.M. \ Overcast
The Grand Knight Territory Kawalerielki – General Chamber of Commerce, Reception Room
I—
Ah, no need to explain yourself, Platinum. We didn't expect this to happen either... Just doing your job, of course?
You do what you have to do.
I screwed up.
Ah yes, a screwup... But we aren't exactly nine-to-five office drones here. In our line of work, 'screwups' are very serious.
Deadly serious, even.
Luckily this time, you only let the Radiant Knight barge into the Infected quarter... Oh, but that did throw a wrench into Monique's work.
Her job was to make a little fireworks show out of those illegal settlements, and then bring the Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub to Area 0.
It was going so smoothly, but suddenly... from the slaughter came the Radiant Knight, and there went all our plans.
She's very unhappy now, you understand? We all know her temper, and if someone was to get in the way of her bounty, you know how troublesome that'd be.
I didn't know what Monique was doing.
No, you did know. Besides, whether you knew or not is no excuse.
After all, your job was to 'track the Infected.' Did I not say a long time ago that our jobs would be changing?
Now now, what's with that icy expression? Oh, were you perhaps born with that face, little pegasus?
How seriously can I take someone who submits seventeen or eighteen time-off requests a month? ...Ah, forget it.
Count your lucky stars that the whole thing about the Radiant Knight rushing into an Infected quarters to fight off a group of mysterious assassins didn't make headlines in the major news outlets.
Though the media are still our valued partners, suppressing these kinds of things costs a lot of money. And let me tell you, they don't pass up on these juicy opportunities.
...I'll accept responsibility for these losses.
Your self-awareness is commendable, but you can't afford to 'accept responsibility' for many more mistakes. After all, the Board of Directors only pays you so much, yes?
Sorry.
Hah. Enough of that. Playing the villain isn't exactly my strong suit.
We're still work partners, right?
If you think so, then so it must be, Lord Roy.

Tisk tisk. When you call me that, doesn't it seem like we're strangers? 'Platinum' Centaurea... See, doesn't hearing that make you feel uncomfortable? Why don't we go with a nickname instead, my little pegasus?
Kazimierz has many pegasi.
It's fine as long as you know I'm referring to you... Oh, right. There's just one more thing, though it's not a very big deal.
If you're talking about it, there's no way I can treat it as anything less than a big deal.
Then it appears that you misunderstand me. I'm willing to talk to you about all sorts of things, from small talk to important matters... Haha.
Mm. I heard you spared the life of Szewczyk's runt?
Czarny was already removed from his post at that time. I wasn't in the mood to listen to him and kill a kid.
Ah, so frank. That's fine.
Though I also feel bad for Czarny, that isn't the real issue here... Put simply, Szewczyk has gone missing.
Plastic Knight does not come from an esteemed lineage. He's just another tournament upstart.

Though we didn't have any plans to remove him from the picture, he is the property of the Roar Guards Co., and it never bodes well to let someone else take the initiative. We'll definitely get complaints from the K.G.C.C.
Did you find out who did it?
The Infected.
I don't understand. How could a few Infected knights accomplish all this?
Who knows, little Platinum? That's exactly what we're trying to figure out right now.
But there's one thing you shouldn't misunderstand.
Oh do please illuminate me.
What we face... is not just a few meager Infected, but rather all the Infected of Kawalerielki.
Mieszko Industries just delivered eight new plates. The purpose of the plate called Area 0 will soon be brought to light.
I've mentioned this before, right? We're going to be real busy.
I understand.
Good, as long as you understand. *cough* *cough* Hmm...
*hiss*... It's too damn hot today, don't you think? How nice it must be to not have to wear a helm...
Hm, you can't see what's different about me?
Oh.
You dyed your hair?
Everything, including my tail. What do you think? Looks good, huh?
Yes, it suits you well.
What's the meaning of that annoyed look in your eyes?
Don't think I have to answer personal questions, do I?
But you see, you hold the title of 'Platinum.' White hair, clad in pure white. Monique is Victorian, natural blue hair, lazurite attire. An absolutely perfect fit.
But look at me, all dark and drab. Standing next to Monique, I'm so out of place, like I'm a janitor or something.
So out of consideration for our public image, I also decided on a color for myself, heh.
Hm?
As long as you're happy.
1:22 P.M. \ Clear
The Grand Knight Territory Kawalerielki – Urban Crevices, Infected Gathering
I'm back!
Took you a while, Sona.
Rude. Some stuff happened along the way.
Ashlock's mentioned it before. Back then, during that 'Industrial Incident,' was it the Radiant Knight that helped you?
Ahaha... Is this the celebrity effect? Feels like everyone already knows...
She...
What kind of person is she?
Oh, that's adorable. I thought you weren't interested in gossip.
My hometown...
Back home, there's an arena.
There were never any tournaments in that small arena. It was just built because that's what you do, you have an arena in a town.
But back then... was when I started to look up to the knights. It was also where I grew up hearing legends of the Radiant Knight.
It's been many years.
Really? You never mentioned that before.
And when did you tell me about what you were like as a kid?
Fair. Maybe that is something we should talk about later.
It was a long road.
The road to the Grand Knight Territory was so very long. I didn't come all the way here to reminisce about my old home.
We're just chatting, alright? No need to be so uptight.
Just chatting, huh...
When the day comes that we can sit around a patisserie with nothing to fear, we can grab Ashlock and Wild Mane and chat the afternoon away.
Good idea.
So... What kind of person is she?
Well... To be honest, ermm, we only made eye contact. I have no idea if she even saw my face, busy as we were.
H-Hey, don't look so down! It's not like I could just strike it up with the champion in front of the Armorless assassins.
But, she still rescued an Infected like me, even with eyes on her.
Hmm...
Maybe she really is the Radiant Knight. The very same... as the Infected dream about.
The what now–– Eh, whatever. How about the poor guy you rescued? The old one?
He...
You asking about me?
My arrow's nocked, Infected.
You'll help me make those lowlife assassins pay the price.
Amiya, and the Doctor of Rhodes Island, please come in. Treat this place as your own home.
Your rooms are upstairs. The doctors are working up on the twelfth floor.
Thank you, kind sir.
Haha... There's no need to be so formal, Miss Amiya.
I never expected the leader of Rhodes Island to be such a lovely, young Cautus girl. Ah, to be young and full of promise.
Th-There's nothing special about me...

opts: It's true, Amiya is the face of Rhodes Island.
Amiya's always making things happen.

lines.x: D-Doctor...
Haha, are you embarrassed?
What an adorable girl. If I had a granddaughter, I reckon she'd be about your age.
Oh...
My my, her face is beet red.
But, I must apologize. Considering your unique status, I can't arrange an in-person meeting for you two.
Mhm... Don't worry, that's perfectly understandable.
So... 'Gravel.'
Your orders.
This young lady here is a fourth-order campaign knight. You can call her 'Gravel.' She's very promising.
Hello.
She will personally take charge of your safety while you are in Kawalerielki. Should you need anything, just let her know, and she will be of service.
We cannot allow any misfortune to befall our distinguished guests from abroad, not to mention such excellent partners as yourselves, during the Major.
We understand, sir.
Thank you for your understanding. I've prattled on for long enough, so I'll leave you young people to your business.
Gravel, please take good care of the Rhodes Islanders.
As you wish.
Now then... I'll be standing outside the door. If something comes up, just call for me.
Right, okay. Thanks, Gravel.

opts: Amiya. Hey, Amiya. Is she staring at you?
Excuse me, but did you need something?

lines.x: There is one thing... Do you mind if I come a little closer? Yes, just like this, good.
......Mmwah.
Hm? What's wrong? Your face is beet red. That's just how I say hello, so don't think too much about it.
Anyway, I'm Gravel, a knight of Kazimierz. It's a pleasure. Hehehe.
What... What was that all about...?

opts: She said it's how she greets everyone.
They have a rather interesting way of greeting people here in Kazimierz.

lines.x: Err, umm... I have heard that sometimes nobles will indeed greet each other with a kiss... And, umm, seeing as how she's a knight...
That's probably all she meant with it, right? Probably...
...Haah...

opts: You're pretty nervous, huh?
I guess that's that.

lines.x: Yeah... I guess I have been a bit nervous.
But, that old lady seemed a lot more easygoing than the other Adeptus knights we met earlier.
How about you, Doctor? Are you tired?
Of course you are. We've been tied up with business and all kinds of formalities since we got to Kazimierz.
At least you can catch your breath now.
Ahaha.... Doctor, were you able to take a breather as well?
It would be nice if all the people in charge of the medical mission were as reasonable as that old lady.
But, this is a rare opportunity for us.
It's said that every few years, the Grand Knight Territory will select a few of the most rapidly developing cities to merge with its own core city.
It's a remarkable feat, if you think about it.

opts: The lights from the billboards outside sure are bright.
I always expected this city to be a lot more solemn.

lines.x: Mm... It's been a bit of a shock for me as well.
Even though... I've always heard Nearl talk about her homeland, I never expected the heart of Kazimierz to be like this.

opts: Want to take a look around?
Is there anything you'd like to buy?

lines.x: Hwuh? Ah, um, n-not really... I just don't get to visit big cities like this very often.
After all, how many Infected get to look out over a thriving city from atop a high-rise hotel...
If Rhodes Island didn't manage to secure a medical business contract, we'd never get to enjoy this luxury.
The only thing is...
I can't stop wondering about Nearl... and how she's doing right now.

opts: I was shocked when she suddenly announced that she was leaving Rhodes Island.
She is the Radiant Knight after all, and this is her home.

lines.x: Mhm...
Ultimately, we should trust her. This is her decision to make.
I just hope she's safe and sound...
But, since Shining and Nightingale are with her, she'll be fine, right?
Yup! Let's put our faith in Nearl.
After all, she is the Radiant Knight—a pinnacle of Kazimierz knighthood, right?
Oh, Doctor! Quick, look at that big screen outside!
Is that... Nearl?
Margaret, I'm done!
Based on the suggestions you made last time, I adjusted the center of mass and added a retracting function. Now, it should be able to...
...Margaret?
What's on your mind?
Oh, Maria... Sorry, what were you talking about?
You don't usually space out like that. What's wrong?
I heard some of the rumors while I was at Rhodes Island...
But this place... really has changed a whole lot.
Yeah... We've sold off a lot of the furniture.
You know Uncle. Were it not for him, we might've moved somewhere else last year.
Yesterday, someone from the Knights Association came by again to harass us. Both you and I, we're always representing some knightclub, or the family. Otherwise...
Don't worry. I'll take care of it.
Hehe, now that's how a big sister should be.
Oh, right, here's your weapon. Master Kowal and I tuned it up for you.
But, you haven't had any matches lately, right? How'd it get worn out so badly? It's like a bomb went off next to it.
I never realized it before. The sheer number of Infected living in the Grand Knight Territory.
...Margaret?
Margaret gazes wordlessly at the skyscrapers in the distance.
She can't forget the dark, garbage-choked underground, or the flickering glow of flames.
Now, the Infected can become knights. The Blood Knight did something incredible. Judging by the results, he saved many Infected from misfortune.
But, this is not what a 'knight' should be. The Infected should not be callously treated as playthings just because they suffer from a disease.
Sona... might've just wanted to help those Infected as well.
The factory is running, the mines haven't stopped. The city is still developing, and more and more Infected will end up at the bottom.
They were all going to be exiled into the wilderness... but Pinus Sylvestris paid for them. Bought all those Infected with no home of their own.
That's what Master Kowal told me.
Except, that doesn't change anything. They still don't have a place in this city.
Right... some Infected knights have done similar things before, but there's a limit to how many of the Infected poor the General Chamber of Commerce will allow to hide in the city.
...and the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi Kazimierz will?
The ruling class is even more reluctant to watch the Infected walk openly in their city, and they'll also take measures.
All the news talks about lately is the Infected, and Oripathy research conferences...
They're whipping up hysteria.
They media's trying to make ordinary people fear the Infected, even if it threatens to undo the stabilizing effect that the Infected Knights Act has had––
––and the scary part is, this kind of rhetoric from the media... almost always succeeds.
Don't always look so glum. No money falls into the pockets of the despondent.
The Radiant Knight is answering a higher calling, helping out the Infected hidden throughout the dark alleys of the city. How noble––
And then what?
Can she give them their lives back? Can she treat their Oripathy? Can she help the Infected fight back against the rest of society?
Who knows? Maybe she can.
Cut the crap, Toland.
Do you have nothing better to do? Why don't you leave the Grand Knight Territory and crawl back to your den of thieves?
Now that the two sisters are safe and sound, there's no more room for me here?
You've already been paid back.
That little bit of cash was just a formality. You know exactly the currency we deal in, Master Młynar.
Who gave you permission to come in?
Mmhmm, this carpet isn't half bad.. The Leithanian textiles are being auctioned off tomorrow, yes?
That's none of your concern.
The origins of those two Sarkaz knights certainly raise eyebrows. As a Sarkaz myself, I do pity the fate of those 'knights.'
But I must remind you, this is no longer part of our business arrangement. My work here was done long ago.
I had my own reasons for coming to Kawalerielki, since it's not something I do often. I wanted to see if we could come to a mutual understanding–– but it seems that isn't possible.
Now now, don't make that face. Our relationship is already much better than it was before, Your Excellency.
After all, I'm just a no-name bounty hunter. To the Grand Knight Territory, I'm nothing at all. As for you–– Oh my, haha. Sorry.
It would seem that you're nothing as well. I guess that makes us two peas in the same sickly pod.
Greetings, Madam Droste... What are you reading there?
A wayside poetry anthology, Mr. McKee.
Ah... you've always been keen on the like.
I still remember my first time seeing you, stood in that blood-soaked arena with the stench of iron, carrying a book like this too. Your surroundings were every bit out of place with you.
You recall the details quite vividly, it seems...?
Ah... haha, ashamed as I am to admit, I'm one of your fans too. If you're not busy, I must have you sign something.
It would be my greatest honor.
Excuse me, Mr. McKee.
I'll leave the wine you requested right here.
Thank you. Send my regards to the other staff.
Let's take a moment to celebrate, Madam Droste.
I would be dearly happy to.
A toast!
A toast.
*sigh* Truly, a fine wine. It's a coveted Gaulish bottling, and its value would boggle any mind. A worthy tipple for a day such as today...
And for a knight such as you.
You flatter me. I've simply been of good fortune.
This poetry anthology...
Authored by a poet of little name from my hometown. I very much adore the verses he pens.
Ah, a Leithanian anthology... the title means 'Twin Moons and Marigold'?
Your Leithanian is superb as ever.
Haha, it was simply necessary to learn for diplomacy. I'm guessing you like marigolds? We've very few Kazimierzians nowadays who know how to appreciate poetry.
In the end, the letter can't break free of our mind's eye. These are all but marks for imaginations.
To the contemporary man, poetry is no more than warrens of words lacking aesthetics, too self-assuming, too pretentious.
Perhaps Kazimierzians are accustomed to a different sort of consumption now, a kind poetry could never match. It's a very typical course of things.
Might you be... insinuating?
I'm merely discussing poetry, Mr. McKee.
Don't you have something else you'd like to talk to us about, Madam?
Oh... I wonder what.
Ah. Your new glasses suit you very well.
Haha... w-well, it's sharp of you to notice such things. What a surprise, haha...
*ahem* However, that's not quite relevant to you. No, there's been some prior rumors going around the intercity net and print presses...
Ah... The red wine baths?
Yes, it's been slandering your reputation...
Do people really bathe in red wine? Wouldn't it stick to them so?
Excuse me? I assume... I'm not sure.
*sigh*... And who would believe such news?
Some would, Madam Droste... many would believe it. Sure as they know it may not be the truth, they'll still let the information color them.
Could we not clear the air?
Ah, you always do take non-knightly matters too lightly...
I've gotten in touch with the Rose Paper Union HQ; it turned out to be some entertainment editor's doing.
It seems it had already drawn considerable attention by the time it was noticed, and with all the factors at play, no retraction came...
This is the way they work. I give them no mind.
You're very forgiving, but that strengthens my feeling you've been wronged.
You've donated practically all your income to the poor of Leithanien, to build schools back home, to set up nomadic platforms.
But even if you deny the rumors now, the masses know the taste of delirium already, and they won't care what the truth is.
Hurting and slandering are simple things. They'll grab hold of the rumors and spread the news.
And how many times have you seen, after the truth came out, those who'd worked against you turned to help you wash away the lies, to make the facts clear?
Gossip can be fresh and exciting to people, while the truth is an all too boring thing. By all rights, this is how it is.
*sigh* How are you so unconcerned?!
Others should see an example in an upstanding, honorable knight like you! This ought to be better for your reputation, and besides—
McKee.
Erk...
I'm touched.
You're all too thoughtful a person... and all too tender.
As a Chamber spokesman, ensuring no external factors are of disturbance to any knight is part of my duty.
That said, I'd like you to care more, please, about what happens to you.
Oh, I have a question for you.
When will we have the competition schedule?
*sigh*... How can not even your own matches concern you?
Provided nothing happens, your next opponent will be Graybeard Knight, followed by... the Radiant Knight.
Are you that shocked, Madam?
Of course. The Radiant Knight, Margaret Nearl... I've heard this name many, many times. Such beauty, firmness, and grace too.
The Chamber wishes you victory.
Mm... and yet...
A 'Candle' to the 'Radiant.' What chance do I have of winning?
Don't be like that. Knight titles nowadays are more for catchiness than anything else. They've got nothing to do with true power, Madam Droste.
You're very strong.
Oh—I've won!
Dammit! You cheater!
Ahahah, ol' V's still top of his game~♪ You're the big fat loser tonight, Kowal~♪
You belched on purpose calling 'start,' didn't you?!
Put the stool down, Kowal, that one's not built good. Get another one.
I win, Kowal~♪ You big fat loser~♪
What did I walk in on?
Arm wrestling. What are you carrying there?
Some medicine I just picked up...
Oh, for Młynar?
But he'll turn me the other cheek, I know that. Besides, I don't even think he'll start taking it on time...
They couldn't be more different, personality-wise. But there are some areas where he and the sisters are very much alike.
I heard him and Margaret had a real, proper duel once. Margaret won.
Who knows, maybe that's how you wake Młynar up some.
Pfeh, I don't reckon that boy's got anything to wake up to. He's awake as can be. Too awake, he is. That's what turned him so funny these days.
Oh, Młynar...
...No, never mind.
Hi, everyone! Oh, Zofia's here too.
Maria? What are you doing here alone?
Someone came looking for Margaret just now, so I came over ahead of her.
There aren't a lot of cases where someone's been exiled and then allowed to return as a full citizen. They need to check over so many legal documents...
Funny, that. Their bunch went to hell and back to catch Margaret out, and now they welcome her back with open arms?
Plotting something, aren't they?
That's what was haunting me before. Can't you two talk something nice for once?
We're just worried, you know...
If things really do go belly-up, we'll pack our bags and leave Kazimierz, head to that... Drohes Island whatsit to take a looksee.
...Rhodes. Rhodes Island.
Maria, how's your uncle?
Ah... I think Uncle Młynar's still at work. His shift might be over soon?
The Radiant Knight gave that boy a harsh talking-to, didn't she?
Hah, been a long time coming. If Old Man Nearl was still around, he wouldn't have stood for Młynar letting himself go like this—
Maria?
My sister could only make her return to the field because Młynar lost that duel. He's not going to have much more to say now.
Oho. So Młynar knows when to admit defeat, then?
But—But when they dueled, he didn't even use the slightest bit of Arts until the end...
What? Was he throwing that one?
Scared to go all out, gave himself an excuse for losing? That's my sad reckoning.
Of course not. Admitting defeat, throwing? What makes you think he'd do something like that?
He... he just can't stand Margaret. He can't stand the Radiant Knight.
He didn't deem her worthy of using his full Arts, pure and simple. Nothing else.
'Ashlock' Greynuty, have you seen Sona?
She's right over there... Why is my name the only one you put the title on?
...I can stop.
If you don't like it... I just felt—it's the impression you gave me.
...Whatever.
Sona!
Whaaat—?
Come on! Everyone's here!
Hup—
Alright, alright. Are you all better now?
My wounds won't get in the way... What's that? A figurine?
Ahaha, I'm a girl of my own, right? Is it so wrong to like cute things?
Sona...
Okay, I get it. No leeway for jokes right now.
'Industrial accidents' are on the rise. They're herding the Infected about, terrorizing them and the slums alike.
Seeing as the Armorless Union's dropped all pretenses, hiding away's not an option anymore.
Szewczyk gave us some very interesting information. We need to hurry and—
—Sona?
Huh?
You seem kind of distracted.
If one day... we win our freedom...
...what would you like to do then?
We'll cross that bridge when we come to it. We'll think about it together.
What's with you today...? Why so melancholy all of a sudden?
Ahaha, nothing. C'mon, hop to it.
Let's go meet Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub's new members.
Seems it's nearly all Infected here.
The National Council could be watching, right?
The Council don't hide when they're watching, and in the worst case, we just throw some money around. Money's the key to getting the law on your side.
But if this keeps up... will the Armorless Union stand by and let it happen?
The Armorless Union? Do they even really exist?
Best not doubt them. You'll be wiser for it.
Oh, look over there, the ones coming over. They must be the Pinus Sylvestris knights.
Huh. They look like a bunch of kids, at a glance.
But... maybe some things, only the young can do.
'Flametail' Sona.
'Ashlock' Greynuty.
'Fartooth' Justyna.
(Loud beeping noises)
Huh. I thought they had another one, a Kuranta...?
Are we going to even make it through alive, if we jump on board with these kids' plans? And if we do, what then? What about next month? Next year?
There's something Infected often say, my friend. It all depends on you.
We've got Oripathy. Plenty choose to resign themselves to that... our lives will be short, and they could end at any moment.
But at the same time, my thinking is that's no guarantee we'll die tomorrow. As long as we've got so many 'tomorrows' lined up in front of us, we should damn well be taking them, fangs out.
Our numbers are dropping.
We won't be able to save the Infected like this, Sona.
I know. That's why we need to find an opening, ASAP.
Hmph... Where's a couple of Infected-turned-knights' wealth and privilege going to get you in this city?
All the money you spend on charity for these Infected, all the meaningless work to conceal them, dragging out their miserable lives in shacks and gutters.
The city itself let you have all of this. You've got nothing close to an opening.
Ahaha, that's real hurtful to hear, Plastic Knight, sir.
What about your family, then, Szewczyk? Didn't the knight nobles win all those entrepreneurs over for you, heart and soul?
With the General Chamber of Commerce targeting you, you're no different from us. Take a long, hard look at yourself.
...Keh... fair enough, we're all trash smashed down to the dirt here. No real difference at all.
Your name was Greynuty? The knight nobility have been doomed since the moment all those years ago when the Affinity was established.
Don't tell me you hold onto any delusions about your old clan, little squirrel.
(Did you think he'd go for an argument with you?)
You're calmer than I expected.
Keh. You know what, I'm dying to get out of this place. Heaven knows when you're going to pass that Oripathy around—
But seeing how you're not going to die for the next while, let's focus our attention on other matters—
The Armorless Union.
The Chamber's scumbag lackeys. I'll give them something to regret.
On that note, you're all so willing to face off with the Armorless... so you must have some ace up your sleeve, right?
...Area 0.
Or in other words, the Grand Knight Territory's Infected assembly and treatment section. A place for Infected healthcare, formed by an alliance of several companies.
Aren't you Infected knights regulars in those parts? So you tell me. What secrets are the Armorless Union hiding there?
—Slow down, Szewczyk.
Of course we've got methods... but that doesn't mean we'll tell you about them just like that.
If that's what you really thought, Infected, you would've left me in that wretched hospital and waited for the Armorless to come torture me for answers—you wouldn't have busted me out of there.
Don't you talk at me like this. We're only here now because we all did what we had to. Would your status alone give you the standing to address me, Ashlock?
Or do you think your busting me out means I should be weeping over you with gratitude?
You arrogant noble—
Uh—alright, alright, I'm not against you two fighting it out here and now. Better right this moment than a stab in the back after the Armorless Union shows up, anyway—
—We've been doing this for a few years now, for better or worse... we have ins with some government channels.
There's the impoverished and Infected who can't even get by day-to-day, but do you know who else in Kazimierz hates the General Chamber of Commerce?
...Keh.
Either I've gone mad, or Kazimierz has. You couldn't be telling me a ragtag group of Infected are in deals with the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi to take down the K.G.C.C.?
Ahaha...
Look at where you are in life. Look at the knights, Szewczyk.
—They went mad forever ago.
A moment please, Mr. Malkiewicz.
Ah, Mr. McKee. Do... do you need me for something?
It's good to see you again. Have you gotten the letter from the Chamber?
The only spokesmen on competition duty for the duration of the Major will be us two... an arduous task, but a gallery of eyes will be trained on us.
If you want to continue your ascent up the ladder after you drop the 'spokesman' title, this is your greatest chance.
Haha... even if you're joking, I'm not really into the attention. I'd rather go off and handle arena event planning and stuff...
Is that your particular expertise?
It's not exactly expertise or inability or whatever, I'm just...
You're being modest again. You've adapted much faster than any of the more senior personnel.
—But I have something else I needed to tell you first. I need you to know in advance.
Let's find a lounge to talk in?
Yes, you're the boss...
What is it that needs such secrecy??
I... shouldn't actually be telling you this, but Mr. Czarny left quite hastily.
On each Major occasion, Kawalerielki picks three of the cities with the most rapidly developing economies to merge into the Grand Knight Territory you see now.

Within the few months following each Major, non-Grand Knight Territory cities aside, the nomadic cities return within their own one-month span to their original routes, following the route engineers' plans.
Each merging of the four cities is glorious. Marvelous flows of people, seamless trade relations, as well as the chance... to deal with a few problems.

To be honest, if it weren't for how excessively enormous the Kawalerielki Alliance is in scale, how unable it is to effectively move and avoid Catastrophes, and how difficult it is to handle supply distribution and mining...
I'd hope to never see the disbanding of the Kawalerielki Alliance.
But what I'm going to tell you today is about... mm... an unspoken rule.
Regarding...?
...The Infected.

This year, Mieszko Industrial Group has provided the Grand Knight Territory seven nomadic platforms, to rival other countries' whole cities in scale... of course, this is how it appears on the surface.
I heard about this from my colleagues.
The main thing we should focus on right now is Area 0. It shows up nowhere on the legal listing Mieszko Industries has given.
It is a unique plate—a small-scale nomadic platform, currently linked at the southwest corner, outside Plate #17.
That's where...
Yes. It's right next to the Infected shelter and treatment center.
Do pardon me, Mr. Malkiewicz, but every word we're about to exchange must be on video record.
This is by regulation. No spokesperson is permitted to act in any way which would betray the General Chamber.
...I understand.
You look pained. I understand. When I first heard of it, I felt much the same as you.
It's just, this is too cruel...
You are now an executor of the Board of Directors, a spokesman of the General Chamber of Commerce, Malkiewicz, my good man.
That's a reminder, nothing more. Of course it's not an admonishment or a threat—haha, Mr. Czarny always did carry that air, didn't he?
Get a grasp of it all. Know what he's told you. Do as he said.
I don't mean it that way, Malkiewicz. We have to help each other.
You need to chew thoroughly over what I've told you. I'm off to check on a few Knightclubs.
Be seeing you.
...Młynar?
Are you there? M—
Aunt Zofia?
Oh... Margaret. Why are you still at home?
Didn't Maria say you were going to the Knights Association? That girl...
It's rote procedure. It won't take much effort.
Heheh. You really have changed.
Is it thanks to that 'Rhodes Island' place? You never were any good with the paperwork side of things before.
Do you still remember when you'd just applied to become a knight? Independent knight Margaret Nearl, always getting her documents and processes mixed up...
Yes. I'm lucky I had you then, Auntie.
*Sigh*... I understand it with Maria at least, but aren't you the same age as me?
I spent so much energy getting you to call me by name. We're apart a few years and now that's undone?
We can't have gotten strange from each other, can we? Hm?
Heheh, don't worry, Zofia. It's just been a long while since I've talked with family like this, face to face.
Were you here looking for Uncle?
Yes. Is he still at work?
Still not home anyway.
You know, Młynar...
...I know.
I'll be civil with him, and I won't blame him either.
So don't worry.
Oh... the match's about to start.
Same as it ever was. Turnout's ridiculous.
That Blade Helmet Knightclub sap there, didn't he show up last year too?
Here, forget him, even their Left-hand Knight fell to Maria. What's the Blade Helmet Knightclub got?
Ugh... it was just luck, all luck. It's always embarrassing when they blow things out of proportion.
Oh. Seems like 'Brassrust' Olmer Ingra's in this match tonight too...
Ha, well-acquainted, aren't you, Maria—
—Learn to shut your mouth.
It's alright, Master Kowal.
I've already given up on becoming a competition knight. It just doesn't suit me.
That's what I'm saying! You've got a wide world of options open to you—no need to choose this or that! That's a bit of old-timer advice there!
Besides, you've made a mighty fine showing already! Cheers to you!
Speaking of, we still haven't done final testing on Margaret's armor, have we?
Her last suit had some decent work on it, but it still doesn't fit a Kuranta as big and brave as Margaret.
Ah... I already started on adjustments myself. I'll need a bit more time.
And there's her weapon...
When Margaret was new to the knight competitions, she used a warhammer. After that, she switched to a knight's longsword. Halos shone at her blade's edge.
Now—she's on a swordspear.
Hah! It's a Minoan beauty forged of D32 Steel. The ultimate in Originium Arts conductivity!
Really takes me back. She came knocking, asking if I could change her sword out for an easier one to handle—the very first time Old Nearl's granddaughter ever asked a word of me—
Enough nostalgia, Kowal, the Radiant Knight's down in the arena next week. Are you people even helping her?!
Repeat that? You think I'm anything like you? Nothing but drink and idle hands all day?
(Brassrust Knight's lined up for... capture the flag? Why's Bloodboil Knightclub squandered an elite knight on such a high-risk low-reward format...)
(His opponent is... Oh, they're all small-time Knightclubs...)
(...Hold on, there's an Infected knight in this...)
The main Major matches. Mm-hm.
When you became an Infected, did you ever picture a day like this?
Ever picture being in this arena, winning yourself more wealth than nine lives could make?
I know... I know.
It's been my honor to join Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub, Wild Mane.
Hah, just call me by my name.
Fine! Once I get that prize money, I'll take you for drinks, Iwona.
Deal! No one ever drinks with me except Ashlock. Here I was getting bored out of my skull—
—Do you still remember the rules of capture the flag?
I do. Stay inside the score lines and avoid direct clashes—
Hey, that's all the boilerplate Sona teaches you—if you ask me, you smash those noble knights' armor to pieces, you're the champion.
Anyone makes for your points, you sort them out. Easy as easy comes, yeah?
Ha, hahaha... if I can do it, yeah.
Have a little confidence. You don't sound off proper, they won't give you any respect.
If you want others looking up at you, it's simple as dumping them on the ground, right?
But signs say someone's out for you, hah. Didn't Ashlock beat him bad enough?
Olmer Ingra... is he only here to knock Infected heads?
He sure has a narrow mind.
Kid nobles, pteh.
Looks like the Ashlock thing hurt his self-esteem some. Puffed up bags of hot air don't even deserve to stand in the arena.
Ugh—man, I'm scrapping. Maybe I should find a chance to beat him up in private.
Now—will the knights please enter the arena—
Gettem.
Alright!
The kids are still waiting for us to get back.
...Gahah. Pinus Sylvestris...
The Infected, a living scourge... dares to call itselves knights?
To Hell with you!
Hyaaargh!
Damn you, Infected—with the nerve to—
Go on, leave. You don't deserve to be here.
Peh! I'll drag you out of the scoring pen at the least!
—! Ugh!
That's fine, I've still got my points—
Is that right? Oripathic filth?
Ungh?!
Tch, Ingra...
That's it! Brassrust! Keep it up! Get him out of the scoring pen!
Break his hand! Keep going! Don't let that Infected get away!
Shh! Keep it down! Look at the seats over there...
Huh? Why's she all alone there...? 'Infected Knight seating?'
Not too loud. She was looking this way earlier.
Hah, aren't there divider walls between the seats? She can't hear anything, right? Besides, you think an Infected would have the guts to climb over here to get at me?
This is a knight's swordsmanship, Ingra. All that blunt movement of yours really is unwatchable.
*Spit*... Infected refuse... daring to lecture me? Wait until I've flayed you and pulled your bones apart... then you'll regret it.
(He really is no brains, all brawn. I just need to wait while he loses his cool.)
(Oh, here it comes!)
Goddamned Infected trash—
—Trash? Why do I feel like it's you knights, resting on your laurels, who can't stand the heat?
Grgh! He can push me off?!
No, your tiny little playsword could never... is it Arts technique? Wind Arts? Or just pure driving strength?
Would I ever tell you? Olmer Ingra.
You—take this!!
(Wait, what—?!)
Tch, it really was wind.
It seems it's only good enough to bolster your movements. Far short of what deranged things the Wind Knight can pull.
(Analysis? So he isn't just a simple-minded moron...)
Hmph, Infected. That sword of yours has no Arts unit, yet still cast... disgusting. The Arts given to you by the Originium in your organs will not change the outcome of this.
That rotten Blood Knight is the same. Just because some Infected knights have cobbled together a false renown, he forces the common folk to recognize the Infected?
The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi have been just too kind to you all. Know how grateful you should be, Oripathic filth.
Grateful?
To you lot?
Hah, such attitude? The Infected crawling and struggling along Kazimierz's lowest layers, you genuinely dare to cast contempt on the knight nobles?
Do you know the price the Infected pay for our legal emancipation?
Hah, why should I know? I only know your coming death will be wretched—
Do you know how brutal a female gloompincer is when it's been snatched from its egg? Do you know, when Infected knights are selected... the tacit homicide that happens?
Oh, 'your coming death will be wretched.' Big words, Olmer Ingra. You just relish in bullying the weak. It's a miracle you have the guts to step into a deathmatch.
—Have you seen what happens to all the lives deemed cheap? You knight noble filth?
There's only five still standing their ground in the scoring pen?!
Kurde! I spent all my savings on this bet, and that idiot doesn't even touch the pen for half an hour?
Why's there still an Infected? Are they fighting 'Brassrust' Ingra?!
Hey! Get rid of that filthy asshole! Ingra!
Ingra! Ingra! Ingra!
Wh-What was that? An earthquake?
No, no, it's that Infected knight over there... she stomped the ground something fierce just now...
...Tch.
These dumbasses... starting to gang up on the Infected, huh.
How's that?! Are you hurting now, Ingra?!
Wha... am I being surrounded?
...Infected...
You have no right to be here.
Ugh—?!
Forfeit, Infected.
I don't like how Ingra does things, but neither my Knightclub nor my family would stand for the humiliation of an Infected sharing the podium.
You... love a good excuse, don't you...
Don't get me wrong. Only one can stand in the scoring pen in the end.
This is a brawl, Infected, but right now, you've made yourself the prime target.
...Feh.
Heedless wretches... once I dispose of this Infected, you're all next.
Sir Ingra... I'm wounded and would be hard-pressed to vie for the title, but at least for the moment, we should work together to topple that Infected slave.
These depraved Infected with the gall to talk their tripe at knights. You all know of the disturbance the Infected caused before, don't you?
Pinus Sylvestris Knightclub always was a suspicious lot. Rumor has it they orchestrate the illegal Infected congregations.
Exploiting the riches from winning alongside us to harm the safety of Kawalerielki, and even Kazimierz in whole? Should we be indulging such action?
...Illegal Infected?
All I saw was a tragedy of people without a home! A tragedy of people dumped out of the factories, the farms and the beast ranches!!
Less of the row, Infected.
If that's how you truly see it, you ought to go on your merry way back to the Infected shelters. No need to stay here, overwhelmed as you are.
Let's be brutally honest. You ache to put every knight to death, don't you? You're welling with rage, gasping for vengeance, aren't you?
Then what are you waiting for?
—You!
Ghakh—hah, hah—
Cur! I'll cleave your hand off and slap you awake with it!
(No, I can't stay any longer in this fight, I need to—)
Not today.
You lot... Get out of my—
Ungh?!
Dammit... What are you people doing?!
Naught but standing here.
You have nowhere to run this time!
Gargh...!
Oh-ho, your armor's up to snuff. That blow should've cleaved your spine in twain.
Gakgh... gakgh... *cough*, *cough* *cough*...
I don't understand why the Knights Association would allow your lot into the Major. Isn't your blood a deadly toxin?
Enough.
Infected, get out of here. Just give up.
You've already been gravely wounded. Any more, and you'll never win another match.
—Shut up!
You... you people... sanctimonious thugs...!
Ugh?!
Sanctimonious. Is that what you think of me?
We'll see how long you can keep up the sniveling little traitor act. You'll be kneeling, begging for forgiveness...
Hey, where are the judges? What the hell is this?!
Are these knights gonna just rig up their own shitshow right here?!
I—
Ingra! Ingra! Ingra!
Long live Brassrust! Long live Kazimierz! Long live the knights!
Get that Infected up! Make him get up!
Get him on his feet! Keep him fighting!! Let's see how tough he really is!!
...*Hiss*!
What? What the? St... Stay away from me, alright??
What say I toss you down there? Get you closer to the action? Huh?
Wait.. you...
What are you doing?! You're a KNIGHT and you're gonna assault the audience?
...What...
Where's security?! Security! There's an Infected assaulting civilians!!
You sonnovabitch!
Gagh...
Y... You...
Still have strength in you?
Guh—ulgh... blugh...
That's close enough, Brassrust, he's liable to die.
He might be Infected, but still a registered knight. We don't need the National Council pestering us over an Infected.
Tch! What are you gesturing at me for?! Huh? Who do you think you are?
One of us has to lose, Brassrust. There's only one victor in this match.
Exactly what I—
What is this? Wind? You still intend to resist?
Hah. You know, an electric fan destined for junk blows more of a breeze than you do, Infected. What's the matter?
...You'll... regret this...
Hoh...
Has he a death wish?!
The wind's—picked up? He's still got strength?
Ngh—
You!
All of you...
Hey, hey, look at the Infected!
What's he doing?! Is he casting Arts?
You—
—Arts? No! He can't keep this up—
Ingra... and the rest of you!
None of you have the first idea what the Infected have to go through... Did we get Oripathy because we wanted it?!
Families abandon us. Fellow soldiers abandon us. You people throw us into the beast arenas like trash, to bleed alongside starving gloompincers.
I'll have you—
I'll die here.
Right. This is an arena. A Major arena. But in the end, it was only ever built to hold greater and greater show tournaments.
No.
No matter where it is... what does it matter when an Infected 'has an accident?'
Don't resist, and I really will be beaten, wide awake, to death.
That's right.
I need to resist.
There's nothing left but to resist.
I'll make you... pay in blood...!
Ugh, the wind's getting stronger...
Hmph. If I hang around here, come soon enough, I won't be beating any of you. My Knightclub superiors will understand.
I was thinking I'd win by playing the bystander. If there's no chance, I'm out, I'm out! You deal with this yourselves!
Infected can cast without an Arts Unit. That's unfair.
I'll be reporting to Northwood Knight Order, Infected have to be constrained... We should withdraw for now, Brassrust.
Brassrust?
Not a sound... He wants to kill me, waste his very life on these Arts so he can humiliate me...
No! You can't run!
Hah! Why would I ever run?
I'll tear your miserable Arts and that face of yours into pieces, and dance with your blood under my boots—!
Come! Unmentionable Infected!
I'll... do you in.. I'll...
Oh.
When we divorced, was she the one who got the photo album?
Shame. I won't get to see my precious—
Wha—What happened, right at the end there? Did the wind stop?
Then...
Then Brassrust's hatchet... it...
No! Shit!
*pant*... *pant*... Such a floundering outburst, and where did it get you?
What's the matter, Infected?! Spent at the last moment?!
You mockeries of—
Hold on... is he...
Dead?
The Infected... died?
But––
Get the medics here! Now! This is an arena! An infection on this scale—
Brassrust! Get away from him!
He died...?
Hahah, hahahah... he tried casting that piffling burst of Arts, and died?!
This is why you Infected all—
Please vacate the arena, Brassrust Knight, sir.
The medics are stepping in. When we're sure there's no risk of contagion, we'll transport him in a sealed container.
...Tch.
Pinus Sylvestris—! Do you want every knight here your enemy?! Well?!
—Put him down!
We're taking these precautions for the safety of the rest of the knights as well as the audience. Please comply.
Brassrust, sir, please make your way out.
Hmph, filthy knight... I certainly don't want my life ruined by Oripathy...
Are you Infected too? Please calm down, this is a Major...
Ugh...
Can't you see he's still got life in him?! Move!
Now!
Steady. Are you looking to provoke the Knights Association?
Leave this to the medical personnel—
*cough*! *cough* *cough* *cough*—!
*gasp*...
Let me over!
Leave her be... she's Infected too. Get the audience evacuated first.
Are you alright?
Heh... not exactly...
Blood loss... least of my problems...
Aches in my infected spots... they're spreading... hurts...
Dear visitors: there has been an incident in the arena. We ask that you follow staff instructions for a prompt evacuation. The award ceremony will be postponed by two hours.
We repeat—dear visitors...
Wild Mane.
Go on.
...Please... you have to...
Sona'll find your wife and girl.
Don't worry.
Look! The Originium is spreading across him! We can't wait any longer, put him in the container now!
What are you doing?!
Preventing infection.
...You... damned...
Iwona!
Remember their faces!
Remember... These knights... These Kazimierzians... These cold-blooded murderers...
Don't let them get away with it!
It's your turn. Deal already.
What's your rush? Just let me think.
What've you got to think about with this few cards left?
We now report on an urgent news story—
An accident occurred at a capture the flag event held today as part of the Major Show Tournament—
—an Infected knight concealed his condition before the competition and sustained heavy injuries during the match, all while forcing Originium Arts—
—went into shock partway through the event, and passed away after aid proved ineffective—
...Infected...
Passed away? Died? Died on the field?
According to reports, medical personnel on the scene have finished cleanup of the field, and the arena will return to normal use tomorrow following decontamination.
With only three hours since the incident, the National Council have already received mass complaints, and look to revise the Infected Knights Act—
Revise the act? Pull the other one, they're just petrified of owning up!
When the Blood Knight came about, they were pressured to recognize Infected as legitimate. If they fiddle with the laws now, how are they going to save their reputation?
The question of the Infected's no child's game... you think 'reputation' could affect their policymaking?
Was the knight nobles' 'reputation' ever any worse? Decades ago?
Times are different. I can't say I know what they're thinking now, but no matter whereabouts, enforcing the rules takes priority over any objective results.
We've all suffered for it.
Cleanup has been completed. All knights who took part will be given a medical examination within the next three days—
With regard to this incident, the Knights Association is paying a correspondingly high amount of attention.

Currently, several Knightclubs have cosigned a statement to the Association on the accidental deaths of Infected, uncontrollable Originium Arts, as well as the risk of large-scale infection breaking out during the Major...
Haah—!
No problems, Maria.
Your adjustments to the weapon core are perfect. It's been a long while now since I've wielded something so effortlessly smooth.
Ah... mm-hm!
I made them especially based on your past fights after all, heheh.
I will.
If we're done with all the theory, we'll want a little practical combat to try things out.
Maria, keep to one side.
Ah... okay!
You take it easy now, both of you!
I will.
My, that's a good look in your eyes, Margaret.
This is for real now. If you throw, my self-respect will be very sore for it.
Of course.
Now, then—
—Accordingly, we have invited numerous experts on knight competition to publish their own views. We now go to the Knights Association Officer-in-Charge.
Ah. Good morning, people of Kazimierz—
—Infected can cast without an Arts Unit; this is known to medical science—
But in the arena, such an ability is 'exceedingly unfair'—
(Fairness.)
Fairness, huh.
And surmounting the rulesetters within their own rules? Lunatic ravings.
Ugh...!
Careful.
Are you alright?
Oof... I—I'm fine, now let go...
I'm sorry, it's been a while since I used a weapon with a handle this long. I'm still not quite perfect at exercising it.
*Sigh*...
—Ma-ri-a? What are you laughing at?
Eh? Ah, I'm sorry, sorry.
Margaret.
You really have changed.
Hm? Have I?
I don't mean your appearance. Your hair's all messy now. Come here, I'll comb it straight for you.
Maria, bring the comb over. It's behind your worktable.
Ah, okay.
Let's sit and take a moment to breathe.
Alright. Thank you.
Honestly... it feels like it's only been the blink of an eye since you left.
(The blink of an eye... and you'll be even farther away from us.)
—According to reports, the deceased Infected appeared to be implicated in the illegal Infected incident. The relevant circumstances are still under investigation.
The Knights Association promises that an incident this minor absolutely will not affect the course of the Major's schedule—
Zofia... that's...
Ingra again, is it? For him to be killing on the field, though...
—Currently, visitors to events involving Infected knights are asking for the Knights Association to resolve the situation.
At the same time, 'Brassrust' Olmer Ingra and several other knights who were directly involved in this event have taken interviews—
Are you listening? Mr. Malkiewicz?
Oh, sorry, Jewell. Carry on.
Yes, sir. Actually, they're forms from the Knights Association, with the personal details and noteworthy points for all Grand Knights taking part this time.
The General Chamber would rather not ruin relations with these stars, so they're hoping we can be more attentive...
Let me see...
(They're all established athletes... oh, the Radiant Knight really is in here, too.)
Hm?
Does this 'Nightmare Knight' not have a Knightclub affiliation? I thought the Radiant Knight was the only case of an independent qualifying for the Major?
And besides, 'Nightmare Knight'... Aren't the Grand Knights' titles usually less of a mouthful? And he doesn't have a 'The' either?
He's not one of the Grand Knights, then? So what's so special about him?
Ah, this one... haha. He's been a great pain for the Knights Association to handle. I don't entirely know what's up, but...
He doesn't care a single bit about knight titles or any such thing, and brushes off all arrangements the General Chamber makes, but he isn't deliberately disobedient either.
He's a weird one.
A weird one, huh?

Yes, sir, but there's no doubt about his strength. And besides, I don't know why, but it seems some knight families are extremely curious about his identity. They've tried asking the Association countless times already.
But we're not too clear on what's going on with him either. Things suggest he came from some rural area near Sargon?
Mr. Malkiewicz?
...I understand. You're free to go.
Yes, sir.
Oh, one thing, Jewell.
Hm? What is it?
I'm still the same Malkiewicz you know... so no need to be so reserved with me. I'm not used to it.
Ah... as you wish, I mean, understood, Mal—Mr... Malkiewicz.

lines.st: How should the National Council rule? With a severe incident on the inaugural day of the Major, what can the Knights Association do to respond?
After this commercial break, we'll be bringing you a live broadcast of the Knights Association press conference.
—Dun-dun♪♪
Blue Ear Wine Cellar—The traditional choice for three centuries, the taste of chivalry—

lines.x: Welcome back, Iwona.
You look like murder.
No shit I do.
Didn't get to mangle every last inch of those knight sons of bitches' armor... grr.
It wasn't your fault.
He didn't tell any of us how bad it'd gotten.
With an infection like that, he should've stepped aside months ago.
He should've found his way out of Kazimierz, followed his dreams. Should've gotten to sort out his final days.
Olmer Ingra... the Kawalerielki knight noble to a T. Despotic, ignorant, no regard for human life.
I should've broken all four of his limbs in the arena when I had the chance...
It's not your fault either, Ashlock.
Dammit. And I was right there!
Did he... say anything... in his final moments?
You wouldn't want to know.
On the contrary, I wish I could.
Fine.
It's not rare at all... for Infected to pass on with regrets.
We'll remember his anger.
Sona?
His name was Jamie. He was a competition knight, and then an Infected after an accident.
He went through a divorce. He had a sweet little girl. He must've been underreporting his condition so he could keep on competing... He needed the money.
But in order to underreport his condition, he'd have to bribe the Knights Association.
I found the letters between him and the Association staff in his room. It was a bottomless pit.
He was the optimistic type. So optimistic to the point he couldn't even tell the sort of burden he was shouldering.
He caught Oripathy, he parted ways with his wife and daughter, but he kept on helping people.
Do you still remember? He was always up at the crack of dawn just to look after the kids.
He shouldn't have died in this indignity. It goes against the founding intent of our Knightclub, but...
But I can't get over it...
It was the end of the end, and someone so filled with passion for life, in his final moments, let loose such haunting, unforgettable hate.
If this is how things are—
...Sona.
You've had too much on your mind lately. Careful you don't get so fixated.
...I'm gonna leave all this knotty thinking work to you guys.
I'm gonna get stronger. Strong enough to crush those sanctimonious bastards between my fingers. Strong enough to clear the way all by myself.
The Blood Knight's a fine example. Since at least Kazimierz recognizes a 'knight' these days, that's the path we should be take.
Don't overthink stuff, Sona. It'll only slow you down.
...Sona!
Alright, alright, I get it. We need to step it up and check with Szewczyk—
The wind... there was a change in the wind's stirring. Even if just for a moment...
Someone's watching this place, and not just that...
The Armorless?
They know how to mask their breathing. But if they're after us, they should've attacked by now.
—Come out.
This is Infected territory. No uninfected would ever just wander in here.
If you don't—
Alright, alright. Keep the noise down, sheesh.
Never dreamed I'd see Infected knights around. I haven't been anywhere near Kawalerielki in over ten years, but the knight bosses and businessfolk are all out of their minds.
Who are you?
Don't get me wrong, I was in the neighborhood looking for someone. But, well, this might just be meant to be...
...I'm thinking we share some of the same interests, my Infected friends. Like... the Armorless Union.
Sorry, but we've been through some very unfortunate things today. If you really want to chat, try some other day.
Oh? That's some rotten luck indeed. My condolences.
Then I can only go somewhere else and try my luck there. Not every day I visit the Grand Knight Territory, after all.
I'll leave you all be, then... but we might see each other again very soon.
I found the knight who took Szewczyk. The runaway Fartooth Knight.
She's mingling with Pinus Sylvestris, of course. Infected knights have a way of sticking together—
—What's that noise on your end? Are you at a bar again?
Fine, looking into the Nightzmora's background, that's a decent excuse. When's the order being issued regarding the Infected?
...What?
Didn't the Szewczyk case end up like this precisely because they were 'still officially meeting to decide?' They sent a whole drone to bomb him—what point is there in still...
Well, jeez... tell me all you like, but the Major's running right now no matter what you say. They can't be all that cavalier about deciding.
Yep, mm-hm, I'm not slacking one bit, trust me.
It's true—hey, boss, you got any fries?
One moment.
Cool. Hey? Monique, you still there?
...Classic, she hung up.
Here's your fries, sir.
Oh, nice. Appreciate it.
Mm...! Who's the genius who came up with slicing a tater this thin and frying it too? The world wouldn't be so beautiful without you, whoever you are.
Come to think of it, that real standout Maria Nearl, she graces this place pretty often with Whislash Knight, if I'm remembering right?
If you came looking for an autograph, you'd better be extra lucky.
After all, this bar's for nothing but old retired fogies.
Who's that there? Couldn't be an Armorless Union spy?
God knows, he hasn't tried anything. Not like we can frisk him, either... hey, is he sizing you up?
Hrrm... just ignore him.
I've been getting the twitchiest eyes since last night... Can't shake the feeling something big's going to happen.
Finally hit the right age for insomnia?
Eh? I'm fit as a fiddle! What do you know! It was the twitches back then that told me we had incoming artillery fire—
What the hell big's going to happen to you? Are you going to squeeze olive oil out of your toothpaste tomorrow morning?
—Hmm.
Marcin. I can sense their aura now... it's someone major.
I've never had a premonition strange as this before... here's hoping they won't make trouble.
(Here they come. Nice and fast, too... mmm, I'll just let it happen.)
–– (Ancient language) The young hunter sets out on his Khaganquest ♪
–– (Ancient language) From dreams he goes, towards the shores of gold ♪
(This language...!)
What's going on? Kids these days wander into bars singing their own songs? What's the point of the jukebox, then?
The Nearls' light can't really have stuck to my lonely little bar, can it? But I've never met a knight like you in my time... V? What's wrong?
I... I don't know, it's just suddenly my heart feels a little...
(Ancient language) ...Batbayar, brethren, the last descendant.
(Ancient language) Why be in a place like this?
You...! You know that name?
The silent knight looks around. Where his gaze touches, the light hides.
Answer me. How do you know that name?
Hey, V, this a friend of yours?
Would you like a drink? Sir? If you've got no business, I have to tell you we don't welcome armed knights too warmly here.
(Ancient language) How sorry this is.
Hold on, don't just up and go—
Kowal, Maria will be borrowing your workshop a while longer—
—Er.
Zo—Zofia, slow down... agh!
Why did you—stop, what's going on?
...You are...
...Nightmare Knight?
What's the rising star of the year doing here? Are you trying to sneak out intel on Maria and Margaret?
If so—
How clamorous.
Watch out!
Wh... What are you trying to do here!?
Pegasus... a weak race. Only those pegasi with blood of flowing gold are worthy rivals.
But once the pegasi abdicated, this so-called 'Land of the Knights'...
Its grasslands turned feeble. You cannot be blind to it, can you, Kuranta?
In present Kazimierz, you people, all are called knights?
And in those... pompous arenas, you test your martial skills?
After your little act just now, I'm not finding the will to talk to you all nice and happy.
Hold it... boy, I still have things to ask you. The last thing I want right now is to put a bolt through your head.
Not bad... you have the will to fight still.
What about you, then?
Uh, me? Like, me? I'm just a fan of knights, just in the neighborhood. No need to waste the effort pretending I exist, I'll be fine.
I can't do a thing anyway, not a single thing, mm-hm.
(Tremoriron Marcin, proficient with warhammers. Vogelweide, campaign knight marksman. Whislash Knight, not much of a threat since she was injured... Maria, no need to monitor for the time being.)
(But none of that really matters... mm-hm... the Nightzmora came looking for Vogelweide after all...)
(Alright... now... what to do. Will the Radiant Knight come?)
This place is not worth even a quick visit. Keeping company with you people makes one uneasy to stand still.
Alright, boy, who d'you think you're looking down on?
(Ancient language) Batbayar, in the past you campaigned on the battlesands, fought with your own two hands, yet could only barely manage to protect your homeland.
(What's he saying?!)
War... you're a campaign knight? You recognize me?
(Ancient language) No, I'm no 'knight.' Nothing so ridiculous. Simply put, war is the only path in which you never shamed your ancestry, descendant.

(Ancient language) Though you stand here... in this city of steel and concrete, neon flitting through the rain, descendant, I hope you can still remember the breathing of the grasslands, and the vow the Khagan swore.
Khagan? What...?
You're going on about some stodgy old tale?
(Ancient language) I intended... before setting out once again, to seek my surviving brethren in this so-called land of the knights. It seems, now, I was simply deluded.
(Ancient language) No matter.
(Ancient language) Kazimierz's frailty spits on Khagan's accomplishments. Someone must set such conduct straight.
Stop right there! You still haven't answered me!
Hm... Though now you've lost all that made you, you're still at least half a brother... I should answer your question.
It was an old veteran who told me your name, and he, he was ruined in his age, not even able to run.
I seek every one of my brethren in Kazimierz. No matter how weak their relation, I seek them and nothing more.
Come again? Brethren? In Kazimierz, is that right?
It's no longer your concern, Kazimierzian.
Hold it.
Nightmare Knight... I've heard plenty about the badly-behaved newcomer you were.
You should've known we wouldn't let you go so easily after you kindly brandished your sword at us.
How about you take off your helmet and we have a nice chat?
'Nightmare'...? Ah, the title those city slaves plastered on me without permission?
Ridiculous... that knights are so obsessed with these names now...
It is only their inherent beliefs, and desire to grasp destiny, that urge knights to embark on their journey.
The Knights Association won't be happy to look the other way with you picking arguments like this. Do you want to be stripped of Major qualifications?
If... only vapid knights like you remain in Kazimierz... then I really am wasting my energy. What harm would losing worthless qualifications be?
However, this pegasus by your side... seems to very much resemble someone I wanted to find...
What?! You mean... my sister?
—What do you want with the Radiant Knight?
What I want...? Look at all the Grand Knights Kazimierz elevates as champions—if there remains any opponent worth conquering in this present country, I fear it may be among them.
Of course... it may also happen that they're all disappointments.
You've gone mad, haven't you. You came here just for a fight?
(Ancient language) Mad?
(Ancient language) Yes... the people who choose to live in the Kazimierz of now often tell me so...
(Ancient language) ...which is why we are no longer brethren.
I said stop! Are you just leaving like nothing even happened?
Given this endeavor is fruitless, I have no need to stay...
Goodbye, descendant.
Grr...
What in the world was wrong with that man, all high and mighty...
I can only hope Margaret doesn't meet him in the arena.
Seems he came for a quick look at you, then? Is he from your home place?
He's... a Nightzmora?
What? There's still Nightzmora alive today?
Here I thought they were all in legends, or they were holing up in Sargon or some sort.
So what? What's someone from your grandpa's grandpa's side doing finding you?
...He's calling to us.
...Tch.
Oh! Oh! This is history, being made before our eyes!
Can 'Left-hand' Tytus Topola lead the Blade Helmet Knightclub in a breakthrough here, and return to his spot as one of the top sixteen tyrants?!

This battle reeks of revenge! All of you watching! I hope your blood's on fire! He's fallen short of the Final 16 over and over, but today, will 'Left-hand' Tytus Topola have what it takes to overcome himself?!
It all comes down to one thing—his opponent!!
In the long and storied history that is the Topola family's knightly record, only one time! Only one opponent! Has brought such colossal shame!
Damn you...
What have you been doing, all these years?!
(Why... to hell with this...)
(How do I fight now? Do I make some room? No, she... hff... why is she using a swordspear, of all the things...)
(Most important...)
(...is that she... somehow has me... pressured?)
Tytus Topola shuts his eyes.
He considers this a scene of impassioned vengeance, a long-brewing opportunity to prove his worth.
But all his resentment, visions and fervor, have here and now vanished like smoke.
He cannot help but concede, this is no standoff.
Only a challenge.
A challenge, striding for the pinnacle.
(Since I became the Blade Helmet Knightclub's frontman, when was the last time I felt like this?)
A challenge, an advance, climbing to an ever higher domain.
Hah... heheh, it makes me want to laugh...
The Radiant Knight, are you truly the same woman?
To force me back to this degree... and yet you don't feel a hair of pride, not a drop of excitement? Where did the mettlesome you of yesteryear go?
Look at you now, so serene one could gag—
I don't mean to look down on you, Tytus. I never have.
However, you've already lost your way.
In the hunt that was the melee, at least, you were the last one standing. You forced your way free on nothing but a single arm like you had originally done, imbued purely with the desire to win.
Hahah... you've even learned to preach, now? Could it be? Your days of banishment have made you the lecturesome kind?
...Tytus.
Why would you let those logos bind you?
—Listen, Radiant Knight!
Weren't you always prattling on and on about those rancid traditions... urging us to—use the most traditional methods to decide victory?
A charge, a skirmish, an all-expending effort.
I will trample... all that you are!
Left-hand Knight is composed and steady! Does he plan to decide this with one final plunge?!
And the Radiant Knight is—now the Radiant Knight's slowly raising that bizarre weapon—
Who will be stand victorious after this final clash? What are you waiting for, audience?! You have the power to show your support for these knights, so do it—!
Is that the Candle Knight, Droste? What's she here for?
Maybe she's waiting for someone... quit staring, hurry up and sneak a picture... who knows if we can hit on some major scandal...
The Candle Knight! Madam, I'm very sorry to leave you unattended. Are you looking for someone?
It's alright, it was brash to come without notifying anyone. I just wanted to take a look at how the Radiant Knight cuts her figure.
After all, I imagine it's almost over.

Yes, Left-hand Knight Sir Tytus Topola lost ten minutes ago to the Radiant Knight. Sir Tytus has incredible strength, but he can't be too hopeful after falling into the losers' bracket in the first round.
Indeed.
Did you... really come just to meet the Radiant Knight?
I want to see. See just how different she is now, compared to what I've heard in the rumors of knights.
All the crowd's gone rushing over.
R—Right. Actually, ever since the Radiant Knight officially returned to competition, she's barely cooperated with our standard service activities.
She constantly leaves arenas without saying a word, and scheduled interviews and all sorts of PR items get rejected. It really makes your head spin.
Though actually, I think there's even more reporters mobbing the Radiant Knight than usual today. I wonder what that's about?
Ah... sorry, can you just let me through...
Miss Margaret, what are your views on the Infected knight who passed away on the field? Will this affect you in any way?
Madam Radiant Knight, do you have anything to say about the Knights Association's recent decision? Can infection actually contribute to an unfair playing field?

Ah, Miss Margaret, is it possible to reveal anything about the state of your infection? Can you confirm if the Knights Association's measures to keep it confidential are a disservice to the spectators and the other knights?
There are reports of you getting close to a few Sarkaz women! Who are they to you? Give an explanation, please!
Madam Radiant Knight! Please respond—do you intend to become the spiritual leader of the Infected knights?
—Will you be the second coming of the Blood Knight—
I won't be answering your questions.
Can we take that as an admission? Then what relation do you and those Sarkaz—
Well met, Madam Nearl.
And you are...?
The Candle Knight, Droste! The Candle Knight was waiting outside the arena for the Radiant Knight!
Give the schedule printout to me... that's right, they'll meet each other on the field... hey, get a snap of this, quickly! Snap it!
May we... walk and talk?
(I presume you don't want the media haranguing you any longer, either?)
Ah... yes.
Right then. Let's go somewhere else.
I'm sorry, please keep your distance—the press conference will be held at a later time, please can everyone just be patient for now! Patience, please!
Now then...
You've been through a lot, legend of Kazimierz.
Your banishment has conversely cast much of a veil of mystery on 'The Radiant Knight'... the holy, haloed knight, the awe-inspiring golden pegasus, the hero who returned like a shooting star...
I saw as I came close today, you were still very young, and disconcerted by such a sudden situation... Margaret Nearl, this is the real, living person.
Come what may, I thank you for helping me just now.
I assume you're a knight too, though I don't believe I know you?
It's been a while since someone asked me who I was. More than who I am, they care who they wish I was.
Er, I'm very sorry. I haven't been back in Kazimierz for too long...
...Heheh.
Are you concerned that because you don't recognize me... I may fly into a rage?
I can tell, you're quite unlike anyone else.
My, now, thank you for the praise.
I'm very pleased myself to get to know such a Radiant Knight. My name is Viviana Droste.
Or perhaps, you may also call me The Candle Knight.
The Candle Knight... no, Miss Droste, thank you once again for your help today. Were you seeking me for any reason?
Before long, we'll face each other on the field.
You are an Infected, and one who was once met with banishment. Make no mistake, I have no habit of connecting with my opponents before a match.
I come from Leithanien; I am an expatriate, living in this bustling city under an identity I could never imagine.
Kazimierz's recognition is already yours, Miss Droste.
I hear the Radiant Knight never approved of the current system of knighthood, and so, you were consequently banished. Do you still feel the same way?
The knights of Kazimierz are forgetting their obligation as we speak.

Have you ever been to the most destitute corners of Kazimierz? Conflict, starvation, infection... the common person suffers enormously, while the knights hide in their cities, making great shows of peace and prosperity.
The spirit of knighthood is almost completely lost. It's a legacy for capital to wantonly toy with.
How is it not a form of disgrace?
...I see.
It seems—it is you who stands as a true knight.
But you are... still no more than a knight.
How about your sister? She's been active on the field for the past while.
Has she renounced... the path of knighthood?
Miss Droste... and Miss Margaret, good evening.
Good evening, Mr. McKee.
Thank you for tending to the commotion just now.
...*Sigh*. So you do have that much self-awareness...
With two Grand Knights such as yourselves, the masses could make each and every move a hot topic. Put the two of you together in front of the media...
But never mind all that, there's always some way to sort it out. Miss Droste, you should be preparing for your meeting with the Leithanian noble tomorrow.
If you would, I'd appreciate you avoid exacerbating uncertain factors for the next while.
Such as, meeting with an Infected knight?
It seems the noble in question has come for your sake, Miss Droste. You have a celebrity status to the Leithanians too.
Mieszko Industries is very willing to assist them with construction planning in Leithanien, but the one putting the final seal on this trade contract is you, Miss Droste.
Of course... it is my obligation.
It seems I must apologize, Madam Radiant Knight, that I'm unable to continue my chat with you tonight.
It's alright. You gave me a pleasant surprise... that Kazimierz still has knights such as yourself.
The next we meet will be on the field.
Yes.
I look forward to the day.
A final word of advice... Madam Radiant Knight.
The storm is nearing.
The trails will be especially radiant in the starry sky, these coming days of ours.
I've been hearing you reported Olmer Ingra to the National Council?
True...
If a fatality during the Major isn't enough to warrant punishment, then what does the law even...
We're all well aware of how the National Council operates, Malkiewicz, my man. You had to deal plenty enough with them in your entrepreneurial life, didn't you?
Don't spend too long at a loss. You have a trip to the Infected intake-treatment zone in a short while. You need to make the Knights Association and a few healthcare firms see eye to eye.
Think on the bright side. This work is saving lives in and of itself, isn't it?
Don't get too worn out. You'll grind yourself to dust at this rate. If you'll excuse me, I'm off to a meeting.
...*Sigh*.
Hello, Malkiewicz speaking...
Spokesman Malkiewicz, hope you're having a good day.
I'm a deputy justice of the National Council. Your suit has reached us, Spokesman.
Ah...
How do you plan to proceed?

It is the obligation of a spokesperson to report those knights out of line with the regulations of the General Chamber. As such, we will prioritize our considerations your... complaints against Brassrust Knight.
However, matters with said Knight Olmer Ingra are somewhat complex.

You should be aware it was not long ago that Ingra's application for bail was given fresh approval by the National Council; to try him again now could have adverse implications for the National Council's authority.
We consider, of course... that the spokesperson's demand equates to the Board of Directors' demand. Such as is the only interpretation we can make, we would not refuse you.
Which is why we're having this call. I understand. What are the National Council's conditions?
