So you have the Radiant Knight and Młynar. The two of them together might put up a little fight, but what can they really do? Can they really take on the thousands of assassins here in the GKT and escape legal liability?
I don't remember you being such a talkative gal... Are you nervous?
Maybe.
I mean, before, all I had to do was draw my bowstring, aim, and let go. Then, my target would die after a quick flash of pain.
These orders I've been getting, though? Not only do I have to go after kids now, I also have to arm wrestle with the Radiant Knight. *Sigh*... Work sucks.
The Armorless Union's really gone to hell lately, hasn't it?
Report!
Did the Radiant Knight give up?
No, it's just... E7 and E9 didn't answer during the regular check-in just now...
Why are you standing there, then? Get ready to fight.
All units, deploy immediately. Secure vantage points A1 and A2. Do not give the target a chance to escape.
Numbers three and four, cover me. We'll deal with the infiltrators.
(An explosion? They aren't the least bit worried about the hostage, are they—?)
(—No, that's not right. They're not here to rescue Maria.)
Hostiles in the smoke! Watch for attacks—
Nngh–...
(Loud clicking noises)
What's this...? A delivery cart?
Well, hello, Armorless lackeys.
Grr... Infected knights, huh...?
Whoa, huge backflip. Look at that distance. You're a nimble one—Hm?
Maria Nearl? What are you doing here?
It's... it's you...
Hey, can you move?
Y-Yes!
I don't know what's going on, but I ain't about to complain if this means the Radiant Knight's gonna owe me a favor.
Let's get this out of the way first. It's a serious crime to assault the Armorless Union. Haha, not in the legal sense, but whatever.
You can run away if you want, or—
—If I don't stop them here, they will keep going after my sister.
Whew, not a bit of hesitation. I'm starting to like you.

Too bad, though. I'm not gonna have it on my conscience to drag anybody into our fight against the Armorless. Besides, I get the feeling Sona's pretty fond of you. You'll have to do this another time!
Toland!
W-What?
Huh? Who are you?!
Come with me, will you, Young Lady Nearl? Oh, and by the by? You and your uncle are nothing alike.
Report to the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi... It's confirmed. The Armorless Union and the Infected knights' skirmish has begun. Maria Nearl's been taken away too.
It will do as long as the Radiant Knight's match goes uninterrupted. Over.
How long has it been since the match started? No one in the arena today has ever seen two knights duel like this!
Even Arts laymen can tell right away theirs are on another level!
This is a battle of lights! There's no doubt this has become the theme of this match! A weak candlelight versus a full-blown radiance!
Up till 9 o'clock, we've had two million viewers watching this match via the intercity net and other means!

For the first time since the 22nd Major, when the Radiant Knight took her title, we've broken the highest concurrent viewer record! There are countless viewers watching Margaret's heroics right this moment!
Ah, what a shame! I wish we lived in one of those sci-fi novels where fans from all the other countries could watch this match live—Ahhh!
The giant rays of light clash again—How blindingly bright! But amidst the rolling waves of light—That's right! The Candle Knight remains unscathed!
Phew...
Brilliant... Viviana.
With light and shadows... you weaved an untreadable swath of land.
I am honored that my little candle-flame could hold the Radiant Knight down for so long.
But you don't seem to be giving it your all. Are you perhaps worried about your family?
What are you doing is contradictory, Margaret.
You take pride in having such wonderful companions, but the course that you have charted for yourself... will cause you to leave your friends behind.
Perhaps one day, your sister will meet her fate in one of the Armorless Union's conspiracies, and the General Chamber will stand in your way, wherever you will be.
Viviana.
When the knight charged toward the endless waves, did you imagine that he was trying to overcome the entire world?
Ahh, so you are familiar with that knight legend.
My sister's a big fan of the book.
Centaurea's been ambushed by the Infected. It was Wild Mane.
If she can't take care of the likes of Wild Mane, then she should hand in her resignation already.
That said... What should we do? The K.G.C.C. made it clear they want the Infected dealt with... and as soon as possible.
Hmm... They are quite a pain in the rear... but we need them to keep stirring up a ruckus for us...
You're our star performer. It's your call.
Oh? Was that a compliment? You know, I've always thought that I look kinda like some star out there, ever since I dyed my hair blue.
Okay, okay, stop glaring at me like that. It's very simple. Kill a few of them, and as cruelly as possible, too. Spread the fear.
The ones left behind will come to hate Kazimierz and the K.G.C.C. even more, and things will only get more intense.
So?
Eh, so, let's dump all the troublesome mess on our little Platinum.
Let's kill some Infected. The ones who aren't knights or in the limelight. Just the perfect amount of cruelty, like we're hunting them down.
...Huff...
I see, so the moment your candle goes out is when you actually start casting.
Even a brief moment of darkness is enough to swallow my Arts whole. In that case, perhaps you can engulf the entire arena's light with each flicker of your candle's flame?
Impressive observation. There has never been a knight who saw through my Arts during a duel. Is this the result of your years-long exile?
Too bad, then. It seems I have yet to truly make contact with you.
I used to hate Arts more so than anything. The more outstanding my Arts are, the more I am reminded of my upbringing—
And the more I come to realize... the situation I am in.
Tell me, Margaret Nearl, the Radiant Knight. Each and every knight who keeps the faith feels particularly curious about you.
Tell me your answer. Your answer of what it means to be a knight.
—Both the Radiant Knight and the Candle Knight have stopped. They're cautiously sizing each other up, taking a stroll around the arena—
What's the matter? Is this a tea party to the two of them? Are the two knights going to share a dance together after all that careful probing?
Hoh, did you hear that? He said we could dance.
Please, then.
Like the gentle morning sun, her beaming radiance begins to extend its reach toward the candle-lit darkness.
Soon after, the light begins to flood the entire arena.
Kawalerielki tonight is as brilliant as any bright, clear day.
The Nearl girl went this way, didn't she? Quick, find her!
—There's no one here!
How can that be?! This place's listed in her file! It's Tremoriron Knight's bar!
Check if there's an attic or an exit out back—
Misters, I'm afraid we're not open right now.
Don't you dare!
Erk...
Don't you kill them, V. Not unless you want shit to really hit the fan.
Hah, how about you have a guess at how many artillery casters I captured during the war all those years ago to earn my military honors?
Son of a...! How dare you?! We're—
The Armorless Union? How about before that? Were you a robber? A bounty hunter? A veteran, or perhaps a down and out knight?
Let me tell you. We aren't scared of you.
You dare cross the Union even once, the Union will hunt you to the ends of the world.
It might happen today... maybe tomorrow. It might happen when you're eating, when you're asleep, or when you're spreading marmalade on your toast... You'll have nowhere to run!
Thanks for the advice. *Spit*.
To be honest, though, the Armorless ain't nearly as scary as the vanguards.
Hah! I haven't felt as good as this in years!
Looks like their buddies are on to this... Sorry, Marcin, but that might be it for your shop.
Oh, that's too bad. I paid two years' worth of rent upfront.
You! What have you done?!
You dare resist—Form up, ready your crossbows—Uh?
The Armorless points his crossbow forward.
The bowstring has snapped. More accurately, it is being twisted.
W-What?
Take this!
Ugh?!
It's been a while since I used any Arts... Guess I've gotten rusty.
Hmph, since when did the Armorless have this many men?! The K.G.C.C.'s got too much money to throw around, huh?
Hah, that's why we wiped the floor with all of them!
Watch out! The bald guy's Tremoriron Marcin, and the other two are campaign knights!
Keep two squads here. Keep them alive. They will be our trump card against Nearl. The rest of you, find Maria—
Hey, this isn't looking good, V!
You want to try begging for mercy, then?
Ready, fire—
What? Who is it this time?!
You...
What have you done to Maria?!
Radiance. The word 'radiance' is starting to be wear on everyone in the arena.
The sun-like prismatic colors dye the area behind the Radiant Knight. Standing opposite her is a small candlelight that has lit anew.
Then, right when the audience is starting to grow tired of finding the two knights in the blinding glow...
The faint sphere—Its rays, its halos, and all its light blink away.
*Pant*... *pant*...
Viviana... You are truly impressive...
Look, my light... have been swallowed whole... *Pant*.
I'm honored to have exhausted the Radiant Knight.
But it seems you rely on more than just your dazzling Arts.
With her Candle Sword held up high, the Candle Knight begins to steadily lower her blade.
The candle's flame has extinguished, leaving behind a glistening brilliance on its milk white cross section.
The candle, now halved, flutters down toward the earth like a feather.
You've broken my blade. It seems I have lost.
I, The Candle Knight, Viviana Droste, admit defeat.
The Candle Knight has conceded! I didn't hear that wrong, did I—? The Candle Knight has just accepted defeat! The committee has confirmed her request. So there you have it—
After this excruciating, protracted duel, the winner is our young legend, the Radiant Knight!
The Radiant Knight, suddenly returned from exile, has defeated her first Grand Knight opponent after an extraordinary match of the highest level!

The Candle Knight may have suffered defeat, but there is no doubt she is basking in glory! The two knights are shaking hands and trading salutes! Take a good look! This scene is going to be all over the headlines the next couple weeks!
What?! The Candle Knight lost?! That's a couple months' paychecks down the drain!
Serves you right for not listening to me... Not that I earned much myself, but well... *Sigh*... This match's probably giving me some kind of eye disease down the line.
Hnnf...
To be a knight is to be the noble light that illuminates the land...
Hmm...
Viviana.
I won't comment on your background—and that matter that weighs on your mind. However, you are among the strongest knights I have ever met.
That is more than enough.
In the classic knight tales... the knights always opened their hearts to each other by crossing swords.
Reality has proven to be less romantic. Though I've only just met you today, Margaret.
Show me how far you will go.
I'm looking forward to seeing.
Tch—
(—I managed to block that strike, but I still almost lost my grip on my weapon—She's tough—)
My next shot will pierce through your legs. You won't be able to run anymore.
The next shot after that will pierce through your armor.
The final shot will blow right through your chest. You'll struggle in agony for a moment, before dying in the dirty, wet street.
Hah... No wonder Sona told me to run as soon as I ran into an Armorless elite.
You've really hit it big for someone your age. Bet the people higher up the ladder there are some serious monsters, eh?
You'd better pray you never find out—Hm?
Wild Mane! We'll cover you! Get out of here!
(Hurried beeping noises)
Alright, Justice, if that's what you say. Today's not the day for us to settle the score with the Armorless anyway.
See you next time, Platinum!
*cough*, *cough* *cough*...
She's gone.
What's her deal with explosives...?! *Cough*, there's ash everywhere... and I just washed my hair.
*Sigh*... How am I supposed to explain this...?
No need to explain anything.
We saw all that.
A fair number of the Infected just now are part of the Pinus Sylvestris.
They banded the Infected together fairly quickly. Since when has the Grand Knight Territory had this many Infected?
If we have to play the blame game, I guess that's on both the sloppy engineering contractors and the Catastrophes.
That's why we need Area 0 to do its job... Oh.
Little pegasus? I have a favor to ask you.
Ask away... I just screwed up in front of my bosses. I'm not about to object.
Will you investigate this company for us? They have dealings with the Radiant Knight, and they are in Area 0 right now.
We'll take over this whole thing about the Infected... And this company has to do with the Radiant Knight anyway. I'd say it falls under your purview.
Rhodes Island.
I just need to investigate?
They were even gung-ho enough to get in touch with the General Chamber. Man, it looks like this foreign company is extra greedy.
If they prove beneficial to us, then they can do whatever they want. I'll even be okay if they end up solving the Radiant Knight problem with money.
But this whole affair is up to the Board of Directors. Somebody there will come looking for you soon. No need to worry about what we think.
What will you two do, then?
Since when have you had the right to question the Lazurites' business?
I... I wouldn't dare.
Now do your job.
It's the Candle Knight! Please sign this for me!
Candle Knight! Look this way, please! Ah—!
You look like you're in a good mood.
Did you know, McKee?
I heard that the Radiant Knight's legend as the youngest ever champion ended with her exile.
She was infected and then exiled, but it wasn't until I met her six years later that I realized this: They are all tall tales.
Just now...
...she was as radiant as the sun at high noon.
Phew... That was a pleasant meeting, Dr. Doctor.
I'm sure you've gained the favor of the top brass. Those were all key members of the General Chamber of Commerce.

opts: Thank you very much. This is all thanks to you.
I learned quite a few things.

lines.x: But my guess is... You are seeking more than just a mutual relationship on paper, right?
I'm curious... are there many researchers familiar with the battlefields of commerce in other countries?

opts: Perhaps...
Society is changing. It's a necessity.
I might just be one-of-a-kind.

lines.x: Haha...
Your hotel isn't much further.
Oh, this is pretty close to where I got my first job. Back then, I was just your average trader—
Urghh—
—! Step back, Doctor!
An Infected knight, injured from unknown causes, turns to your face with a stupefied look.
You can tell that there is not a hint of anger, fear, or sorrow in this man.
But you have not had the chance to truly analyze his complex emotions. Right now, he is like a burdenbeast being flogged.
The man strenuously climbs to his feet and runs away.
Mr. Spokesman, this is...?
Who was that just now?
An illegal Infected. If you would like, we can send you the documents later.
I apologize. Even though you are a spokesman, I can't divulge more when there is an outsider in your presence.
Please take your friend and evacuate immediately. There's been a confrontation in the neighborhood.
My apologies. I hope that didn't dampen your spirits.
I didn't expect we would run into trouble like that after the banquet...

opts: Can you help the Infected?

lines.x: I'm afraid I don't have that power...

opts: For our friendship.
For your friendship with Rhodes Island.
For the betterment of Kazimierz.

lines.x: Malkiewicz looks silently in the direction the Infected knight ran away.
There is a small alleyway, from which he can hear music playing and see waves of heat wafting out of a midnight diner.
The knight's blood trail extends into the distance.
Like gemstones sparkling with humanity.
Doctor?
You know, you've been frowning ever since you came back from the restaurant.
If you're feeling worn out, then I'd be more than glad to––

opts: What'd you think of the meal?
What do you think of Malkiewicz?

lines.x: It went well. You're getting pretty slick around these kinds of events, aren't you?
I can tell. Even though the directors didn't show up in person, those corporate execs are all fond of you...
I said it already, but a certain spokesman took the fall and resigned over the Radiant Knight incident.
Malkiewicz was given that position in turn, but before that, he was a complete nobody, a minor player at best.
Though he still has a bit of that unvarnished human touch... I don't think it'll last very long in the environment he's in.

opts: Do you think Rhodes Island's plan is feasible?

lines.x: Let's set that aside for now... So, do you really trust me?
The infiltration of the General Chamber isn't some kind of conspiracy. In the face of money and a changing society, even if the K.G.C.C. did nothing, the knights would still fall to capital.

The courtesy that the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi has extended to Rhodes Island is almost certainly linked to the Radiant Knight. That being the case, should a person as particular as you... inadvertently fail to return that courtesy, you'll be the one to suffer.

opts: Thanks for the warning.
That much I already know.
So, what are you thinking?
What should I do then?

lines.x: Brassrust Knight... is going to fight again?

Yes... Based on video evidence and the testimonies from other knights, the Knights Association proved to the National Council that what happened to that Infected was an exacerbation of his disease due to excessive casting.
Because the cause of death was 'Oripathy,' Brassrust Knight was not directly responsible, so...
Even if that was the case... then we should at least...
Leave me.
Very well.
Put me through to the associate justice of the National Council... on a private line.

lines.st: The busy tone rings out twice from the phone.
Malkiewicz suddenly flashes back to his childhood, to the copper-colored telephone that sat on the desk in his father's study.

lines.x: Spokesman, have you come up with a solution?
As long as Mr. Czarny no longer has the evidence, it's fine, right?
Of course... But as a friendly reminder, if this goes public late, we will both end up taking the blame.
Therefore, the safest option... is to get rid of him.
You have my assurances.
My assurances... that I can keep Mr. Czarny muzzled.
There's no need to concern yourself with my methods.
Very well then... As long as you can demonstrate your sincerity.
Tomorrow, I will begin the retrial of Olmer Ingra's case. If you can do that within seven days...
Olmer Ingra will thence disappear from the Major.
...I will.
Right. At the very least, you must prove to me that Mr. Czarny truly 'would never betray us.'

lines.st: Malkiewicz remembers. He remembers that bronze-colored telephone.
Telephones were not as popular back then, and cables still coursed above bars, hostels, and roaring construction sites.
When it rang, it would bring joys and sorrows alike.
That was life in Kazimierz... Life in modernity.

lines.x: There's one thing I hope you understand...
You're provoking a spokesman.
You know as well as I do that I'm only a stand-in. I enjoy no support among the Board of Directors.
But that does not mean that I will abide your butchery.
The power to command the Armorless Union... was once in the hands of Mr. Czarny. And now...
Please don't mistake my intentions. I'm not trying to challenge your authority.

lines.st: Authority.

lines.x: I'm simply reminding you.. to make sure our future cooperation goes smoothly.
After all you've said, it wouldn't behoove me to run amok. That would be too discourteous after all.
All I need is your assurance. Olmer Ingra is just another corrupt noble, and it hardly makes sense for someone like that to get in the way of our relationship, yes?
Indeed.
Then, there's nothing more for us to talk about.
Wait! Hold on, Mr. Malkiewicz, please––
You smiled.
Woah! Mr. McKee, when did you...
I've never seen you smile like that before, my man.
Did you need something?
Naturally, the Armorless Union has already received their orders, and we still have a role to play in these matters. However...
Actually, never mind. Carry on with your work.
Alright, Mr. McKee.
Authority is the sweetest taste. Wouldn't you agree, Mr. Malkiewicz?
Nothing beats strolling through these streets in the evening.
As long as there's no work to be done, that is.
Reporting. Monique has led her team to the designated location.
The target of this operation has been confirmed, and there are now about a hundred Infected hidden throughout the area.
Ah. Score whatever kills you can. As I said before, the pay is determined by headcount.
Same old rules apply, no friendly fire.
Yessir.
Hey, hey! We've got Armorless!
Wh-What? You're kidding, right? This is the middle of Kawalerielki! They wouldn't dare start a bloodbath?!
Not enough.
What? Wasn't Wild Mane supposed to distract the Armorless like you wanted?
But, it's not enough.
Did we overlook something...?
Prepare for the worst, Fartooth.
Don't underestimate the Armorless.
Run, run now!
Calm down. What did you see?
B-B-Blue... A blue bow.
Huh?! You're kidding!
H-Hurry, tell the others, spread out and run! Hide in the garbage dumps if you have to!
Tell the Infected civilans to not resist. They can't resist, no matter what––
N-Now, if there's anyone who can contact Pinus Sylvestris... Please hurry...
Haha... Ahahaha...
Reporting. We found three Infected.
Hahaha... Haha...
Right, did I tell you about the offer I got from the Board of Directors?
300 gold for an illegal Infected. Twice that for an Infected knight.
Stop right there!
Dammit! They found me!
Each Infected is worth a ticket to the Major. An Infected knight is worth half a good car.
So.
How should we deal with a special individual who isn't in that price schedule?
Huh?
How many forms of code signing have you been trained in?
Um, I think I remember how to use three.
Hmm... Forget it. No need for any of that. If you see me... Mmm... Yes, snap my fingers.
If you see me snap my fingers, act immediately. No hesitation.
Yessir.
Alright. I'll head up to say hello.

lines.st: Lazurite Roy walks forward, and the street lights flicker several times.
The blue-brick road stretches forward, not a single person on the street.
There is a bench on the side of the desolate road.
And upon the bench sits a desolate person.

lines.x: Good evening. And a wonderful one at that, yes?
Did you happen to see the news? Tonight, it's nothing but the Radiant Knight.
This place sure is empty. Haven't seen anyone else around. I hear it hasn't been too peaceful around these parts either.
Ahem.
What are you doing here all by yourself?
Roy... I just finished work. Just resting here for a spell.
My, did you only get off work just now, this late? What a workaholic.
But... What kind of work would you be doing around here?
The only folks around these parts are the Infected, illegal immigrants, and black market traffickers, right?
Just waiting for someone.
What, are you even going to ask who I'm waiting for?
No, no, no, why would I?
Waiting for someone, huh? Let me guess... you had a date with a beautiful lady? Haha, just kidding...
But, we both have business here.
I'm afraid I have to ask you and your friend to step aside.
You mind your business, I mind my business.
Do you have any idea what you're saying, Młynar?
Was I speaking Gaulish?
Oh, come on. If you're here, my underlings might slip up because they're so nervous.
Sigh...
Oh, Młynar, did you know... if I wanted to kill you right now... how little effort it'd take?
Roy slowly lifts his hand.
In the dark, the Armorless Union assassins wait, poised to strike.
Though perhaps nothing than a faulty premonition, the masters of hiding in the shadows always feel most prone to being seen.
Młynar Nearl.
They begin to pray, praying that Roy would not give any signals––
A white-horned Sarkaz with a sword.
*Sigh*...
It really was... a beautiful lady, eh.
Am I late?
No.
What are you looking at me for?
Do your job, Roy.
Hm.
Roy's eyes meet with those of the Sarkaz for a moment.
There is only one thing on his mind.
How to strike up a conversation later, without embarrassing himself too much.
Haha, I suddenly had a change of heart. Turns out it'll rain later tonight, and I happened to forget my umbrella.
But...
Should I take this as evidence that the Nearl family intends to shelter the Infected?
Take it however you'd like.
Margaret won again.
You ought to stop her.
Forgive me.
But if that is what she wills, then I will support her... I will be her pillar.
You? You're a Sarkaz...
As for the Infected situation, I'll assume you already contacted Rhodes Island.
Your assistance is appreciated... The Armorless Union is a powerful enemy.
If he insisted on slaughtering every Infected hiding in this area, I fear we would be powerless to stop him.
I just wanted to sit here.
Nothing more.
If you want to know what Margaret has been through the last few years, I'm willing to tell you what I know.
Of course, if you'd prefer to ask her yourself...
...I don't care.
I already understand, just from the look in her eyes.
Her mother and father, and even me in the past, all embraced that era of resplendence and glory.
You... are her true family.
You already know everything you need to know. From now on, I won't be lifting even a finger.
You can tell Margaret, and that... 'Rhodes Island.' Tell them beneath the glory-gilded exterior of the Grand Knight Territory... the extent of the rot on the inside.
But a more practical suggestion I have is to leave the Grand Knight Territory as soon as possible. Leave Kazimierz.
Try your best to leave this entire era behind. That's probably one of the few right choices anyone can make.
What a complicated person you are.
You... stay away from me!
Damnit, are you with the Armorless? You––
No, you're not... You're... a Sarkaz?! Who are you?
I come in peace. The Armorless Union has already left.
Wh-What are you talking about...?
Just now, just now, that Sarkaz blocked the Armorless...
Just who are you? How do you have the guts to stand against them?
I'm a physician for the Infected.
A Sarkaz... who claims to treat the Infected? What...
It's so crazy I can almost believe it.
Fine then... So what do you want?
Kazimierz has the means to provide medical treatment to the Infected... That being the case, why do you still hide here, within the cracks of the city?
You're not from Kazimierz?
No, this is something that even most people from Kazimierz wouldn't know... Wait, you said you're a physician for the Infected?
You... You're from Area 0?!
I'm from a pharmaceutical company. This Area 0 that you mentioned... is that the place that shelters the Infected?
The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi Kazimierz has funded the establishment of a clinic hub specifically for the examination and treatment of Infected knights...
Shining is in no hurry to finish her sentence.
In the eyes of the Infected in front of her, she sees doubt, mistrust towards her, fear, and the slightest glimmer of hope.
You really aren't from Kazimierz... But that makes sense, they also hate the Sarkaz here. Ugh, sorry, but that's just how things are.
Do you treat the Infected over there?
Yes.
Don't you think it's strange?
Such a big mobile platform has practically become a cage for the Infected knights. Did you know the Infected knights are examined and treated there, but they also 'live' there?
As if existing like that can be called 'living' at all.
Alright, it seems you outsiders don't even know this much.
But if all the Infected were to live there...
Then there's not enough space, right?
Those Infected who can still make money will step back into the arena, or carry on... finding whatever odd jobs they can.
For those who can't become knights, they'll be sent to off to do some iffy work... Mining, transporting, all in dangerous environments.
And if you get hurt, and you can't even do that kind of manual labor...
Take a guess, outsider. Where do you think those Infected from Area 0 end up?
You may think it's a hospital, and the knights may think it's a prison. But let me tell you, it is neither.
The Infected are shipped there, washed of their dignity, stripped of their basic worth, and finally sent to the cutting boards to be culled.
What you're looking at, Sarkaz, is a glorified slaughterhouse.
Guah... Uhhh—
Aaah... ah...
Report. The Withered Knight is displaying rejection symptoms after the injection.
No... We didn't observe similar reactions in the other knights. The pharmacists think it could be an allergic reaction that occurs in Sarkaz.
What? T-That's a waste...

It's almost as concentrated as the restoration gels they sell in stores as it is... This stuff is for fixing armor, and they're human. If we keep injecting this into their bodies, we'll end up causing irreparable damage to their brains...
Okay, I understand... They're Sarkaz... not human...
*Sigh*... You heard them. Increase the concentration by another 0.3%... We'll find out the Sarkaz limit.
What... are you?
Gkhaa... Gkhaa...
You... Nightzmora... must die...
The young Nightzmora lets out a long sigh. He looks up to find the familiar night sky has been dispelled by the bright lights of the city.
Is this, too, a symbol of a civilization's advancement? Is Kazimierz conquering the night skies with its bright neon lights?
He finds himself struck with a sense of melancholy.
Hey!
Maria!
Thank goodness... You're okay!
U-Uh, Zofia? P-Please. Don't cry...
I'm glad you're alright, Maria.
How were we supposed to face Old Nearl if something happened to you?
Not that we can face him the way things are now.
Ahaha... You're not that old. What are you talking about? *cough*.
Uhh... Zofia? Could you let go... Please?
Alright.
Sorry to interrupt this exemplary show of sisterly love... but it seems Margaret is missing?
Uh... When did you...?
I've been here since the hugging.
Ugh... Who are you again?
Oh, this guy helped me out just now—
It's you! Y-You're...
A case of mistaken identity, Old Knight.
So? Where's Margaret?
After all that ruckus, is the insensitive Radiant Knight still going to carry on with the Major, foolhardy as she is?
I... I don't know if we should tell her...
Heh.
The Armorless Union isn't going to give up so easily. They'll attack again sooner or later. You're all in danger!
But... telling Margaret is exactly what they want us to do...
This is the Grand Knight Territory. There is nowhere to run. They'll always find you.
These are different times, not like before. The General Chamber of Commerce is growing even as we speak.
This is for your own safety, Maria. We have to tell Margaret.
Is that really going to solve anything?
Are they really going to give up just because she pulls out of the Major?

lines.st: Do the knights clinging to life in this city have no way out left?

lines.x: (W-What am I thinking...?)
It's not a bad thing to be so lost in thought. So long as you're not like a certain someone, anyway.
Okay, if Margaret isn't here, I still have something else to attend to. Cheerio.
Hold on, what are you—
Zofia.
Let him go. Don't ask any more. It's okay.
Alright then...
I owe you for saving Maria though. If there's anything we can do to help... let us know.
Hm... Right. Your attack glanced to the side, Maria. Your balance was off, and you were a little too slow too.
Seems like this is an emotional problem, though, and I can't pretend I know how girls work.
Is it an emotional problem?
Knights don't fight on the battlefield.
You won't be getting very far on borrowed ideals alone.
Confessarius. Seriously? Just the name gives me chills.
If you're going to question that, try Darksteel.
I'll pass... He's a Sarkaz too, isn't he? Something like a legendary mercenary?
A Sarkaz from Kazdel, the Radiant Knight, and this Rhodes Island... Interesting.
Interesting, and also in the way.
We have to deal with both the Board and the Knights Association, we have to take care of the Infected so discreetly, and we even have to be careful and cautious about it. When will all this be over?
Soon...
Right... soon.
But Darksteel doesn't step up. Doesn't that mean we'll have to take care of all this dirty work ourselves?
Wait.
Let's show some mercy. Why not?
They lost. I have the right to do whatever I want with them.
They are my kin, and I can't bear to see my kin end up like this in Kazimierz. Let me take care of them.
Your kin... heh.
These filthy creatures don't deserve to be my war prize.
I'll break one arm each for their punishment. Take them away, the eyesores.
Ah, that's it? I thought we'd have to fight to come to an agreement.
Eh, hey now. Keep your blade where it is. Forget I said anything.
Reigning victorious over the Candle Knight, the Radiant Knight is making huge waves in both in the media and on the intercity net.
Furthermore, citizens are trembling in fear with Infected terrorism on the rise across the Grand Knight Territory.
Area 0's officer-in-charge and a General Chamber of Commerce spokesman have announced they will hold a press conference in the upcoming days.
Anyway... That's about the size of it, Doctor, Miss Amiya.
I'm afraid... things aren't very calm in Area 0 these days, and the Infected are taking the fall for the uprisings that happened the other day.
These are extraordinary circumstances... but we don't want to put a stop to the sheltering and medical treatment just because of this.
Please avoid wandering around town the next couple days. I'm very sorry.
Doctor... the news...
It's not 100% false, unfortunately.
The Infected have actually been making a lot of noise, especially after the Radiant Knight defeated the Candle Knight.
We had eleven cases of violence just yesterday.

opts: Gossip is a fearful thing, and the media knows that best.
That can't be true.

lines.x: Apparently some of the Board members were assaulted?
Whoa... There are even protests going on.
I just hope it won't affect the knights... That lady yesterday said she'd buy toys for her siblings with her winnings.
Then... does that mean Nearl is...
Deport the Infected! The Infected don't belong in the Grand Knight Territory!
The Infected quarantine zone is an Oripathy petri dish!
What's going on here? I just got out of my meeting. How did things get so out of hand?
Hm? Look at that city block over there. Isn't that the Infected shelter and treatment center?
The protest's let up a little already. It was way worse this afternoon. They had a huge banner, too.
Do you need something?
The Radiant Knight... Do you know why those Infected dare do this out in the open?
They're committing terrorist attacks all over the city, dragging innocent citizens into the fray. Do you know where they got the courage to do this?
It was you! It was you and the Blood Knight. It was you infected knights who gave them the right the resist!
I... I know you're no villain, champion. I even used to be a fan of yours.
But by staying in the Major, you are only giving the Infected false hope.
False hope that... they can change the status quo.
You'd do well to keep your nose clean, Radiant Knight... Don't end up regretting your actions.
What's the matter, Nearl?
You don't look like a knight who just won a match.
No...
Nearl.
If there's something on your mind, tell us.
Right... I'm just thinking about the Infected.
About that... we have another lead. Amiya knows already. There's no doubt that someone is behind all this.
But you don't need to worry about that right now.
Trust me, and trust Rhodes Island.
Alright.
Keep your light shining bright, and worry about nothing else, Nearl.
At the same time... Please don't push yourself either. You're strong, but your exhaustion is building up.
Try to take it easier once in a while.
Kazimierz... is my home.
I'm very happy to see Maria all grown up and aiming to become a knight, and that Zofia and Old Marcin are both doing well...
But, right this moment, I feel more relieved that you two are here by my side.
Are we your family too, Nearl?
It's hard to say, Liz.
It'll be some time before winter comes, but it still gets pretty chilly at night here in Kazimierz. Liz, go back to Amiya.
We've all been worried about you ever since you 'left' Rhodes Island.
I know, but that's exactly why I can't drag them into my own mess.
But you're fine dragging us into it?
You're free to leave now, if you want.
Well, well, look at you. Our Radiant Knight acting all snarky.
Music...? Is there a bar nearby?
It used to be a row of regular old offices here. If I remember correctly, there was a publisher.
The Grand Knight Territory wasn't this big...
But, for the past couple decades, every time there was a Major, the Grand Knight Territory would pick out three cities to dock with after a round of competitive bidding.
The Kawalerielki Alliance.
The city's changing... and even though this is my home, it feels a little... foreign.
You haven't been back for very long yet, Nearl.
And you dove straight into the Kazimierz Major right away... you chose to throw yourself into your ideals.
You haven't started living a new, proper life here just yet.
I will, sooner or later. But now's not the time.
You must be tired... I can feel it.
Should we take a walk? The night breeze might sweep away some of your exhaustion.
Sure. Take my hand.
Of course.
How tough was the Candle Knight?
I was surprised to find a knight like her in Kazimierz these days.
She's strong, but her hesitation proved to be her weakness. It's too bad we couldn't face each other when she's at her best.
Did you enjoy it?
I used to despise knight sports. No... even now, I feel nothing but sorrow for the shape Kazimierz is in.
But... haven't we been through a lot together?
The three friends walk slowly down the street under the Kazimierz night sky.
Far away from the loud markets and hustle and bustle, only traces of the city's warmth can be felt in this alley.
The warmth comes from liquor and song, as well as the aroma of baked goods that yet lingers as pedestrians come and go.
This doesn't change what we need to do.
And that's even if, after everything has come to pass, my journey is destined to be ridiculed.
—Like wings taking flight in the night breeze.
—Like a traveler with a heart full of dreams.
—The knight sets out on her journey.
—In search of true love.
What a beautiful voice...
Maybe it's a wandering singer.
Is there a place so romantic in Kazimierz too?
It's proof that there are people who live here.
—A fate as fragile as a cornflower.
—To face a test fierce as windstorms.
Hmm... Hmhmm...
Liz, would you care to dance?
Huh? Me? What...?
It's fine. Take my hand.
Alright...
Oh... S-Slow down, Nearl...
Alright.
The blue bird takes flight in the gentle night sky.
It dances amongst the glowing neon towers and navigates through a gently flowing river of time.
Taking the Sarkaz's hand, the knight waltzes back and forth under the dim lamplights.
The sight is reminiscent of the noblest statues found in Laterano.
You're beautiful, Liz.
R-Really?
Perhaps you will actually be able to dance one day.
When that day comes... will you be there by our side?
Of course.
The knight stops and kneels down before the lady of the lamp.
I promise, Liz.
Mhm...
We'll be waiting, Nearl.
At the end of your journey, before your light burns out, we'll be there with you.
When the time comes, if—
Nearl.
Good evening, Radiant Knight... and the lovely Sarkaz ladies.
I'm absolutely delighted to see you again so soon.
I was wondering why you would have that immobile Sarkaz along with you.
Now I know.
Um... Monique? I'm not sure I get it...
Then shut your mouth and stop embarrassing yourself.
The Armorless Union, is it? What do you want?
First things first, please allow me to apologize to the Radiant Knight.
That is, for the Armorless Union's kidnapping of your sister, Maria—
—Hey, hold your beasties. It wasn't my doing.
You... you wretched henchmen!
Maria should already have made it home safe. Relax. We have no more tricks to pull on you, and suppose we were going to do anything else, we would've gone for... less perfect results.
We at the Armorless Union are just doing our jobs, even if it's not exactly what we want to do. We've got eyes on us from above. You know what I mean?
You're attacking the Infected.
That's how the bosses arranged things. Don't lay the blame on us.
The cause doesn't clear you of your crimes.
I guess you're right...
But isn't that why we're paying you a visit today?
We have nothing to say to each other. If it is a fight you want, it is a fight you'll get.
Hey, now, let's cool our tempers, shall we? I'll be frank. We want to shake off the General Chamber of Commerce.
What...?
We're here today to make this clear. We're on different sides. I mean, we still belong to the Chamber...
But would you be willing to sign a peace treaty with us?
You, the Radiant Knight, the Nearl family and its subordinates, as well as this 'Rhodes Island.' I am hoping you will all refrain from getting in the way of our plans.
In return, the Armorless Union won't interfere with your quest for glory or lay a finger on anyone that's got anything to do with you.
What about the Infected...?
Sorry, but we can't possibly do everything either.
The Infected are a threat to us. Not to worry, though. We have a use for those Infected knights too.
To take the fall for you?
That's not what I want, Armorless Union.
—What is the Armorless Union?
Long ago, the local farmers and affinities pulled together and hired a group of veteran assassins to stand up to an indulgent knight noble.
But they all failed—Every last one of them.
The knight hanged the traitors atop his castle, to make an example of them. The villagers in the territory could only keep their mouths shut and give up their fight.

Some time after that, however, there was an archer who took matters into his own hands. Back then, everyone figured that bows and arrows were no match for a Kuranta knight's speed, so they weren't really holding their breaths.
Yet when the cruel knight's family awoke the next morning, they found the knight impaled through the ground by a long spear, mid-charge, his head still looking up.
When the crowd got closer, they realized it wasn't a spear at all. It was an arrow. An arrow forged by Darksteel.
'Darksteel,' the very first member of the Armorless Union.
I'm very well aware that we're hired killers, but nonetheless...
Legends like these aren't always without merit.
—Nearl! Above you!
The Radiant Knight lifts her head.
The streets are only barely lit, and the stars on the night sky are twinkling brilliantly.
Among them, a lone shooting star twinkles a few times before straying from the rest of the meteor shower's path.
It falls straight to the ground.
—An arrow?!
Break it!
Roger—!
Aaah—?!
Liz, watch out!
(Nearl and I both struck it at the same time... and... it didn't break?)
Are you two okay?
I-I'm fine... W-What was that just now?
Margaret looks to her side in silence.
There is a pitch black arrow long as a spear burrowed into the concrete.
Impressive... I've never seen anyone react to Darksteel's shots in time, much less dodge them.
Your swordsmanship is as impressive as the rumors say, Radiant Knight. That was an eye-popping maneuver from the Confessarius, too... Luckily, the arrow's not life deadly—
—Is that the smell of souls, Sarkaz?
I never expected to see such fine swordsmanship and Arts... from a doctor like you.
Save it... So much for your peace treaty.
I'm sure you know already, but that shot wasn't meant to take your lives. The Sarkaz sitting over there would be thoroughly dead otherwise.
So, now that we've broken the ice, please do give our proposal some thought.
I hope you understand that one shot just now, but the Armorless Union isn't begging on bended knee—
There are three 'Darksteels.'
How many of their arrows can you dodge?
Stop right there!
The Armorless Union members' figures disappear into the gap between the neon signs and the moonlight.
All that remains is the reverberating jazz music.
'Darksteel...' a metal with an obsidian-like luster. Very heavy.
It came falling down from the dark night sky... just as the name implies.
From the... night sky?
The night sky...
The Radiant Knight looks up again.
There are twinkling stars everywhere, seemingly ready to strike down and ambush them at any moment.
This arrow... They must have shot it from far away. It was the high slope of the trajectory that made it seem like it fell from the sky.
That makes no sense.
The Armorless Union... and 'Darksteel.' If everything's just like the smooth-tongued guy said, they were the origins of the term 'Armorless Union.'
These assassins might have their eyes on our heads right this moment.
You're right. We need to be more alert. This is Kazimierz, after all.
It's the fortress of their crimes.
Malkiewicz...
What's the matter, Mr. McKee?
It seems like you've lightened up a lot the past couple days. Did something happen?
No... I just figured out what it is I should be doing.
Oh yes?
I heard you have a close relationship with Rhodes Island Pharmaceuticals, and that you even arranged for their leader to meet with a few other corporations the other day?
That's true... Is there a problem?
I'm sure you know, but public opinion on the Infected has been getting out of hand. We'll have to do something sooner or later.
Rhodes Island is one of the few foreign companies that operate in Area 0, but they have the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi's support nonetheless.
We must treat this Rhodes Island seriously. Actually, a few of the executives have already made clear that they want us to investigate... and make sure they're dealt with.
Dealt with... huh?
There are certain secrets about Area 0 that we can't let an outsider uncover.
We're deploying the Armorless Union here, and the responsible party will again be given the Platinum's command.
The Armorless Union?!
However... I also proposed to the Board that they put you in charge of this.
Mr. McKee! Thank you!
Don't mention it.
I'm hoping your relationship with Rhodes Island will keep things simple. When you do business in Kazimierz, you have to play by Kazimierz's rules.
I understand.
Another thing... Please make sure word doesn't get out on this whole affair, lest things turn dangerous. Even though Platinum and most of the Armorless Union are still under control.
There remains a problem with the Armorless Union's internal affairs. A huge problem.
...Doctor.
Spacing out again? Are you bored?
Lately, a lot of people here in the Grand Knight Territory are boycotting the Infected knights.
If it weren't for that... Hehe... I would've loved to take a stroll around town with you.

opts: Amiya is under a lot more pressure.

lines.x: Well... but of course.
I find myself wondering... what is your company trying to do here in Kazimierz?

opts: Closure's always complaining that we're in the red. We had to think of something.
Nearl also told us there are a lot of people here in Kazimierz who need help.
I asked her what her plan was.
I decided to give her my full support, and so did Amiya.
That's why we're here.

lines.x: That's a pretty unsurprising answer.
Though, in the past, I would've thought it an excuse... I guess the Radiant Knight has pretty high standing at Rhodes Island?

opts: I can't say I've given anyone's 'standing' that much thought.
I respect Nearl, and that's got nothing to do with standing.
Margaret Nearl is one of our operators. I've never thought of her as the Radiant Knight.

lines.x: Kazimierz companies these days like to boast about having flat management structures, but it's actually a ploy to get the bottom-rung workers to let their guard down.
But if that's what you say, Doctor... then let's go with that.
Does this mean that if I come work part time at Rhodes Island right now, we'll be... on equal footing?
My, I'm so jealous.
You turned a blind eye to the Radiant Knight's title and status, treating her only as a person... and you still respect her nonetheless?
What about everyone else at Rhodes Island? Do they have your respect too? If so... I think I'm a little curious about this place now.
There are a lot of knights who respect the Radiant Knight, though it's always for the same two reasons.
It's either because she is a 'Nearl' or that she's 'the Radiant Knight.'
As a knight of Kazimierz, it's interesting to be given a different answer.
...Doctor.
I was sold as a slave to Kazimierz. Ever since, my master taught me to be ready to give my life for others.
Even now, I'm ready to die for you, you know? Hehe.

opts: Because you were tasked to be my bodyguard?
Because that's an order from the Adeptus?

lines.x: That's how campaign knights are trained.
That's why the Radiant Knight's actions feel somewhat distant to me.
Do you think it's a noble thing to give your life for somebody else's ideals?

opts: Of course.
Yes.

lines.x: What if that person's ideal is to give their own life for somebody else? What would that get us in the end?

opts: A better world.
As long as it's something better, I'd call that a win.

lines.x: That's a naive way of thinking. It's nothing like how sharp you were when dealing with the K.G.C.C.

opts: Say, Gravel?

lines.x: What is it?

opts: If you don't think I'm worthy of your sacrifice, then don't make it.

lines.x: How could you say that? You're doubting my loyal—

opts: If you think I'm worthy of your sacrifice, then live on for us.
You're a brilliant knight, and you're a partner of Rhodes Island.
You're free.

lines.x: What's on your mind?
Nothing, it's just... it's been a while since I last saw Darksteel make a move.
When was it?
You weren't a Lazurite yet... It was when we took care of the last Platinum.
That Platinum was Centaurea's master... or something like that, I guess?
It was raining that day. The time was 4:47 P.M., and I was just about to go on a business trip to another city.
I was the only Lazurite at the time, and I got a call all of a sudden. They told me that 'Platinum' betrayed us, and I had to take him out immediately.
So...? Did you kill your coworker with your own two hands?
Nope. If I'd done that, I'm not sure I'd ever get a decent night's sleep again.
Actually, by the time we made it there... Haha... It was an unfinished building.
The whole third floor was wrecked, and the traitor... was nailed to the wall high above the ground with one of Darksteel's heavy arrows pierced straight through his chest.

Now, I may be an amateur when it comes to on-site investigation, but I could tell that 'Platinum' tried to run away, only to get shot through the chest by the arrow mid-air, and pinned to the wall like that.
I was standing right under the wall, and the poor guy who fell in love with his target and tried to betray us was nailed right there above me.
I can still see it in my mind. I'd never seen a wall as white as that, and he looked like he really suffered.
Only an idiot would be fooled by somebody spouting freedom and platitudes. They don't understand what it actually means to survive.
It's me.
Hm... Are they finally making their move?
Pinus Sylvestris, huh...?
Right, they're very cautious... but... they couldn't hold back any longer.
I mean, we've had those Infected boycott protests the last two days. It's ironic. I'm not even sure who's the victim here anymore.
*Sigh*, another phone call? Does it ever stop?
Hello—
Why didn't you position yourself at the specified coordinates?
The Confessarius and the Radiant Knight wouldn't have blocked that arrow.
You had them face north, and they saw my arrow in time.
I could've killed the Sarkaz sitting on the chair at least.
Ahaha, I'm not sure what you're talking about—I can't just laugh this one off, eh?
That was my quick thinking, Boss. The Radiant Knight's a stubborn one. If you kill one of her friends, you'll just end up provoking her.
If she'd lose her cool, I don't see why we shouldn't have.
That would've been too dangerous, Boss. Sorry for only cluing you in after the fact, but I think the threat level we're at is just right. Let's not overdo it.
Trust me. The Radiant Knight won't get in your way... By the way, may I ask how things are going right now?
The Board will figure this out soon... but no matter. When the city is paralyzed by the Infected...
I will end the last of the insiders, personally.
And then no one alive will know the identities of the Armorless Union's highest ranking members.
In position.
Loud and clear.
All ready.
Yeah! We can do this!
Sona... The backup power's kicking in way too fast. They might have improved their emergency measures.
Please... Please be careful.
Hah, so you're saying I should put on an even bigger show?
That's right, Wild Mane! Bash those Armorless Union bastards' brains in!
Of course! I'm ready, anytime!
Iwona will ambush the Armorless Union's patrol head on.
After half an hour, Ashley will ambush the supply sector. If we don't run into any surprises, the Adeptus's guards will be 'sent away' at that point.
I'll infiltrate the K.G.C.C. and take what we need. Easy peasy.
Okay... Let's get this started.
I can help you sneak into the building...
But that's all I can do for you. You'll also need somebody to keep your escape route clear.
That's more than enough. Thanks, Toland.
Don't forget what I said. What you folks are doing means much more than you can ever imagine.
Now, let everyone see this city cry its eyes out.
The Infected are making their move.
They'll really charge through the front door?
They'd better. The bigger their ruckus, the less we leave the Board to use against us.
How are things at the power plant?
Are you doubting me?
I wouldn't dare.
Can I kill Wild Mane?
Of course... now that things have come to this.
If we find ourselves some prey, that'll help make our owners less grumpy.
They're prey who got away to begin with... I'm just taking back what should've been mine.
Contact Team 3. Have them pack it up and come with me.
Wild Mane spotted! Wild—
Too slow, Armorless!
Urgh!
Pulling dirty tricks in the shadows... Don't tell me you people have forgotten how to take on a knight!
Filthy Infected!
This is for Jamie and all the innocent who died by your hands!
Eek—
—Iwona! Don't forget what Sona said. Avoid killing anyone if you can... At the very least, when you kill them, make sure you're prepared to handle it.
Hey, you. Armorless... How many people have you killed?
Heh...
Trying to act all righteous now, are we? Hypocritical brat. Back when I was a bounty hunter on the Golden Prairie, I never gave a crap about that!
Have you forgotten what life outside the cities is like? Those who stand at the top, the strongest survivors, are all cold-blooded bastards! That's the way it goes!
—Say whatever you want, but I've won.
When you've lost, when you're dead, nothing you say matters.
You—
He probably sent out a distress signal just now...
Hopefully that'll lure the Armorless Union here. That'll make Greynuty's job easier.
Fartooth, have you spotted any Armorless over there?
No.
What about you?
Greynuty's already in position. Just waiting on our signal.
That said, the Armorless can't be this slow to react... Did Wild Mane really manage to lure them her way?
She probably...
Armorless spotted. They're heading your way from an alleyway on 4th Street, Iwona!
Iwona...? Can you hear me? Iwona?
Hey?! Szewczyk? Iwona?!
The transmission... got cut? No way... In a city with this much transmission traffic...?
This silence is creepy... Iwona. I have a bad feeling about this.
Are those Armorless guys really such pushovers that we can take them down with an ambush?
I'm not sure.
Bigger problem, I'm not getting any of Justyna's transmissions, and she should be staring down at us from somewhere up high right—
(Shudder)—
—Move!
All of a sudden, Iwona is overwhelmed with an aura so suffocating that it threatens to make her puke.
She isn't sure this is really a biological response. It could be some kind of toxin or an Arts effect.
Then, a figure leaps down from the top of a tall tower.
The figure lands on the ground. The landing is silent as the veil lightly covering her.
You jumped from so high up, but you didn't even let out a peep, huh?
Must be nice to be a Feline.
Team 3...
What?! When did they—
(The difference between these guys and the ones we fought just now are night and day... She's wearing blue. Is she the Lazurite from the other day?)
You go on ahead...
But!
Go!
You'd better survive this!
Aargh?!
No way. I didn't even see her pull that bowstring—
The Armorless Union operative in blue maintains her one-handed throwing posture.
A hint of dissatisfaction comes through her cold expression. After all, this hunt started on an unlucky note.
She... threw it?
Aghh! My leg. My leg!
Grit your teeth and keep running! Don't stop!
Hey, stop looking around! I'm right here!
Are you Infected all idiots? Look how many bows we have aimed at you.
Wild Mane Knight, you should be dead several days by now. Good on you, though. You still have the chance to play hero today.
Now run. I don't want this hunt to end on a such a boring note. Let's kill some time together.
Hah, you seem to be mistaken.
Do I look like the kind of gal who hides in the shadows?
You look like the kind who's getting herself killed.
*Spit*. We Infected are sick of toiling our miserable lives away like that!
You're just a bunch of despicable bastards hiding behind the K.G.C.C., doing all kinds of shady business. Every one of us living out in the open is stronger than you!
Remember their faces!
Remember... These knights... These Kazimierzians... These cold-blooded murderers...
Don't let them get away with it!
Armorless scum...
Have you ever fought a gloompincer on the brink of death? Do you know how a dying, cornered animal fights?
You're too full of yourself.
I may die, but that doesn't mean I'll lose. And you. You'll pay your dues right here.
Then... My friends will...
Look at you. No fear of death on your face. You don't want to live, yet you still want to win. Ridiculous.
The blue Armorless Union operative takes out an arrow from behind.
Come.
You're going to use that arrow as a blade? What, you looking down on me?
Of course.
You little...
Urgh—!
So that's the speed you Kuranta are so proud of?
Don't underestimate me—
Hraaagh––!!
Don't be such a crybaby. I made sure not to kill you.
Now let me see how much blood you have to spill.
After I've painted the entire street with it, the Infected hiding in the dark will probably stop resisting?
Um, Lazurite, she is Infected though...
If you're worried about catching it, you should look for a new job.
Once death calms her down, it'll be much easier to deal with the resisting Infected.
It's been a while since I heard from Justyna...
Maybe she ran into some kind of trouble?
Either way, there's five minutes till our ambush.
Sona should be in position by now. We just have to trust each other.
It's almost time...
Everyone, be safe...

lines.st: Four minutes.

lines.x: You didn't even last a few minutes.
Go on... There should be seven or eight Infected hideouts around here, though I wouldn't be surprised if they're empty by now. Search all of them, one by one.
Get... back... here...
I'm not... done yet!
I'm leaving her to you. Not worth any more of my time.
Right.
All units, prepare to fire—
Didn't I just say... I'm not done?!
—She still has the strength to fight?!
I don't want to kill too many people here...
The old and the young can go, but I'll take the heads of ten Infected knights back to keep my bosses happy.
You... You murderer!
What did you do to Iwona?!
She should probably have passed out from the blood loss by now, though it sounds like she might still be putting up a fight.
You monster—!
You should know that even if you manage to get away now, it won't vindicate you.
I'll say it again. Ten Infected knights for the lives of everyone else.
How can we trust you?! You're just a bunch of murderers, a bunch of sick bastards!
I'll count Iwona... Just the lives of nine more knights.
You—
Are you sure you can win?
Never mind, then... It'll be faster if I do it myself.
Now what's the rush?
If you want to keep your bosses happy, surely you'd want quality over quantity?
Wouldn't you agree?
Toland Cash.
Not to toot my own horn, but I'm sure I'm worth a hefty sum on the wanted posters?
If you are willing to give me your own head, then I won't need any more.
Don't taunt her, Toland! She's—
'Lazurite' Monique of the Armorless Union, am I correct?
I said it before... I don't like to be interrupted when I'm working.
If you don't plan on giving yourself up, that makes three times you've interrupted me, Toland Cash.
The injured Infected knight isn't able to see everything clearly.
By the time his eardrums react, there's a sharp clashing sound.
Phew~ You really are quick on the draw with that bowstring.
You saw it...? No, your stance... That's the swordhand of a knight.
Since when do bounty hunters have tricks like that?
Oh, don't say that. If all we knew was shooting arrows, setting up traps, pulling dirty tricks, and waiting for our prey...
Would we really be any different from the scum in the Armorless Union?
You won't dodge many more of my shots.
You sure?
Whew, that was close.
Might want to watch your step, though.
Nngh—
An explosive trap...? You're still a bounty hunter in the end...
Needless to say.
—A charge... You've received knight training?
That's it. You're helping the Nearls... so you must have something tied up with them.
This really piques my interest, but work is work. We can't waste too much time on you.

lines.st: Two minutes.

lines.x: Man, what a pain... There's just no catching a taxi during the Major, is there?
Excuse me, which way is the Holiday Knight Souvenir Shop?
Hey, look at the time. Let's find a restaurant and get a bite to eat first.
Will you look at that?! Our Columbian newbie is about to overwhelm Wisteria Knight once again!
Impressive speed! Now just one more—Hold on, hold on! What's going on?!
Unbelievable! A devastating accident at the very last moment! Did our newbie trip?! Come on, this is the Major! Get up!

lines.st: One minute.

lines.x: Do you always leave work this late, Młynar?
To what do I owe the pleasure...?
If I had known in advance, Lady Russell, I would've received you more properly.
Don't worry yourself. I won't be taking up much of your time.
Tonight will be messy. There will be lots for us to take care of.
Młynar, is the sword-bearing wanderer back?
The wanderer traveled through corpse-laden battlefields and boundless wastelands, at last making it to the fortress lit ablaze by cannonfire. Is the wanderer not back yet?

lines.st: Thirty seconds.

lines.x: —I still haven't heard anything from them, but we can't wait any longer.
In five minutes, we'll begin our ambush and cut off the core power source. The core district, where the General Chamber of Commerce is, will lose all power.
Then, and only then, will Sona have a chance to break inside.
A big show then, huh? I like it.
Our Adeptus 'friends' should have withdrawn the security here, clearing a path for us.
We need to be quick. Get ready—
W-What's that?! Where was that explosion?
T-The power supply sector? Why?
Ashlock! Why did you start the operation early?!
No, we haven't started the charge yet—
—We've been had. Get out of there!
Now sleep, Kawalerielki, my beloved Grand Knight Territory.
Good night.

lines.st: Three.
Two.
One.
Everything has descended into darkness.
Did Greynuty succeed? Did Sona make it inside?
When all is said and done, will we really be able to live in peace here in Kazimierz?

lines.x: Don't kid yourself.
Think of what we had to go through.
We had to fight wild beasts in the underground arenas, sometimes even our fellow men. We still carry the pain and scars that will never go away.
The bosses wanted more blood and excitement, they wanted us to throw matches, and do their shady bidding outside the arena. We had no choice but to do what they wanted.
What do you think all that was for?
Look at all those places outside Kawalerielki. Where do you think the Infected can go?
Where we can go...?
We have Oripathy, an incurable disease. We will die sooner or later, and when we do, we'll lay waste to whatever unfortunate patch of ground we collapse onto.
We are ominous, we are uncontrollable, and we are different. We are here to be despised.
Do you think the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi will really allow the Infected to start a proper knightclub?
Is there really room for the Infected to live in Kazimierz... no... anywhere in this world?
And even if there were...
...what does that have to do with you?
She's not moving... Is she dead?
I sure hope so. I don't really want to fight the Infected. I mean, what if we catch it?
Go take a closer look. If she's dead, we'll need the damage control team up here.
*cough*...
Who... are you calling dead?
C'mon!
You dirty... Armorless! I'll... take all of you down!
You murderers... You all have Infected blood on your hands!
She still has the strength to move...?
She's bloody all over. She can barely even stand. Just give up already.
Come on... Come at me!
None of you... are getting away... *cough*!
She's got backbone, I'll give her that. Let's show her some mercy. Finish her off.
I mean, she's going to bleed out in a few minutes. Man, Monique really knows what she's doing with those techniques.

lines.st: If... If I were stronger... and faster...
Maybe I could've...
...escaped these unfortunate times.
Escape?
No! What am I thinking?
Move... Dammit... Why is my vision so blurry...?
Open your eyes...

lines.x: It's okay. It'll all be over soon—
All of a sudden, the Armorless stop what they are doing.
(Footsteps).
The footsteps are heavy, not like the light footsteps of these murderers, like whomever is making them is carrying a boulder that weighs a thousand pounds.
(Muffled conversation)
(What are they... talking about...?)
(Muffled conversation)
(...? Why are they...?)
No... Don't go... Stay right... where you are! I can still...
Let it go, knight.
You've done very well.
What's going on? Why is it dark all of a sudden?!
What's going on?
Huh?! Is the power out all over town?! Lord, how much money are we going to owe for this?!
Hey, you, the knight over there. Come help evacuate the audience!
Phew.
Whoa... It's pitch black outside...
Are you sure you guys aren't overdoing it, Greynuty?
Still...
The K.G.C.C.'s building has its own power source... Grr... We're almost out of time.
Huh? Someone's here!
Why did the power go out?
Look outside! The whole city's—
I-Is there a problem with the power plant again? Is this another Great Separation?!
Calm down.
Oh, Mr. Malkiewicz...
Fortunately, the spokesmen's meeting is today. The directors are all inside the Chamber building right now, safe and sound.
That's right. Follow instructions and help evacuate the crowds. Contact the Adeptus leadership. Our first and foremost priority is to make sure there isn't a lapse in public order.
Each district should have its own backup power source. Have the officers-in-charge make the necessary arrangements.
Yes, sir!
The Board of Directors meeting...
No, my priority right now is to find the machine room—
Lazurite, sir!
Well, things have gotten way out of hand...
That's why I said we had to be more decisive with the Infected. We can't give them an opening.
Lucky the directors summoned me here today. Who knows what could've happened to our bosses?
We'll review this series of events later.
No problem, but most of the Armorless Union... and Monique are rounding the Infected up and snuffing them out right now. It might take them some time to get back...
Very well... For the time being, please put your original mission on hold and ensure the directors' safety.
Okay, though I... no one would dare go after the Board, right?
Snuffing them out...?
No... They'll be okay... I...
I gotta hurry.
Please... Please be okay, Iwona.
Where are you?
Fartooth!
Plastic...?
Have you seen the others? They took out our comms—
It's not that simple. We're in the middle of the city. If they managed to cut off our comms, that means our channels were intercepted right from the start.
It wasn't Ashlock who took out the power either. The power plant went down before she could start her ambush. She's probably heading to where Flametail is right now.
What...?
The Armorless... They've been planning something, and they're passing the buck to the Infected—
Find the others! We're in real hot water right now!
Iwona!
Justyna finds the prideful Infected knight collapsed in a pool of blood.
Her armor is stained a gory red.
Iwona! Wake up!
S-She's still breathing...
Szewczyk, give me a hand—
Hold on... Has someone else... been here?
The Armorless assassins don't use axes... And even if they had an axeman, these marks...
The wall... is oozing blood?
It's him...?
Download complete.
So this is the plan for Area 0... and some sensitive information on the Armorless.
It feels weird being able to get my hands on this stuff so easily...
Now let me get out of this stinking place—Argh, my tail—
What are you standing there for?! Hurry up and contact the National Council!
And you! Isn't your department supposed to help evacuate the Fireblade Arena? Get moving!
We'll escort the spokeslady. Is she on the second floor?
(Things are a mess here... Perfect.)
(I just hope I can get away without much trouble.)
No way...
Did someone seriously sneak into the K.G.C.C. building during the chaos?
(Did he see me—?!)
Graah...!
Owww... Where am I... Was I leaning on the door? I didn't even notice...
It's dark. Uh, is this a meeting room...?
I gotta find a way out—
Sona has always felt as though she is fighting against something.
Against the Armorless Union, against the General Chamber of Commerce, against all the injustices the Infected face, and against even fate.
She has never actually doubted whether her actions are necessary.
But, right this moment, she feels a chill reaching from her head all the way to her feet, causing her to waver a little.
Indeed, this is the first time she has ever wavered.
It's as though she's just seen a tremendous ice sculpture in the middle of summer.
A call is coming in.
The ringing echoes throughout the empty room.
Hello?
Flametail Knight... Sona.
I appreciate your efforts. Excellent work.
Huh...?
Right now, you have two options.
'Lazurite' Roy is standing outside the conference room you are in, as we speak.
This is going to be a very simple transaction. You are holding two storage devices.
Hand over the one on the Armorless Union. You may keep the information on Area 0.
That will be more than enough for you to bargain the right to a legitimate life from the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi.
The way I see it, you have no other choice.
Get a grasp of the situation, Flametail Knight. I have nothing against the Infected, much less you.
Be it knights, merchants, or Infected, they are all narrow-minded.
Don't make an enemy of society, Infected.
The server has already been destroyed. The chip in your hand is the very last copy.
The choice is yours.
Perhaps you are thinking of smashing this telephone right now.
Then do it... if you believe that's the answer to your revolt against fate—
Whoa... He figured it out, huh? That's something.
So, the Lazurite is right outside the door now. What do I do...?
Which floor is this again...?
This... This is way too high, isn't it—?!
Sona!
Ashley, catch me!
—What are you up to this time? Why are you up ther—Whoa—!
Owww... Good thing I used Arts to soften the impact...
Wh-Why are you looking at me like that? Alright, no more wild moves... Now let go of my tail, will you?
I'm glad... you're okay.
Somebody took out the power plant before we could get to it. Whoever they are, they're using us.
No matter... We have what we need.
Ta-dah!
This...? A small little chip like this... can change our fates?
We'll just have to hope it can.
Whew... That's gotta be at least a dozen meter jump.
And look at you, still so full of pep, Infected. Haven't had enough yet?
Hey! That getup...
You know me?
The Armorless Union keeps its top levels secret from regular old knights... But...
'Lazurite' Roy. You're the only Lazurite assassin who hasn't been replaced in the past dozen-or-so years. There are all kinds of rumors centered around you. Of course we know you.
Really? Haha, glad to know I'm famous. It's not necessarily a good thing for an assassin to be famous, though. I mean, the Armorless Union's not like you knights. We can't cash in on our fame.
So, either you hand me the chip and walk out alive, or I kill you and take the chip myself.
Personally, I'm not very fond of fighting to the death, especially when there's no money in it.
Sona... are you okay?
Phew, I'm fine. This is gonna be a tough fight, though.
We just need to stay alive.
Heh, how long has it been since the last time we fought side by side for real? Since our fight against the gloompincer?
Come on, give me more credit than that. I think I can at least put up a better fight than a gloompincer.
Good thing I have these emergency lights. I never thought they'd actually come in handy.
What's going on? Another Great Separation?
The power's really out for the whole town! What kind of sick joke is this? How could this happen while the Major's on?
The power will be back soon... but... just who managed plunge a city that never sleeps into darkness?
Ridiculous... Just ridiculous.
Kawalerielki has entered a state of emergency, confirmed.
Correct signal code, confirmed. Correct squad identification code, confirmed.
Heed my orders.
Remove your disguises, and enter the city.
It'll still be some time until the power is completely restored.
The Rhodes Islanders have returned to their rooms, as advised. If you two would like to review...
Doctor, I want to go see them.

Everything happened so quickly, I'm sure there are many operators who still don't quite understand the situation. With all the anti-Infected protests going on, there's been a lot of pressure on everyone.

opts: I'll be counting on you again then, Amiya.
Are you sure you don't need my help?

lines.x: Oh, it's fine, Doctor.
I know how busy you've been with business negotiations and social events recently. To be honest, I feel bad about it.
You should get some rest while you still can.
Ahaha, you must already be worn out, Doctor.
After all, you've been dealing with those businesspeople nonstop since yesterday. Of course you'd lose your voice, right?
But it's okay, with Hibiscus here, I'm sure we won't have any trouble.
I just want to go around and see how everyone's doing. I'll be right back.
There's no need to worry, Amiya. I'll take real good care of the Doctor while you're gone.
Oh... Okay.
See you later then.
...DoctorDoctor.
There've been some developments in the General Chamber of Commerce that may be disadvantageous to Rhodes Island.
Coupled with the ongoing power outage, I'm afraid that what happens next will be nothing less than an open conspiracy.
For the time being... do not leave my side, whatever you do. Of course, it'd be even better if you decided to stay with me of your own accord.

opts: Want to take a look around?

lines.x: Heehee, I didn't expect you to be so forward. Caught me off guard.
Did something good happen?

opts: Miss Gravel, what do you think of the K.G.C.C.?

lines.x: Didn't you ask me something like this before? Or, do you just love the sound of my voice that much?
They're parasites on Kazimierz.
The business class bands together, corrupting the knights and the city, just to chase their ambitions.

opts: No.
The General Chamber of Commerce, as its name implies, is just a 'general' assemblage.
There is little that binds them beyond common interest, and they can be divided.
It feels like they're acting as a unit because they're fighting against the knights.

lines.x: What did you do?

opts: There are roughly four types of executive directors in the K.G.C.C.

lines.x: Heh... Care to tell me more?

opts: One is committed, so radical as to eliminate the knight class.
One is old-fashioned, a rock-solid faction that moves forward step by step.
Another has no principles, just a group of opportunists flocking to potential profit.

lines.x: And the last one?

opts: The type that Rhodes Island is not ready to contact yet.
Rushing to contact them will only hurt us.

lines.x: So what you're saying is there are cracks in the K.G.C.C.? And you... have already taken stock of them?

opts: I wouldn't call it 'cracks.'
More like it was never possible for them to 'unite' in the first place.
Live for the sake of profit, die for the sake of profit.

lines.x: But this is only your first time here, and you were only able to get to know a portion of the executives through your ties with a spokesman.
How can you be so sure of yourself? The moment you place your trust in the wrong person, the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi will no longer bear the consequences.

opts: Rhodes Island has never relied on the sacrifices of others to advance its goals.
As if I'd approach those banquets if I wasn't ready to sacrifice something.

lines.x: No.
Of course not.
Do you still have time to waste?
About the bounty placed on the Infected, once the interference from the Great Separation has passed, you'll have no escape route.
Ah, by the way, the reason it's called the 'Great Separation' is because after power from the core is lost, each plate will experience a short period of drift.
Right now, the connections to the plates around the K.G.C.C. have broken off, and you won't be able to escape.
Ugh... Goddammit...
This doesn't seem like an enemy we can take head-on.
I'm glad you finally understand what you should've always understood. So let me ask again, will you hand me the goods? I wouldn't mind letting you go.
Heh, when is the Armorless Union a charity?
I guess it was a matter of mindset. After a long, long struggle... I finally saw the light.
From the ruins of Bolívar, to the prairies of Kazimierz, and finally here.
No normal person finds joy in the slaughter of others. Only idiotic pampered princelings derive any pleasure from seeing people bleed.
Now, you can save me, Infected, and I can help you dismantle the Armorless Union.
Sorry, but even if that's true, I'm not about to give you this.
Why not?
We're sick and tired of being manipulated.
Ashley!
Got it! Have a taste of this!
Urgh...
*cough*, *cough* *cough*... What's the meaning of this...?
Ashley, run!
I know!
Running into the substructure? One wrong step, and you'll be mincemeat in the engine.
There's nowhere for you to run––
Hngh.
Ashley! Watch out!
A cyan arrow pierces Flametail Knight's armor.
Sona tries to block it, but the massive force of the impact sends her petite body flying.
Struggling in midair, she is launched into the gap between the city's plates.
Sona––!!
(Off the mark. Something blurred my vision for a second there...)
(An illusion caused by my fatigue...? No, something got in my way after all.)
You've lost your mind.
Tisk tisk, firing from such close range. Are you trying to get us both killed?
You... How dare you... do that to Sona!
I'll admit, it was careless of me.
I haven't had a chance to check if the chip is still intact. That's bad news, I wouldn't want that responsibility sitting on my shoulders.
Shut up, bastard!
(What to do, what to do. Hurry and kill Ashlock, and look for a way to go check the corpse?)
(No, this is far too dangerous. The power is about to come back on, and I wouldn't want to be caught between those plates.)
I'll have you pay in blood––!!
So straightforward, so predictable. You've been all too easy to read since you lost your ability to think––
Oh really?
Hrmph. Was I the careless one?
She's gone?
Was she only pretending to be enraged...?
Become a knight for the Infected.
Fight to the bitter end for the Infected.
That is my honor.
There is no greater honor... than to die for a glorious cause.
I would've liked to go out in a blaze of glory, but... what's going on now?
Roy is still around here somewhere. He'll discover my Arts sooner or later, so we mustn't tarry here.
