Contact your companion and have her escape. I'll do what I can for her.
Hey, Ashley, I'm fine.
Don't let him know, and look for a chance to escape. I slipped the chip into your pocket when I blocked that arrow for you. Go ahead and double-check.
No need to reply. See you on the way back.
You broke my fall? But how?
Arts can be applied in many different ways. After extensive studying, almost all Leithanians are able to use basic Arts, regardless of the nature of these Arts.
You're the Candle Knight, right?
Why would a great knight like you... save an Infected like me?
There's someone who wants to see you.
Who?
I can't tell you yet.
Oh... You should already know that we're pretty busy right now, paralyzing the city, attacking the K.G.C.C... I don't have time for a picnic, you know?
It's understandable that you would distrust me.
No, I believe you.
You can tell what someone's thinking, just gotta look into their eyes.
Of course, this trick doesn't work a hundred percent of the time, but this is desperate times, right?
Ugh... What the hell is going on? The roads are blocked, and I can't get a taxi. Do I not get to go home tonight either?
What an idiot... 'finish in one day' my ass... That report should've been done yesterday...
Wh—What... what's happening?!
Out of my way, or I'm shooting through all of you.
Eep! P-P-Please don't, I'm moving––
Stop moving around, lad. You wouldn't want that noggin of yours finding a new home, eh?
B-But she told me to move––
Would you really trust someone who'd take your head off on a whim?
Toland, have you no shame hiding behind a civilian?
Aaaaagh—!
Good point. I'll count to three?
P-P-P-please, I'm begging you, l-leave me alone!
One.
Nooooooo––!!
Two.
Three!
They're both insane! They're both insane––!
Where does this road lead–– Wait, over there, that's––
Mr. Knight! Mr. Knight!
I'm an employee of the Rose Paper Union! Please help me! You know who I am, right? I'll recommend you to the editorial department! There are two criminals over––
Eek...!
Do you ever run out of arrows?
Grrr... This is the first time my prey's eluded me for several minutes... Not half bad, Toland Cash.
Is it your goal to stall me here?
Haha... What about you? Are you here to play with me?
No, there's a handsome bounty on your head, and we're always in need of money.
Oh? Do you have a sickly little brother back home? Or maybe an elderly father who needs your support?
This job brings more wealth than you can imagine, bounty hunter.
We know the rules of this society better than you, better than any of the knights.
We simply chose to take a little detour, and after tonight's hunt, we'll usher in a new beginning.
But right now, it seems there's another idiot hellbent on messing things up.
To think you could still see such a splend'rous night sky in this death-choked city.
This is the kind of night I've always known. Let this manmade malediction be left behind... and shed every last drop of this city's blood! Bravo, ye faceless rebels!
What a strange knight. The private duels must not be paying well.
Clown.
Is everyone okay?
Margaret, I just found the spare battery... Alright, see, now the light's on, yeah? Doesn't feel like it'll last long though.
Looks like everyone's okay. V and Kowal went to check on the situation. They'll be back later.
Where's Młynar?
He stayed home. Said it would be difficult to get away from work, and wanted some time alone.
What about Shining and Nightingale?
They were worried that the Armorless Union might have their sights on the Doctor, and rushed back to the Infected shelter.
Don't worry. They're both very dependable.
No, it's just that... I still don't know anything about the things going on around you.
Sorry, but I deliberately kept you out of the loop. I can't afford to get Doctor and the others tangled up with my personal affairs.
But maybe one day, you'll get to know each other. Maybe one day, Maria, you'll also find yourself aboard that ship.
Their dazzling idealism will eventually guide everyone forward. Even if they hold vastly different beliefs and walk different paths, nothing can shake the conviction in their hearts.
That reminds me of...
The knights from the stories.
I'm back!
It's pitch black... The flashlight merchant next door should've restocked by now, right?
Oh, it's Margaret.
It's an absolute mess outside right now. I say we behave ourselves, stay indoors, and wait for the power to come back.
Just sit here doing nothing? Wasn't there supposed to be a match today?
It was supposed to be the day the eight strongest knights were decided.
Have you seen the latest news of the Blood Knight?
More or less... He's the undefeated king this season, from the qualifiers to the Major... But beyond that...
He's also the hero of the Infected.
Where are we going? I'm not complaining, but I really don't exactly have a lot of free time on my hands.
We're here.
...the Wall of Champions exhibit hall?
This is a secret meeting, so try to keep your voice down.
So which knight wanted to meet me?
Knight...? Well, you're not technically wrong. She was indeed a respectable knight.
Haha, but don't be nervous... She's just a regular old lady now.
Head on in.
You're not coming?
Let's just say... she's one of the few people in Kazimierz who scares me even a bit.
Wow... Even the Candle Knight, bearer of the Candle Sword, is afraid of someone? What chance do I have then?
It probably won't be the same for you.
After all, she counts as my half adoptive mother, at least in name.
(It's the same emergency power supply as the K.G.C.C... Was this place not affected by the outage either?)
(Both the knights... and the General Chamber...)
(...are still shining through the darkness.)
The lights flicker.
And so does Sona's train of thought.
The knights, and the money, were able to avoid the deep night.
Who drowns in the darkness?
Flametail Knight... Sona.
Come here and let me have a closer look at you.
You! You're...!
Now, now. I didn't come here today to talk business.
I heard... the Infected knights made some kind of deal with the knights of the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi.
Between the Knights Association and Demian, a member of the Adeptus.
In order for the Infected to survive in the Grand Knight Territory, they agreed to participate in this operation with his cooperation.
The Great Kawalerielki Separation... the Adeptus gave in, letting it happen once more.
Look at this wall... These knights were supposed to become real heroes. Not... this, symbols for spectators to gawk at.
Does this sight not fill you with great sorrow?
It certainly does, ma'am.
But I feel more sorrow for... the Infected who still need equal treatment, and for those totally lost to entertainment and materialism.
I cry... for each and every person struggling to make it to the next sunrise here in Kazimierz.
Truly a spectacular inaugural speech, young squirrel.
Can the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi really give us a normal life?
Paperwork, procedures, any documentation the National Council needs. Of course, the Adeptus will handle it all for you. That is what Demian promised you.
But what about the Originium crystals on your body?
I admire Pinus Sylvestris, but the problems you must overcome... are not so well-defined.
In time, Demian will reach out to you. He may not be the sharpest tool in the shed, but trust him. He has his ways.
Alright.
Good. My knightclub will be here shortly, so hurry and find your companions.
Also, tell Viviana to come and have a meal with me when she's not busy.
.........Ma'am, what does it mean to be a knight?
The answer to the question is different for everyone, child.
You wouldn't be interested in mine anyway. Us old folks, we've seen too much.
Go, little squirrel, go.
See if the sunrise in Kazimierz still blazes ever-bright.
...I lost her.
Would it be more efficient to post a surround and suppress mission later?
I'm here. What's the matter?
What...? How many?
Exceptional swordsmanship.
Ahh, I'd prefer not to waste any more time here with you, old friend.
Goddamned maniac...
I'd best get out of here after I kill Toland.
I'm in a game of hide-and-seek with the 'Guildmaster' and a Nightzmora. Not the best time to chat.
No... I'll head there now.
Let's wrap up our little game, Toland.
You're running?
No.
Same goes for you, Nightzmora.
What? You're a Nightzmora?!
Let's call it here for now.
You came to Kawalerielki to find your people and a good fight, right?
Don't speak so lightly of our tradition of glory, Kazimierzian. You don't have that right.
No, I'm just offering you a warning, Nightzmora, out of the kindness of my heart.
Don't waste your energy on us.
I can agree with that.
Boastful nonsense.
Hmph. See you never, dumbass.
Is the traffic still stuck? I heard the power's already come back in some places.
Shouldn't the Knights Association compensate us for our terrible losses?!
Yeah! The government should pay us back our travel expenses!
Ugh... I hope the meat in the fridge didn't go bad.
Are you sure?
Sigh...
You know, we can take out any single one of their people, alone.
Two of them, we'll need to bring help.
Three of them, and it's going to be one hell of a rough battle.
If there's three or more of them... that could take a full tactical squad, and the Armorless Union can't afford those losses.
If there's more of them than you can count on your fingers, calling in the entire Armorless Union might not even be enough to put them down.
I know, I've seen them before. Your concern is better placed elsewhere.
So, how many did the scouts see?
Roughly forty.
Well shit... Right at this juncture?
Yes. I've already notified the Board of Directors.
Do they intend to capture the Kawalerielki Alliance?
Impossible!
No, this can't be! Where are the military consultants? Where are the people monitoring the knights?!
Those campaign knights dispatched by the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi... Where were they in the last report?
Five hundred and seventy-three kilometers.
There's no way a normal squad of campaign knights would be able to march that fast... It's impossible...
Did the Adeptus mobilize another branch behind the General Chamber's back? No, that can't be...
Wait.
The Silverlance fast-response squad... What's their fastest record?
Could they have disguised their military identification code? And even their armor and lances?
Hm? It's a little brighter now, isn't it?
Look over there! That little halo of light on the other side of the road! The power must've come back over there!
But... the lights still won't turn on...
That looks a lot more like... moonlight?
Moonlight.
The prosperity afforded by the advent of the energized glass tubes gradually led to modern man forgetting the appearance of the primordial moonlight.
This city, engulfed in a brief moment of darkness––
––is illuminated by a silver glow.
Are..... are those knights over there?
Th-The campaign knights?! Here?!
The knights pass through the crowd.
Through the city known as the City of Knights.
Through the city that does not recognize them.
Through the city that they do not recognize.
They came from a faraway fortress close to the border.
Their bodies are stained with the clear and tangible scent of the soil.
A scent mixed with flesh, blood, bones, and the lives of countless heroes buried on battlefields past.
Inform all members of the Armorless Union to stop whatever they're doing this instant, no matter what it is.
Do not make any moves unless you want trouble. Do not act rashly.
Hmm.
Monique?
That one... over there...
Nostalgic?
No.
...I certainly am.
I miss... that time when I stupidly thought this place wasn't so bad. Those were good times. Exciting times.
If you had really given up, then you wouldn't still be here, would you?
Młynar, take a good look all around you. The city sleeps, and nobody has ever even woken up.
But what about you?
Reporting! A portion of the communications lines are back––!
––Connect me to the associate justice of the National Council.
Yessir.
Your honor?
You already know what I'm about to say. This band of campaign knights trespassed into the Grand Knight Territory without authorization, violating the Second Act––
Former Associate Justice Duke of the National Council, has been detained on suspicion of bribery, smuggling, murder, and dereliction of duty.
I would advise you to choose your words calmly and carefully, Spokesman.
The Grand Knight Territory is in a state of emergency due to a blackout, and the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi has the authority to send troops into the city during a state of emergency.
Do you have any further questions?
None. I apologize for the intrusion, sir.
Mr. McKee.
Yes?
Communications are more or less restored, Mr. McKee.
Look how busy they are over there... What do you think a phone is?
Huh? Obviously a communication tool...
No. The phone is the spindle and plough of modern society, the pathway that weaves the fabric of life as we currently know it.
It is a symbol of greatness, Mr. McKee.
McKee's words fail him.
Suddenly, he senses an aura of sorts from Malkiewicz.
Over the last few months, a business venture failed spectacularly, amounting to nothing. Those who have been whipped almost naturally give off a certain kind of aura.
He has seen this kind of aura in many people before.
Most of them were chewed up and mangled by the changing times or by the competition. As for the few who survived––
––nearly all of them turned out the same.

lines.st: 'This world is the very last enemy that the knights shall face.'
'The cities should have stood still above the earth. Instead, they fed on their citizens' sweat and toil, and even began to creep their way forward.'
'The cities are living monsters. Now, gather everyone to rise up against these fortresses. Let us return to them the purest glory there is!'
'Let grassland be grassland again. Let sky be sky. Let the people be resolute once more. Let our glory last forever!'
'I shall be the last knight!'

lines.x: What's happening? Where are the campaign knights headed? The Adeptus?
No...
They went to the Wall of Champions exhibition hall, where the champions of years past are granted their awards and have their portraits displayed.
Why...? Shouldn't they be going after the government?
The Board of Directors is discussing that right now. The way I see it, the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi's trying to save face, and this is their first step.
As for their next step... well... they might try to impose martial law with the garrison.
But in this day and age, things aren't going to be as simple as holding a knife to somebody's neck... The foolish knights haven't realized that.
The era of violence is long behind us.
Hm...
Sorry, but you'll have to pay Area 0 another visit. The blackout will take a toll on the Infected shelter and treatment center.
See if it's run into any problems.
Okay.
Report, Mr. Spokesman. The power grid... is starting to recover! We're working to restart it now!
A silver ray of light makes its way through the darkness.
The extravagant appearance is surrounded by a boisterous audience.
The streetlights flicker, before the colorful neon lights start to rouse the city from its brief slumber.
The Silverlance Pegasus seems out of place against the backdrop of the neon billboards.
However, all those who are present: tumultuous passersby, the panicking knights, and the excited tourists...
All of them have the same misconception. They think the Pegasus is the source of the light.
And the city is slowly coming back to life behind them.
Grandmaster Ioleta.
It's been a while, Liam.
You've ventured far. Alas, it doesn't seem we have much time for rest.
We don't need rest. There is nothing more urgent than cleaning up the filth for Kazimierz's sake.
Heh, look at you... Impatient as always.
Would you like to see tonight's main course?
Margaret Nearl...?
She should not have refused your invitation. She could have been the finest among our ranks and avoided her fate.
Yet she's back in Kazimierz now, even stepping into that false arena... Did exile really change Margaret Nearl?
You fought her before. Do you think that's the kind of person she is?
Hmm... Time can change everything. It might have changed her too.
Look at the wall behind you, Liam. Look at the champions of Majors past.
Why do you think these knights of this slowly collapsing era shine so brilliantly, even now?
An exceptional knight or two means nothing, Grandmaster.
The knight competitions are still blasphemous and the root cause of Kazimierz's downfall.
Each of my journeys back to the Grand Knight Territory fill me with sorrow. Our people are losing respect, and our noble ideals have become a tired joke.
Perhaps... but that has always been the case since the campaign knights left the Grand Knight Territory.
Alas, the times belong to the young ones. Our conclusions no longer matter.
So you too have realized as much, Doctor.
I should have expected no less.

opts: We can't go too overboard right in the middle of Kazimierz.
My first priority is the safety of Amiya and all our operators.
None of you would want us to act rashly.

lines.x: I understand... Nearl didn't want us to return to your side during the Major to begin with.
But I couldn't ignore the information I got from the local Infected. It was getting more and more dire by the minute... I thought I had to let you and Amiya know.
No, you made the right decision, Shining.
If the Infected are really facing... inhumane treatment in Area 0, then we can't stand idly by for the sake of our own 'safety.'
Still... the Doctor's concerns are reasonable too... Kazimierz, Ursus, and Lungmen are all different...

opts: Imagine.
We could bomb the facility now, rescue all the Infected, and skip town.
We could talk to the General Chamber and buy out all the remaining Infected.
We could report this to the National Council, and let the Adeptus handle things.

lines.x: Violence is out of the question.
If we cross that line, Kazimierz has the power to knock us over in a split second.
We're in the dead center of the Grand Knight Territory. This country might be fundamentally different from Ursus, but... this is still a nation of knights.
No matter how intense its internal conflicts have gotten, this is still Kazimierz.
Rhodes Island can't make an enemy of this place.
Uhh... W-We probably can't do that...
Even if we could buy all the Infected with money... what happens after that? You think the people of the Grand Knight Territory will just stop getting infected?
Besides, that would only turn the Infected into a commodity.
And that wouldn't solve anything.
The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi... We still don't know the whole truth of Area 0.

The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi and the K.G.C.C. are not on the best of terms, and the Adeptus's attitude towards the Infected is ambiguous at best... It's possible they've been tolerating them right from the start.
If that really is the case... what could Rhodes Island do?
Doctor...
What will you do?

opts: We'll solve their problems their way.

lines.x: Over there! After her!
Gah!
(The power's back already... Knowing Sona, she'll find a way to rendezvous.)
Call for backup right now—
Turn the corner here—Nng!
Just as I'm enjoying my time lounging around Area 0... the lights go off on me.
I almost got the impression the Armorless Union was finally so stingy they stopped paying their checkpoints' power bills. Up until I got the backup request, that is.
Platinum...!
You again...?
This really makes me wish you and Flametail Knight got a harder beating last time.
Too bad... harder I'm beat, the harder I fight back.
Now out of the way—!
You never learn, do you...? Guess I'll have to teach you another lesson.
(She's keeping her distance—As long as I block her shot, I'll have a chance to return her attack!)
She's here! Help Platinum!
(Crap! Behind me—?!)
Urgh!
What...?
Ngh...! Did she shatter the streetlight?
What if the shards cut my face? Sheesh...
Justyna!
Ashlock, get out of there.
We'll hold her back somehow.
Alright!
Don't even think about it!
Grr... what a pain. Let me take cover first. I'll find you...
A standard-issue light crossbow doesn't have that range...
No, are there two of them—?
Fartooth, looks like she's on to me.
Time to move, then.
Never get into a sniper war with the Armorless. Then again... It's two-on-one right now.
(They stopped... Did they change positions? Smart.)
(If I'd known this would be such a pain, I would've ignored the call for backup...)
(Still...)
Platinum closes her eyes.
She thinks back to something her parents told her long ago.
They told her that her sharp eyes would bring honor and glory to the family.
Has she found glory yet?
I got you now, Szewczyk.
Urgh—?!
An arrow brushes past Szewczyk's face, fierce as a windstorm.
A few centimeters' difference and his brain would be torn to pieces.
—She's fighting from such a distance?!
Talk to me, Szewczyk!
Dammit! Don't stop! Keep moving!
Hnn—
This arrow's a present for you... Merry Christmas.
Special arrowheads... And they go hard.
She can pin down our positions even in complex urban terrain like this?
They're gone...
Did they get away?
I'm not one to run like that, Platinum.
You... What did you do to my family?!
What did you do to them?!
You realized you can't fight me at range, so you're making this a melee?
Good thinking.
Ngh—
Pathetic... A father ending up like this because he crossed the Armorless Union.
You little—!
I didn't kill your wife and child.
—Where are you hiding them?!
If you just did what the Armorless Union wanted, I could get you on the phone with them..
You...
You want me to betray Pinus Sylvestris?
Are you a father... or are you a knight?
Do you have to go so far for the Infected, Szewczyk?
Don't move.
That's as far as you go, Platinum... At this range, your reflexes are no match for my bolts.
Make your choice.
Grr...
None of you... give a damn about us knights...
Anyone can become a knight by joining the competitions. It's easier if you're born into knight nobility, though, yeah.
The knighthood... is kind of just a disappointing dream.
Tch...!
—Is pride more important than your family?
I was never going to trust you anyway, you dirty assassin!
Do it, Fartooth!
Looks like order has returned to the streets.
That was a huge ruckus...
Did Sona and her friends do all that?
Perhaps... there are others fanning the flames.
Their hearts are filled with anger. They want to rise up against their unfair treatment, and the Infected's fight... will end up exploited by another party for another purpose.
I've seen tragedies like this time and again.
Are you thinking about helping them?
Your choice is between fighting for the Infected and fighting as a knight. Those choices couldn't be more mutually exclusive these days—
—The injustice that the Infected face is just the tip of the iceberg. Suffering takes more forms than our eyes can see.
I believe there will be those who will uphold justice for the Infected, and I trust that they will achieve just that.
Margaret...
But it's not up to us to rescue them. It'll all be meaningless if they find themselves rescued by those who already have a firm conviction.
A rare visitor.
It's been some time, Sir Młynar.
Don't 'Sir' me. You're the only knight here.
If you're here for Margaret, I'm afraid you'll be disappointed.
It has been years since I last came back to the Grand Knight Territory. I am shocked to find our hero's estate... in this shape.
Have the sisters moved out?
My apologies... That is your family issue.
Was there something else...?
Sir Młynar—
Stop calling me that.
Others may look down on you, but we will not.
Even if Margaret strayed onto the wrong path and her younger sister is still young, the Nearl family didn't earn our respect riding on a legacy of fame.
The boastful merchants have not realized this fundamental idea, and that is why they do not understand the actions that knights take.
And, truly, just seeing you in good health eases my mind.
You mean my appearance? Spare me the pleasantries, sir.
If you truly have any respect for me, please leave.
Sir Młynar.
We have the utmost respect for you... no, for the Nearl family.
The age of heroes is long behind us...
Does a mere citizen of Kazimierz like me really deserve such respect from a Silverlance Pegasus?
What a bizarre evening, Doctor...
It'll be daybreak soon. I'm sorry I couldn't come sooner. The K.G.C.C. has a lot on its hands, after all...

opts: Tell me your thoughts on Area 0.

lines.x: I'd like to think... there's no hidden meaning in that question.
You are a clever one.

opts: Regrettably.

lines.x: Right...
Do you think that... the way the Grand Knight Territory treats its Infected is a reasonable measure?
You don't need to answer me. Both you and I know the answer—It's only a compromise.
We could neither make the perfect, humane solution nor bring ourselves to go with the bloody, primitive answer, and this is the result.

opts: That's unreasonable, Mr. Malkiewicz.

lines.x: I'm not sure if you've noticed, but our society today is built on top of all kinds of unreasonable things. You might want to poke through our history books a little.

You may not know this... but after the nation of pegasi was overthrown in the upheaval wrought by the Nightzmora and this country of knights came to be, it was the affinities who actually banded together first.
The affinities toiled away for the knights' profits and tended their lands. Then, they came together, and chased the tyrannical knights off the stage.
And now? The K.G.C.C. controls both a hitman agency and the competition knights, and those who are being controlled will always try to rise up and break free of tyranny—
History repeats itself, Doctor.
The spokesman before me had some dirty dealings too. He paid the price for them, and now it's my turn to step into this position, pressing forward no matter how hard the job.
Does all this fit your concept of morality? Is it 'reasonable?'
I don't know how you've come to know this, but you've no doubt already noticed...
The way the General Chamber has decided to deal with those who get injured in the arena, those whose Oripathy has progressed beyond control... is to put them out of their misery.
Is it unreasonable? Of course. I want to tell them 'no' myself.
But do we have to keep treating those Oripathy patients, when this disease is incurable?
Each day that Oripathy remains incurable is yet another day where coexistence is out of reach.

opts: What you're talking about is murder.

lines.x: Everyone who found it unreasonable has been eliminated, creating this thoroughly reasonable history. Fortunately, I've accepted this truth. You understand me, don't you?
You're trying to take on Kazimierz... and I suggest you stop.
We may have other options, and if you can think of anything, I'll be more than willing to help.
But not right now.
The prime suspect in last night's blackout is none other than the Infected knights.
Let's suppose the blackout caused a traffic accident... or an elderly person who relies on a ventilator stopped breathing because of it—
Will we have to turn a blind eye to tragedies like these in the name of helping the Infected?
Allowing the Infected to participate in the competitions in large numbers has been a source of stress to everyone—
And this stress comes not from some organization or people out there. It comes from society itself, Doctor.

opts: But the Infected are dying as we speak, and we are doing nothing about it.
You're right. 'We may have other options.'

lines.x: There are many people dying of unnatural causes right this moment. Disease, Catastrophe. We can't help everyone.
I'm sorry.
True...
If you have an idea... I'm sure you'll get it done.
But please remember what I just said. Don't treat this whole affair like you're helping the elderly cross the street.
The contradictions that persist in Area 0 are complicated.
Sorry... Perhaps I've gone too far with my words.
It'll benefit the both of us if you could calm down and think all this over, carefully.
...Doctor.
Someone here to see you.
I'll get out of your way...
And don't get the wrong idea... If possible, I would like to be on your side.
That said, it's difficult to find the one best answer out of all the unreasonable things we can do nothing about.
You're right... The blackout, the Radiant Knight, and the Blood Knight... It all fueled the Infected knights' will to resist.
Before everything gets out of hand, I hope both you and I can keep casualties... to a minimum.
'Goodness pays off,' or so it's often said, but I'm afraid this no longer holds true in our society. Unless, that is, you reach out and grab hold of the payoff yourself.
Ashley!
Sona... Thank goodness you're okay!
You fooled Roy, huh? What a performance. How did you pull it off?
I just imagined how it'd feel if I actually lost you.
Whoa, a method actor...
Where are the others?
Iwona's hurt. Bad... But she'll live.
Iwona...
(Dejected clicking noises)
She's the one who fought most like a warrior... She stood firm to the very end.
Justyna and Plastic's gone to deal with Platinum... We have to hurry and help them!
No need. She's gone.
We aren't so rusty that the two of us can't take her on. She's not that strong.
Where's Justyna...?
Today's matches must continue as normal. Is that understood?
We've made sure the Major competition venues are fully operational.
Good. Keep the spectators calm. Tonight's match must go off without a hitch.
Remember. It's not just the audience. After all that ruckus, the directors will be paying keen attention to this event.
If we perform well, we'll all get hefty bonuses. If not...
Understood!
And I appreciate your efforts, Mob.
Don't mention it! I'm honored to commentate tonight—
I suppose it goes without saying, but pretend as though nothing had happened last night.
Of the four events tonight, the match between the Radiant Knight and Nightmare Knight would undoubtedly be the hottest one.
However... After the Infected instigated an incident of such scale... the Board of Directors isn't very keen on letting the Radiant Knight continue her streak.
So... they want the judges to disqualify her?
We'll make a decision as the competition evolves, but you must 'convey' to the audience how reasonable it is once the judges make the call.
As for the Blood Knight, we've already sent someone to work this out with the Crimson Chalice Knightclub. We should come to a decision on that matter soon.
As for the matter on hand... the Radiant Knight and Nightmare Knight... They're just two maniacs.
Understood! I'll head off and prepare for the match!
What's the matter? You look pale.
Darksteel disappeared after the blackout. All three of them.
This is still confidential, but several of K.G.C.C. executives were assassinated during the blackout...
Things are hectic at the crime scene. All evidence points to the Infected, but... I don't think it was them. The Infected aren't that good.
As we speak... something terrifying is slowly unfolding.
You mean...?
Yes.
The Armorless Union has betrayed us.
Batbayar, do you always have so much free time on your hands?
Look who's talking!
Aren't you a titled Major knight? You have no training grounds. You don't have so much as a home. Do you seriously wander the streets of the Grand Knight Territory day in and day out?
When a hunter loses his way in the forest, he will naturally provide for himself and build himself a wooden shed.
You are standing in one of the greatest cities of the world right now...
—City!
The unusual roar stuns the old knight.
Everyone in their vicinity are staring at them as though they are wild beasts of the forest.
The city... is a symbol of weakness.
Right after this civilization conquered barbarism, they built their tall towers here.
In these towers, humanity is no longer necessary.
What are you even on about...?
Kawalerielki has warriors worthy of fighting. Here, they have warriors worthy of my trust.
Yet they are all resisting this city. The obedient are frail. The rebellious are eternal.
This is a symbol of civilization's corruption, Knight. We must return to those times of blood and glory.
Son... You're living in the past.
I live in glory.
That era is not yet behind us, and I hope to experience it tonight.
Well, I've got no more to say to you, then. Margaret will win.
She has the scent of a hero...
I only hope her talents have not been eroded by the hustle and bustle of this era.
Welcome to the Kazimierz Major, and welcome to tonight's Kazimierz Major!
As the events continue, we're getting bigger and bigger audiences joining us here in the arena!
I'm honored to announce today that the cumulative spectator count and the total prize pool have once again pushed to the highest numbers in the history of the Major!
There's no doubt Kazimierz has a bright future ahead!
Can't I go to the arena to watch the match? Margaret should have gotten some tickets, no?
Silly girl. Do you want to be attacked by the Armorless again?
Ugh...
We're openly standing up against the Armorless Union... Do you know what that means?
We have to keep a low profile. If we're going to fight them, we might not even be able to stay in the Grand Knight Territory.
Honestly... I really have no idea how things turned out this way.
Hmm... If this isn't...
Czarny's lackey, huh...?
Hello, Sir Tytus.
You can sit.
Becoming a spokesman has really lifted your spirits, hasn't it?
Not at all...
You were given those orders in such a perilous situation. Did you not find it all strange, under those circumstances?
Mr. Czarny left in such a hurry... There were a lot of things I never had the chance to ask him.
Do you want to ask him?
I'm somewhat well connected with the K.G.C.C... If it pleases you, I can help you find out where Czarny was exiled.
Don't get the wrong idea. Losing to the Radiant Knight again proved to me that I'm not the Tytus Topola who defeated seven knights with one hand anymore.
Before I retire, I have to curry favor with you spokesmen... What's that look for?
To tell the truth, I never expected this from a man of your caliber. You're the core of the Blade Helmet Knightclub, once a top 4 hopeful, and a candidate for the title of Grand Knight...
I never imagined you would entertain the idea of retirement yourself.
You are so... prideful in the arenas... I thought you would...
It's not easy to be a competition knight, Malkiewicz. Outside the arena, we're no different than you, and we need an exit strategy.
I'm plenty satisfied just to be able to fight this far with all four of my limbs intact.
The times for ideals are over, Malkiewicz.

In tonight's match, not only will we get to see two of the biggest names in the tournament duking it out, but we have two independent knights facing off. We haven't seen a spectacle like this in years!
No corporate sponsorship! No knightclub training or support! If they need their helmets polished and weapons sharpened, they have no one to rely on but themselves!
Yet they both stand here today in the arena. And one of them is a former champion! All eyes are on our champ, eager for her return!
Please welcome the young legend, the champion from the Nearl family, the Radiant Knight, MARRRGAREEEEET NEAAAAARRRLLLLLLL!
And on this side, the descendant of a race left on the pages of the history books, a rare Kuranta, war-god scions who flay the years past—
We could go through the entire history of the knight sports and find no one with purer blood, purer horror, and purer might than him!
Now, here comes the lone wanderer from the grasslands, the last Kheshig, Nightmare Knight TOLAAAAAAAAA!
What's this?! The Nightzmora's horn! The match is off to a fiery start! Will the champion fall to the ancient race?! Will this new conquest be cut short by the Radiant Knight?!
Let us—
Radiant Knight.
Ohh? Nightmare suddenly slammed his spear into the ground. W-What is he doing? Is he saluting his opponent?
I've been waiting for this moment... for far too long.
Kazimierz's knights have been a disappointment.
I've heard of you from Master Vogelweide, Nightmare.
What are you seeking?
I am seeking my fate, pegasus.
All who belong to my ancient race must set off on a Khaganquest when they come of age...
None of us know what awaits us during our Khaganquest, but it is only when our journey is at an end that our lives have truly begun.
Whether we fight fierce beasts or visit foreign lands, a Nightzmora who finishes his Khaganquest will know its meaning by the journey's end.
Then, we become plumules of terror itself, spreading fear as we make our way across the lands.
Only by knowing the world will we know our destiny.
Have you found your Khaganquest's meaning yet?
Not yet, Pegasus. The road ahead... is like a forest shrouded in fog.
I truly hope that you will be my meaning.
Come.
Are you Plastic's... Szewczyk's family?
Who are you?
I'm... a knight. A friend of his who's a knight.
Have you... been mistreated in any way at all?
Mistreated? I'm not sure I follow...
That day, a knight in white told me that Szewczyk was killed by an enemy he made in the arena.
After that, she brought us here, and we've been hiding ever since.
I got this from Szewczyk...
Oh! This is the wood carving I gave him.
Where is he? Is he doing okay?
I'll take you to him.
Let's make a deal.
As long as you can promise me that you will never again make an enemy of the Armorless Union, I can tell you where your wife is.
What...?
And you, Fartooth. Convey this to everyone in Pinus Sylvestris:
There's been some issues within the Armorless Union, even I noticed.
No one wants to be used for no good reason. That obviously sucks...
Unbelievable.
Can you alone represent the will of the entire Armorless Union? I mean, you may be Platinum—
But I'm still only middle management, huh?
Yeah, that's the deal... Most of the Armorless were hired by the K.G.C.C. I'm only here to manage things for them... and no one can stop the Board of Directors's plans.
Still though, wouldn't things be a lot easier for you if us middle managers stopped doing anything?
After all, the Board has been pretty angsty lately, and our new recruits kinda subpar...
What's that? It was the Armorless who took out the power?
Roy even wrecked the server room and laid the blame on us.
Is the Armorless Union... trying to shake off the Board's control?
Grr...!
So we've been used, right from the start?!
Our fight... our sacrifice... all of it—!
(Dejected clicking noises)
Sorry...
No.
After the Candle Knight rescued me, I ran into Ioleta.
—The Grand Knight?! You met the Grand Knight?!
Ahaha, she's a little younger and kinder than she seems on the papers and TV news.
She agreed to give us all... the legal status we wanted. Our deal still stands.
Ashley, do you have the chip?
Of course...
But, as things stand right now... the public thinks the Infected are the blame for everything that happened...
Sona, does this 'legal' status actually... mean anything anymore?
Words can't describe how I feel right now—
No doubt all of you are already familiar with the Radiant Knight's strength, but look at Nightmare Knight! See the stunning techniques this warrior of yore has in store for us!
This is just unbelievable! Neither of them are using any serious Arts. This is a contest of pure combat and weapon prowess!

This is more over-the-top than any action blockbuster I've ever seen. Look at their lightning quick moves, their powerful slashes! It's only been ten minutes, but the whole arena's already absolutely stunned!
Look! Check out Nightmare Knight's moves! He's on the same level as the champions!
I knew he'd be worth keeping an eye on! I spent four hundred on him!
What an extraordinary match.
I've never seen anything like the way the Nightzmora wields his weapon.
It's like the campaign knights' techniques, but also not quite the same... Hm...
What's the matter, Left-hand Knight, sir?
Looks like we have an unexpected guest in the audience... The rumors from the other day are true, then.
What do you mean—Ugh—!
A ray of silver light stands solemnly in the corner of the audience at the very back.
His presence may be matched by none, but even he is engulfed by the audience's cheers and screams.
It's my first time to watch the Major up close like this...
It makes me want to vomit.
And what's so revolting about this, Liam?
I see. I know your answer now.
In that case, from your point of view, what do you think of these two knights in the arena, the Nightzmora and the pegasus?
I have to admit... their techniques are beautiful.
The Nightzmora may not be a battle-hardened veteran, but a certain obsession has driven him to become a true warrior.
As for Margaret... Hmm...
What's wrong?
I thought your eyes on this would be sharper than mine, Grandmaster.
She's far too different... from what she used to be.
Haah...
Hff—
The exhilarating exchange of blows has ground to a halt! This is the first time the storm of battle has paused!
Don't these two need to breathe?! Are these moves even possible for mere mortals?!
Beautiful swordsmanship, pegasus.
Likewise.
You... excite me.
Did you know? According to legend, the Khagan once lost to a pegasus with golden blood, in his younger years.
I'm curious... Is there really a Kuranta in this world with golden blood in her veins?
Sorry to disappoint, but the blood on my face is red.
Warmup is over, Radiant Knight.
You live up to your name. And so—
The air is starting to freeze.
It isn't until the audience come back to their senses that they notice they forgot to breathe.
Is it fear?
Why are they afraid?
The entire audience... including all the knights feel a strange sense of repression.
—Let reality give way to me.
This is the first time Margaret has used her Arts subconsciously in the arena.
The intense radiance is almost like that of the sun, lighting up vast night sky.
Despite the blinding light, the young Nightzmora stands unflinching as ever.
Just as the name implies, a knight competition is nothing but a competition.
However, in this instant, Margaret feels something. It's the same aura that she has felt from her strongest enemies.
Catastrophes, the wails of the Infected, and their roars, the blood of the Infected, and their last struggles on the brink of death.
The young Nightzmora has never seen any mountains of corpses or seas of blood for himself.
Yet his lineage taught him how to control fear the moment he was born.
His pure lineage.
...(Ancient language) The young hunter sets out on his Khaganquest ♪ From dreams he goes, towards the shores of gold ♪
(Ancient language) A halberd in hand, stained red with blood of kin ♪
A tragic yet heroic song...
...telling an ancient story.
Hah!
—Ugh!
Wh-What just happened?
Some kind of Arts?! He used some Arts, and everyone in the arena is feeling a—uh, sensation, I guess!
But! With a single step, Nightmare Knight roused up a blast wave that slammed right into the artificial boulders!
What a fierce strike! The Radiant Knight had no choice but to fall back!
A strike that pushes back a champion! A strike that dispels her light! Even the most radiant of lights cannot stop the Nightzmora's conquest!
There's no applause...
Everyone's immersed in... these emotions.
That strike...
Did you notice something?
Any other knight... would be dead by now.
Uhh...?
He's doing this for real.
He's the real deal...
Just a lost child.
Poor kid.
What's going on?
(What?! So sudden? You sure? Uhh...)
This just in! The Arts that Nightmare Knight just used have been deemed an adverse effect on our audience members and judges!
This is a clear violation of Articles 3 and 7 of the Major's rules!

'Competition knights may not harm or threaten the safety of the persons or property of the audience' and 'Competition knights may not in any way interfere with the judges' ability to make subjective rulings!'
(Dammit, this is way too much of a stretch! Couldn't they have at least picked a better time?!)
This match is now over—! So—
—Come!
Light...
Gather round.
W-What's going on?! It's like the knights didn't hear our ruling!
The match is over! Separate immediately and wait for the judges!
Hah!
Ah! What beautiful swordhand!
In the blink of an eye... Right before my blade could slice your skull, you countered my attack...
You've witnessed death. You've witnessed cruelty. You tremble in the face of neither fear nor me!
...Huff...
Strong opponents and insurmountable difficulties do not frighten me.
On the contrary, the paralysis and degeneracy this city shows even right this moment are much more terrifying than you.
Somebody, stop these two! Pull them apart!
Modern Kazimierz is indeed sickening—
—Come, let fear open the eyes of these spectators. Show them just what it is they are watching.
That's enough.
—What?
A figure reminiscent of blood fissures the earth.
The ground between the two is cleaved apart by a giant axe appearing from thin air.
I said: that's enough.
The match is over...
It's you... The Blood Knight.
Who could have known?! No one could possibly have anticipated that the one who would finally put a stop to this unstoppable battle would be—!
'Kazimierz's Crimson Goblet', the hero, Dikaiopolis! It's not an easy name to remember, but be sure not to take your eyes off this—
That's the champion of the Kazimierz Major, undoubtedly the most popular of them all, the god of war from Minos! THE—BLOOOOOOOOOD—KNIIIIIIIIIGHT!!
(Man, thank goodness he showed up. Guess I put on a decent enough show today? Hey, one of you, go check on the spokesman and tell me how he's reacting!)
Dikaiopolis... A Minoan name. So he's the Infected who took the title three years ago.
This is my first time seeing the Blood Knight fight in person too... What a potent aura.
Hmph...
Whoa! The Blood Knight leaped into the arena! And he's walking toward our brawling contestants!
It's been years since Kazimierz has seen something like this!! Two champions! We have two champions on the same stage! What a sight to behold!

Of course, of course! The man standing on the other side isn't exactly your average Józef either, having just demonstrated his immense strength. What's more, that's Kazimierz's last Nightzmora we're talking about—
This here, everyone, is a million dollar marvel! Do not ever forget this moment! This is one for the history books!
You... another champion...
Your next opponent is me, Nightzmora.
And my next opponent is you.
Hah... hahaha... I sense you imply I will lose to you.
Absolutely.
How will a phantom who lives in the past possibly defeat me?
You, Infected, I look forward to the day I fight against you.
Do you think you can just walk away?
–––Hraagh!
Nightzmora, you should understand that you can only instill fear in those who live in the 'present'.
(He managed to deflect that strike from such an angle...?)
We will meet on the field.
Margaret turns around to look at the axe-marked ground.
The Blood Knight walks over to retrieve his weapon.
It seems as though blood is spilling out of the lifeless fissure in the earth.
Our champion, having appeared out of nowhere, has put a stop to the battle with a thunderous momentum!
Last night's match was cut short when Nightmare Knight's Arts were ruled a violation, and the one who stopped things from getting even further out of hand was the hero, the Blood Knight!
The match between the Radiant Knight and Nightmare Knight ended in a tie, and Nightmare will be facing the Blood Knight, whose opponent has dropped out, in his next round—
A tie...? Nightmare broke the rules. Shouldn't they have promoted the Radiant Knight to the next round?
They don't want to let an Infected to just move up the ranks like that, of course.
Where's Szewczyk?
He's with his family. Give him some time.
To be honest... I didn't really care much about him to begin with.
We can tell.
True enough. Were you trying to hide it?
He's still alive, real flesh and blood, right?
The city makes us forget it a lot... but we're all alive.
We should have our own choices, our own lives... instead of drifting about in the endlessly beating waves, never to find out who we really are.
The battle of a lifetime between the Blood Knight and Nightmare Knight will begin tomorrow evening!
Be sure to tune into the Night of Knights channel tomorrow at 8 o'clock for the best competition experience!
(Loud beeping noises)
Oh... Justice! You're okay!
Iwona! You can't get up yet.
Come, I'll help you.
Heh... Thanks, Ashley.
I thought I had exclusive rights to the nickname Ashley?
Haha, let me borrow it for a while.
You're gonna have to pay for using my trademark.
Iwona, that day...
Yeah, I saw the Blood Knight's silhouette.
There were traces of blood Arts...
It's... a really awful kind of Arts. As things are, just casting normal Arts is enough to make me ache. I can't imagine how much pain the Blood Knight has to bear, controlling Arts like that.
The Blood Knight... He's strong.
Everybody knows.
It's thanks to the Blood Knight that the Infected won the right to fight in the Major.
We became the K.G.C.C.'s playthings... We lost our dignity and freedom... but we're still alive.
It's only by staying alive that we have the chance to run.
Between the Radiant Knight and the Blood Knight, who do you support, girls?
The Blood Knight. No doubt about it. He's the one who opened up the future for us Infected.
But what we need is an unflagging fight instead of the status quo... so I choose the Radiant Knight.
And you?
I pick Sona.
You can do that?!
You called...?
The Board has made their decision... Area 0 will be wiped clean.
Our current strategy with the Infected is a mistake.
A mistake, huh...?
Moreover, Rhodes Island's leader has been digging too deep into the Area 0 affair. This is a lapse on the Armorless Union's part.
Several executive directors are strongly demanding the Armorless Union eradicate this threat. Do not let Rhodes Island's medical team leave Kazimierz alive.
Any issues?
None... but this is only an order from 'several executive directors' and not the whole Board. Right?
That is not for you to question. We have authority over your command.
Point of order, it's Mr. Malkiewicz over there who has authority over me.
Malkiewicz... You know what to do.
Are you hurt?
Yeah... Probably when I blocked his strike.
I couldn't feel a thing at the time... but it might have bruised a bone or two.
Hold still. Let me see...
You need a doctor. This will affect your performance.
Maybe put some ice on it.
Alright.
Can't we heal this with Arts?
That will alleviate the pain and help the wound heal, but if she's got any broken bones, she could end up worse in the future if we don't take proper care of them.
What do we do? You're up against the Wind Knight the next match... That's a strong opponent. If you have to fight this hurt...
Don't worry so much.
How am I supposed to not worry?!
It's just a minor injury. I'm more than used to this.
You call this... minor...?
Margaret... You've worked so hard, and you've gotten so strong... What is it all for?
To become a champion?
'You cannot surmount the rulesetters within their own rules.' Isn't that what Uncle said?
Mhm...
But that's just because Młynar—
No, I get it.
He's right.
But it's not the rulesetters that we need to defeat.
We need to break free of the rules. We need to teach those who have been tamed to rise up, help those who have fallen to regain the light.
Uncle Młynar... He doesn't believe that there are still those who would follow a lighthouse's beam and charge into the storm.
But I don't agree with him.
If we can dispel this darkness, the people will push forward.
You need to listen to the doctor and rest up for a few days.
Your next match is against the Wind Knight, right? If you don't recover by then, it won't go well for you.
Don't end up like me...
I knew my arm was no good, but I pushed myself even further. This is what I got.
Right. You needn't worry about me.
Margaret... Look! Ta-dah!
The new gauntlet?
Already...? That's amazing. I thought it'd take you until tomorrow.
It probably would've taken me another two or three days if I was working by myself.
We didn't adjust your gauntlet around your new weapon last time, not that we'd know where to start.
But that taught us a lesson, and it really got Maria working.
Maria... I know some excellent craftsmen. Perhaps I should introduce them to you.
Really?! Where are they from?
All over. Ursus, Leithanien, Higashi, Laterano... you name it.
Whoa... That's like a whole guild of master craftsmen... Hm? Why would a pharma company need so many craftsmen, though?
You'll just have to find out for yourself.
This reminds me... there was this one year when Uncle Młynar bought you a small drone for your birthday.
You had the thing torn apart that afternoon.
I... I put it back together after that, though.
I was shocked, you know. It might not have flown again after she put it back together, but I never expected the kid to have such amazing talent—
—I half expected Maria to become a knight back then, though. Otherwise, I'd have entrusted her with my workshop.
You better get on that, Kowal. Who knows when you'll kick the bucket?
Huh? Cursing me now, are you?!
Do you want to pursue your interests, Maria?
Erm... I don't know yet.
Hah, Maria's still young.
I just know that... it's been a very long time since we talked like this...
Oh! I forgot to bring the restoration gel!
We still have to try out the new gauntlet... The match is coming up soon, isn't it? I'll head back to the workshop now!
(Margaret... Those are some serious injuries...)
Hm? What's that noise?
The Nightzmora drags his weapon across the floor, sending tiny sparks everywhere.
Enraged, he mutters to himself. He has made his way here following only the smell of the light.
—Aaah?!
Ngh...
You... You are not her... Right, you are her sister.
Where is she?
—! You want Margaret! Why?
Our battle has not yet ended.
But the match's over!
The match...?
My Khaganquest's purpose is not a mere match in a competition.
The Radiant Knight is an essential part of this path... Our holy battle should not have been interrupted!
Then you just have to defeat the Blood Knight and fight my sister the next round—
Enough!
Gah!
Competitions... The Major... They are all for show.
You heard the rules, you heard the crowd! Couldn't you feel how laughable and blasphemous it all was?!
Is this the Major? What is it you won? What did you get from all this?
Glory, wealth, your family's honor, but what is it you're missing...? Hm, what is it you're missing?
And without much time to think about it, as if in pure response to the cheering all around her, Maria holds her sword up high.
Only the pain, sharp, radiating from her hand, overcomes the applause.
Do not take me for a jester entertaining the masses!
I am a descendant of the Kheshig. I must complete my Khaganquest!
Stand aside, I have no interest in frail children! The only business I have is for the Radiant Knight—
No...
I won't let you pass.
You...?
You are nothing but an affinity of the Radiant Knight.
A... An affinity?
Your dream is taken from another. Your conviction is borrowed.
You use your young age as an excuse for your confusion... but what point is there?
The world can laugh at the Radiant Knight's stubbornness, but no one may refute the strength in her actions.
Yet you... you are not even a knight.
Now stand aside, lest my blade slice your throat open.
No.
I won't let you see my sister.
Is she hiding? No... With her brilliant light, the Radiant Knight is above that...
Then... is she injured?
You mean to tell me I injured her in our fight, and I must wait until she recovers before our next match?
Shameless!
Nngh––!
(So heavy—That's heavier than Tytus's lance strikes!)
Haahh... haaahh...
Why am I...
Look at you... already drowned in fear...
You cannot wield your weapon in front of me. If we had met on the battlefield, you would already be dead.
This... has nothing to do with knights.
I just want... to protect... my sister—
The Radiant Knight is not for you to protect, you ignorant pegasus...
Guh!
This arrow... A far cry from that blue archer.
Step away from the child, Nightzmora.
Batbayar...
Will you stand in my way too?
Maria! Quick, stand up! Come over here!
R-Right!
Are you nuts?!
This is not the first time you asked me this question.
I thought... at least you would...
You have nowhere to go, kid.
Tell me, where is your Khagan?
Where is the Kheshig's banner? Where are their tents?
It's been a thousand years... Why live in that past? Why chase that tradition?
What can all that bring you, kid?
Tola.
Your name means 'grasslands.'
You must take pride in your bloodline.
Whensoever and wheresoever you stand, you must take pride in yourself.
Tola.
You must live as a normal Kuranta. This is very simple.
Study, grow up, learn a trade, and marry a beautiful wife.
Batbayar, I made up my mind when my mother died to the fangs of a wild beast.
It was this nightmare of mine... that made me a Nightzmora.
What kind of nonsense are you on about—
I have struggled for years... in this country of knights.
Yet, in the end, I was guided to this path to fulfill my last ideals.
Hold on... hold on.
You can't seriously mean... your journey was all for—
Speak no more.
You are old and frail, but it does not change the fact you once stood on the battlefields. If you insist on standing in my way...
Then I shall trample upon you and destroy one of my last remaining blood kin with my own hands.
Come. I shall charge you.
What—
Master Vogelweide—Get out of there!
Maria, are you okay?!
I-I'm fine.
We can't let this lunatic find Margaret... Or else—!
You would stand in my way... So you will kill me?
Do knights of Kazimierz truly know what it means to fight to the death?
No, you cannot possibly. Batbayar, you are old. And you, naive pegasus, you know nothing of the cruelty of the world.
You would interfere in my fight with her. You are sullying both our names and honor.
No!
Honor doesn't matter at all!
—Then what does your sister fight for?!
You dare spout such blasphemy?! You dare claim the Radiant Knight vies for the Major's title not to regain her glory?!
*Whimpers*...
You heretic! Raise your weapon! You will pay the price!
I... still won't let you pass!
Ever!
Ahh!
Kid!
That's enough. Don't push yourself—
No... Master Vogelweide... Look at your hand...
This isn't the first time. I'm used to it.
It's over. Your strength is beyond my expectations. It was just enough to tide my anger...
You too are a descendant. I shall not take your life, but you will get out of my way.
So you've got your heart set on dueling Margaret, huh?
Wrong. I am merely continuing a fight that was cut short.
What do you want?
You're stubborn and... mad. What are you looking for anyway? What is it that you actually want?
You do not understand...
Like hell I would. For that so-called tradition of yours, you gave up your whole life and everything ahead of you! It's all pointless!
Did I need you to give my life meaning?
I just don't want to see you stay on this path of insanity forever. The 'Khaganquest' is a coming-of-age tradition followed by a handful of tribes. Doesn't that make you still a child?!
Gah! I am done with you. Out of the way.
Over my dead body!
—You will regret this, old man!
Maria, what are you doing?! You're hurt—
This is my first time to see you so worked up, Nightzmora...
The shield of a knight is meant to protect people. That's what my sister told me.
You said my conviction was borrowed. I've got nothing to say about that.
But if I'm going to be scared to walk even well-lit roads, then there's really no point in the knighthood anymore!
How dare you spout that nonsense about knights—
—Sorry, but I'm not a knight anymore. I just want to protect my sister—
I can't let a psycho like you get in her way!
Arts...? It's... Hm?
A ray of light shines off the blood on Maria's face.
Fragile and insignificant. It's true. If he gave it his all, the Nightzmora could easily have strangled the pegasus to death.
He is confident of that.
Yet... why does the blood on her face... have this radiant, golden shine?
Is it the light of the Arts turning it gold?
A pegasus whose name is bound to go down in history... yet so frail... Is it a mere coincidence?
Master Vogelweide, go back without me... I'll hold him here.
Drop the act, kid. He'll kill you—!
Don't worry.
I'll stop him!
Huh...? Why are you turning around...?
...Pegasus.
You cannot grow stronger on the conviction of others. Not unless you truly believe in it.
And you do not.
Dedication and sacrifice.
These virtues border on self-destruction... and you possess them at birth. You taught me a lesson, pegasus, descendant.
Perhaps...
...No, never mind.
Tell the Radiant Knight that my bout with her has not yet ended. I am merely putting it on hold.
Perhaps? Perhaps what?
Perhaps I would have made the same decision if I had been raised in the same environment as you?
Never allow yourself to be so weak, Tola.
Never!
The Radiant Knight, the Blood Knight... The Major has really heated up in the blink of an eye.
If we go by points and nothing else, the finals should long have been in reach of the Radiant Knight.
Doesn't that make you happy?

opts: If only the K.G.C.C. would let Nearl take the crown.
After the Infected incident, will the people accept that?
Nearl's greatest challenge has only just begun.

lines.x: You're right...
But even if the General Chamber is scheming something, I'm sure you and the Adeptus will help Nearl overcome.
That said, is the K.G.C.C. really the problem...? They're definitely not ones to do any work themselves. Instead, they prefer to sow the seeds... and wait for them to sprout.
Many will voice their gripes...
Hehe... but that also happened when the Blood Knight first came along.
But who still remembers any of that after the Blood Knight took his trophy and the money came raining down?
They have short memories, Doctor. No matter who ends up winning, as long as they speak on the Infected's behalf, it'll all work out fine.
Kazimierz loves its champions.
You mean... the Blood Knight? No, you wouldn't say something so skin-deep...
Whatever's the case, I believe you and Nearl will overcome.

opts: How are Amiya and the medic operators doing?

lines.x: They're giving the Infected knights checkups and treatment, just as always.
Rhodes Island is plenty efficient. I can see why the Grand Knight trusts you so much.
That said... I'm here right with you, and you haven't shown even a little bit of care for me.
That makes me kind of sad.

opts: You're right. I should thank you for all your help.

lines.x: What are you thanking me for, specifically?

opts: For being a diligent bodyguard.
For giving us all this invaluable intel while we have our hands tied.
Uhh... for chatting with me and helping me kill time?

lines.x: I'm just doing my job. I was given my orders, and I would go through fire and water to fulfill them.
I've only told you what the Grand Knight permitted me to say.
I'd love to accept your thanks, but it was her who truly helped Rhodes Island.
Oh... so our conversations are just a way to pass the time to you?
Hehe, but in that case, I'll keep talking to you all you want.
So, how will you thank me, Doctor?

opts: Pretend to take a walk outside so you can take a break and go shopping?
What are you into? Rhodes Island's got all kinds of people.
Anything you want.

lines.x: Hehe... That sounds wonderful. I've always wanted to be a model, just once.
I may wear all this armor all the time, but I'm still a girl. I got tired of this outfit a long time ago.
That said... I didn't really have the right to think about something like this, in the past.
What I'm into...?
I've never thought about it before... What do normal Kazimierzian girls like? Food? Makeup? Oh... Let's not talk about knight stuff.
Well... as for outfits... All this armor does dampen my spirits every now and then.
Though a campaign knight like me doesn't really get much chance to wear regular clothes, either.
Doctor... do you know what you're saying? 'Anything you want' is pretty much the last thing you'd want to say when you're being interrogated.
Most of us hand our decision-making power away, probably because we find obnoxious to have to make decisions... but sometimes... the one we give that power may have some unexpected ideas.
Well, then, Doctor... Would you lean back, please?

opts: Uh, sure.

lines.x: Close your eyes, okay? I wouldn't want anything to happen to them...
And then...
Doctor, don't open your eyes.
If you see an Armorless assassin's face, I'm afraid you won't be leaving Kazimierz alive.

opts: I can't let you bear all the risks.
I've got to at least know what's going on, no?

lines.x: You're not just a regular campaign knight, are you?
The Armorless Union doesn't hold the patent on assassination, you know?
Don't move, or I'll cut your throat open.
Now that won't do. I can't let you put the Doctor's life in danger, even if it costs me my own.
It's just some foreign company... Is this one really worth giving your life for, knight—Hm?
The barcode on your arm... You're a purchased slave. Why so loyal to an outsider?
Unfortunately, my desire to protect the Doctor comes from the bottom of my heart.
I see... The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi arranged for a lackey like you to follow Rhodes Island around. They planned to use Rhodes Island to investigate Area 0 right from the start.
Don't make our relationship sound so exploitative.

opts: That's right. If you gotta say it, we exploit each other.
This is what you call a win-win situation.
As long as we get along, am I right?

lines.x: So you are Rhodes Island's 'Doctor.' There's supposed to be a Cautus girl around too...
I'm not into hurting kids. Bumping you off should do the trick, right?

opts: Too bad. Amiya's Rhodes Island's true leader.
You really shouldn't underestimate Amiya though.

lines.x: Doctor, go on without me. I'll hold her here. Take Miss Amiya and leave this place. Look for someone from the Adeptus—
I'm not here alone.
The Armorless Union already has you surrounded... You're not walking out of here.
It's okay, Doctor... As long as I'm here, I—
—Doctor?
Shielding your own bodyguard? What's the idea? You'll give your life for someone else?
Aww, so cute... I've got a job to do though.

opts: A mission from the Board of Directors?

lines.x: What...?

opts: If my guess is correct, the Board didn't give you these orders.

lines.x: I don't know what you're talking about.

opts: Is it Mieszko Industries?
Is it Sun Shrubs Pharmaceuticals?
Is it Cloud Pharmaceuticals?
Of course, Rhodes Island is a threat to them.
But does it matter if some foreign company knows the truth?
I'm sure they have the power to silence Rhodes Island.
What they're really afraid of are the beasts sitting at the same table.
They're afraid of their fellow men.

lines.x: So?

opts: I'm guessing it won't be hard for you to kill me, huh?
What's the rush? You on some kind of flexible work schedule?
Why don't you take a seat and catch a breather?

lines.x: Do you really understand what you're doing...?
Even with this campaign knight in the way, I can—

opts: No, you can't.
The spokesman personally oversaw our contract signing with our partners.
Malkiewicz has been very helpful.
Would you like to know, from a legal perspective, where Rhodes Island stands?
Right now, we're temporarily affiliated with the General Chamber of Commerce.
If you lay a finger on us, you're doing the same to the K.G.C.C.
Anyway, I think there's someone out there who's not about to let this chance pass by.
Of course, you people have a way to get around the law.
But under the veneer of civilization, there is a bloodbath that you can't get around.

lines.x: A call...?

opts: Answer the phone, uh... Platinum, right?
It's for you.
Have a nice chat with civilization, ma'am.

lines.x: Centaurea...?
No need to be so shocked, Platinum.
I'm very glad you haven't killed Rhodes Island's leader yet.
Call it off. Your orders were mistaken. Those morons, jumping the gun. It is not up to them to give the Union orders without consulting the Board.
And stop the madness that Area 0 is pulling on its so-called useless Infected. Rhodes Island has opened our eyes. Their lives still have meaning... They can yet create value.
That's all.
You... You planned this?
How did you do it?

opts: You cozy up to some people, and you square up to others.
Wasn't that hard.
Most people think it's difficult to even bring ideas to their bosses.
What's actually difficult is figuring out how to cozy up and who to square up to.
Thankfully, I got a friend who's a good listener.

lines.x: So what will we do with Area 0...?
We'll be working with too many uncertain factors if we let the two Infected knights make a big splash after all the uprisings the Infected have instigated.
And if the public catches wind of the way Area 0 takes care of its Infected right now...
I suppose there are both upsides and downsides.
We don't permit 'both upsides and downsides.' Whether it's good or bad, what we want is control over the matter.
I have other ways to handle this...
Just to be safe, I've already instructed the Armorless Union to take care of it.
Should I let you go, just like that?
You're an outsider who knows about the Armorless Union...
Don't be such a sore loser, Platinum. You're embarrassing yourself, you know?
Doctor! I've got something to ask you about transporting today's supplies—
Hm? Do you have a guest? I-I'm sorry...
It's okay... Come in.
An Armorless and a campaign knight are having tea together because of you, Doctor. You're really full of surprises.
Um... Doctor, may I have a second? I need you to sign this...
(So that's Rhodes Island's leader... If I take her down now...)
Naturally, she is just entertaining the thought.
Even Gravel, who sits mere meters away from her, can feel not an ounce of hostility. After all, she is merely entertaining the thought.
Be that as it may, Platinum suddenly hesitates, before feeling relieved for her decision.
She is relieved that she did not carry out her mission.
In the blink of an eye, she senses a possibility in the petite Cautus.
If things escalate, will she actually be able to draw her arrow unscathed?
Or perhaps, before that—
Hm...? Miss? Is something the matter?
Never mind. Please excuse me.
I'll send someone to fix the glass I broke. Time to go.
The Doctor...Doctor of Rhodes Island.
I'll be back.
Doctor, Gravel... Did something happen?
No.
We had a cup of tea together. That's all.
Platinum, ma'am... Here's what you wanted.
I'm surprised you found it.
Right. We searched through the communications between Rhodes Island and the Adeptus, and we narrowed it down to this message with the clues we had.
These are the true medical examination records of Operator Nearl... the Radiant Knight.
What...?
The Radiant Knight... isn't Infected?!
Welcome to the Kazimierz Major! It's your old buddy, Greatmouth Mob!
Thanks to Nightmare Knight's mistake, we're treated to today's matchup, the Blood Knight versus Nightmare Knight!
With their terrifying auras, and might unmatched by any other, imagine the fight our mighty Blood Knight and the legendary Nightzmora have in store for us today!
The Blood Knight and Nightmare had a fiery first encounter the other day! And now! They face each other in a formal match!
There will be blood! There will be slaughter! Only the victor will get to bask in fame and glory. Let us welcome—our knights—for today's match—!
No knightclub, no sponsors. Good lord, why are you people leaving that huge pie on the table?!
Somebody, make him a knightclub! You'll be rolling in all the dough!
On the other side, we have the king of blood, Kazimierz's Blood-Red Chalice, the heartless victory machine, and deservedly, the very pinnacle!
THE—BLOOOOOD KNIIIIGHT, DIKAAAAIIIOPOLIIIIIS!
We meet again... Nightzmora.
Are you still searching for the shadows of the past?
Good news, Doctor.
The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi has approved Rhodes Island's applications. 'It's necessary for our valued partners to visit the city.'
If none of these things happened in the first place, you would've been able to enjoy the Grand Knight Territory more freely.
Doctor, were you looking for me?
Oh, Gravel! I still have some healthy food leftovers. Want to take them with you?
Good day, Gravel, Doctor.

opts: We rarely get a chance to visit the other parts of Kazimierz.
Want to take a look around?
It's–– vacation–– time––!

lines.x: Wh-What a battle! I can hardly believe my eyes!
Actually, I should be talking our unknown Nightzmora! Can you believe this is his first Major appearance, and he's holding his own against a champion?!
The clash of blood-red armor on the field tonight will eclipse the moon!
The audience is definitely feeling it too! Tola, 'Scourge of the Prairie,' Nightmare Knight! His crazed determination is addictive and intoxicating!
Nightmare Knight's been surging in the polls since his duel with the Radiant Knight! Will he add another page to this story?! A rise to grand knighthood?!
By the way, Ancients Entertainment is holding a 'Knightly Title Trivia Game!' Guess the knightly titles and win fabulous prizes!
Where'd your bravado go, Nightzmora?
Ngh...
Minoan, in the times when my ancestors flayed the land, your heroes had all become naught but words on the lips of poets.
You waste your breath.
Where's your Khagan now? What happened to those proud kinsmen of yours?
One who lives in a bygone nightmare is a pitiful man indeed.
Minoan, Infected. What are you fighting for?
Survival.
They made me the hero of the Infected... They enshrined me in the annals of history. But I just wanted to survive.
The hero of the Infected?
I see... Do you not also fight for your people?
My people...? No. We don't share the same bloodline, nor were we born in the same place. We are just unfortunate enough to suffer the same disease.
I pitied their plight, so I went to deliver them, to find a way out for them.
You say they're not your people... But you still want to save them...
Laughable.
You are mighty, without a doubt. But, have the prairies of Kazimierz been carved up by the Infected and foreigners?
The Kazimierzians, the Kuranta... Where are they?!
Did I cross thousands of miles to the country of knights, only to fight against the Infected, Minoans, and my own kinsmen?!
––A sick joke!
Woaaaahh... S-So, this is Kazimierz's commercial hub?
Strictly speaking, this is just one of hundreds of commercial centers across the Grand Knight Territory.
I recommended this particular one because it's close.
But there's way too much junk food everywhere! Just one look around and I count three popcorn stands, two pizzerias, and four burger shops!
This is unacceptable!
Haha... Is this because there's a competition venue nearby?
Of course. The arena lies at the heart of every center of commerce.
All businesses revolve around the knights' glorious blood sport. It's the way things are done here.
Is this... a Nearl doll?
Aww! It's so cute!
Wait a minute, this shop... Is it all dedicated to...
Yo! Are you folks fans of the Radiant Knight too?
Then, you've come to the right place! This is a specialty store for everything Radiant Knight! Clay figurines, plush dolls, Nearl family crests – you name it, we've got it!

By the way, if the Radiant Knight manages to defeat the Wind Knight and advance to the finals, our supplier is considering making exclusive commemorative medals from refined Originium! Want to put in a pre-order?
I mean, the Radiant Knight's pretty much sure to win! Hahaha!

opts: (Buy the doll for Nightingale.)
(Ask how much the pre-order is.)
(Rhetorically ask if the store would consider a shield used by the Radiant Knight its greatest treasure.)

lines.x: Thanks for your patronage!
Oh, uh... You only have foreign money? Lungmen Dollars? Err, you'll have to go to the bank first.
Let me do the conversion for you real quick... That should be about 1.3 million LMD?
A shield? Since when did the Radiant Knight use a shield?
But we have warhammers, double-bladed swords, and even the latest model of swordspears. Want to take a look?
It's hard to tell what's going on! Nightmare Knight's ferocious attack was again repelled by the Blood Knight, and the former champion is slicing through the artificial turf as if it was tofu!
It's already been ten minutes, but the tempestuous exchange of blows continues! Who will be the first to show signs of fatigue in this seemingly endless battle?
––As I speak, the champion is readying his Arts again! He evades a sweeping strike from his opponent!
After all that, are the two sides only feeling each other out?! Neither the champion nor the Nightzmora seems to want to take the initiative and cast the first Arts––
Minoan, how did you learn to fight like this?
Unfortunately, I had very little combat training before becoming a knight.
It was only after that I learned as I fought.
...A born warrior.
Young man.
I am not your mentor. But, if it is an opponent you seek, there is one thing I can teach you.
The fear you seek to instill through bluffing means nothing to someone who lives only in the present.
The Blood Knight takes a half-step back.
Fresh blood oozes from his armor, condensing into a stream around him.
A crimson light wraps around his weapon, forging it into a lethal edge of judgment.
Answer me, Nightzmora. In the history you dreamt, in the thousands of years between then and now––
Were the Infected ever given a new lease on life?
Why you...
You call yourself a Kheshig? Then don't back down now, Nightzmora!
Where is your Khagan now? Bring him to me!
Where is your Khagan?
Where is your Khagan?
Thousands of years have passed since the Khagan stepped into history.
You'll only find him in history books, boy.
Kheshigs? They were the Khagan's attendants, the greatest warriors known to this world.
The Khagan conquered many places, but in doing so, did a lot of things that hurt people.
It is in the nature of the Nightzmora to spread fear, and the desire to conquer was practically sustenance for the Khagan.
But, Tola.
You were born into this world, and you will surely find your own––
...Father, rival, master, ideals.
Was I actually... looking for these things in this city?
No! It is time to wake up from my dream.
This will just be a lingering memory. The path I drew for my Khaganquest was set in stone a long time ago.

lines.st: The young hunter sets out on his Khaganquest ♪
From dreams he goes, towards the shores of gold ♪
Nightzmora.
A Nightzmora stands in the arena.
His limbs are at ease. His flaws are exposed. He knows not where to cast his gaze from beneath that helmet.
He hums an ancient ballad.
Until the black night paints over his vision ♪ Until a tower of bone stands erect in his heart ♪
Until hemlock chokes his hazy home ♪

lines.x: Done saying your prayers?
My family is dead. My people are lost.
––The Khagan is wherever the tip of my blade points.
I—
––am my own Khagan!
Come!
Clear weather. No wind. A fine day for shooting.
Targets: the two leaders of Rhodes Island - one hooded person of unknown race, and one Cautus.
Mood... Very bad. Because every time I try to do my work, someone gets in my way.
Addendum. Extremely bad mood.
Do you have a habit of keeping a diary?
Who would deliberately write these things down? Might as well send it to the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi Kazimierz and ask for a speedier sentence.
By the looks of things, you're the type of person who brings her baggage with her to work.
Confessarius, the Radiant Knight, and that Cautus who gives off strange vibes.
What the hell is Rhodes Island supposed to be anyway?
I never had a chance to ask you before, but... You people...
Where did you hear the name 'Confessarius'?
Monique has seen the skills of the Sarkaz before her.
Excellent swordsmanship. Complex, seemingly incomprehensible Originium Arts - perhaps witchcraft. Along with the Radiant Knight, she was able to block a Darksteel arrow.
She had a suspicion at the time, that perhaps... 'blocking arrows' was not the full extent of the Sarkaz's abilities.
But now, she is convinced of one thing.
...I can't tell you that.
I'm also not so kind-hearted that I can just watch an assassin take aim at the Doctor and Amiya, and then let her walk free.
